First, check thestatus of your request. If the host has already accepted it, then it’s subject to theircancellation policy. If it’s still pending, you can cancel, and you won’t be charged for the reservation or the service fees.
The simplest way is to find themessage threadwith your host and selectWithdraw request.
Cancel a reservation request on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to change
For a reservation that saysPending, clickWithdraw request
ClickCancel request

title: Find your reservation status as a guest
content:
Find your reservation status as a guest
As a guest, it’s always good to know the status of your reservation. Your status lets you know if your reservation has been confirmed, or if anything else is needed, like verifying your ID or leaving a review.
If you’re unsure about your status, you can always find outwhat other statuses mean.
Check reservation status on desktop
ClickMessages
Find the reservation message from your host
The reservation status is shown within the message thread
A confirmed status means you’re good to go! You’ll receive an email saying so, along with a text (SMS) and a push notificationif you have those set up. Learn where tofind your confirmation code.
If you sent the host of a place to stay areservation request, they have 24 hours to respond. If they decline or don’t respond, no worries: You won’t be charged and are free to book a different stay. Either way, be sure your account is up to date with validpayment informationandyour verified ID. (Some hosts require it.) For Instant Book listings, your reservation is automatically confirmed.
Not sure about your status? Find outwhat other statuses mean.

title: Understanding your reservation status
content:
Understanding your reservation status
Your reservation status lets you know how things are going as a host or guest.
Arriving today
The guest will arrive within 24 hours.
Arriving tomorrow
The guest will arrive soon, but not within 24 hours.
Arrives in __ days
The guest will be checking within said number of days. Now’s a good time toprint reservation detailsandcoordinate check-inif you haven’t already.
Awaiting guest ID
The host is requiring the guest toverify their identitybefore accepting the trip request. They have 12 hours to do so; otherwise, the request will expire.
Awaiting guest review
The guest has checked out and has 14 days towrite a reviewof their stay.
Awaiting payment
The trip request was accepted, but the guest's payment hasn’t gone through. The reservation can’t be confirmed until payment is complete, so they have 24 hours to update their payment information. Otherwise, the reservation will be canceled, and the guest won't be charged.
Learn more aboutreservations that are awaiting payment.
Canceled
The reservation was cancelled, probably because:
The guest didn’tverify their identitywithin the 12-hour time period
Their payment didn’t go through and they didn't update their payment info within 24 hours
Canceled by you/guest/host/Airbnb
Either thehost, theguest, or Airbnb canceled a confirmed reservation. Sometimes, Airbnb may cancel on behalf of a host or guest.
Checking out today
The guest will check out within 24 hours.
Change pending
Either the host or guest has initiated a trip change.
Confirmed
The trip request was accepted, either by the host or automatically viaInstant Book. Airbnb has collected payment.
Currently hosting
The guest is on their trip at this very moment. Nice!
Inquiry
The guest has asked a question about specific dates, but hasn't yet sent a trip request. In order to maintain their response rate, hosts are encouraged to reply within 24 hours and invite guests to book, decline, or send the guest a special offer before the inquiry expires.
If the status isInquiry—expires soon, hosts can check theirTodaydashboard to find out how many hours are left to respond. Learn more aboutcontacting hosts.
Invite expired
The guest did not accept their pre-approvedinvitation to bookwithin 24 hours of receiving it from the host. They can still book the place, but they’ll need to send a new trip request.
Invite sent
The host hasinvited the guest to bookthe dates shown with automatic confirmation. The guest has 24 hours toacceptby choosingBook Now.
Not possible
The requested dates are no longer available. This could happenfor several reasons, including reservation overlap or a recent update to the host’s calendar.
Offer expired
The guest received a special offer from the host but didn’t accept it within the 24-hour window.
Past guest
The guest as completed their trip.
Request declined
The hostdeclinedthe guest’s trip request, so they won’t be charged.
Request expired
Either the host or the guest took longer than 24 hours to accept or decline the request. If the guest is still interested, they’ll need to send a new request. Learn more aboutdeclined or expired requests.
Request withdrawn
The guest sent a trip request but then decided to cancel it.
Review guest
The trip is over, and the host has 14 days to leave a review for the guest.
Special offer sent
The host has invited the guest to book the dates shown at a different price from what’s listed. Hosts often do this to offer a discount or add an additional amount, such as a pet fee, to the reservation. The guest has 24 hours toacceptby choosingBook Now—at which point, the reservation is automatically accepted.
Trip changed by Airbnb
Our customer support team has changed the trip details on behalf of the host or guest.
Trip change declined
The host has declined the guest’s request to change details about their trip.
Trip change requested
The guest has requested to change details about their trip. If the host doesn't accept, they’ll have to go with the original plan or cancel.
Trip change sent
The host has requested to change details about their trip. If the guest doesn’t accept, they’ll have to go with the original plan or cancel.
Trip declined
Guest asked a trip question and the host declined it, which means the guest is not able to send a trip request. This may be due to a change in their availability.
Note: Times are based on the listing’s local time zone.

title: Why your home reservation request may have been declined by the host
content:
Why your home reservation request may have been declined by the host
Hosts might not be able to accommodate every request that comes their way. That’s why some hosts require guests to request to book a stay—after that, hosts have 24 hours to respond to a guest’s reservation request.
Understanding why hosts decline home reservation requests can help prepare guests for a successful booking. Here are some common reasons why a host may decline a request:
Guest has no reviews or negative reviews.Hosts often rely on a guest's past reviews to gauge reliability and compatibility with their house rules.
Special requests that the Host can’t accommodate.Sometimes, guests have special requests that go beyond what is offered in the home listing, such as early check-in, late checkout, additional amenities, extra parking, or accommodation for more people than their place allows. If these requests can’t be met by the host, they might decline the request.
Dates aren’t available.Occasionally, a home reservation request may be declined if the host's calendar is not up-to-date, and the dates aren’t actually available.
Guest is booking on behalf of someone else.Requests made on behalf of other people, like family members or friends, aren’t allowed on Airbnb.
Unclear communication or lack of transparency.Requesting to book a stay without providing any info—such as who will be staying and generally why you’re visiting—can lead to a home reservation request being declined. Hosts value clear communication and transparency to ensure a good fit between how they host their place and the guest's expectations.
Here are some things you can do that may help you secure a home booking:
Complete your Airbnb profile.A robust profile helps show you’re reliable and authentic to hosts who don’t know you yet, and can help establish the trust they need to welcome you into their place.
Verify your identity on Airbnb.If you're new to Airbnb, considergetting verifiedto help increase trust.
Send the host a message to inquire, or include details when submitting your reservation request.Let the host know why you like their place, who will be staying with you, generally why you’re traveling to their area, and anything else you think is important for them to know.
Read the host’s house rules and home listing description and let them know.If you’ve read their house rules, you can let them know that you have and if you’ll adhere to them during a stay at their place. If you have any questions about their home listing, you can send them a message to ask.
Note:Hosts aren’t shown a guest’s profile photo when responding to a home reservation request. It’s only available to them after they’ve accepted the request and the home reservation is confirmed. Find out more aboutwhat guest info is shown to hosts before they accept a request.
Hosts on Airbnb must follow ourNondiscrimination Policy, which includes making every effort to be welcoming to guests of all backgrounds. If you feel your home reservation request was declined for a reason that violates our Nondiscrimination Policy,please let us know.

title: How long a host has to respond to your trip request
content:
How long a host has to respond to your trip request
You’ve found a great place to stay and sent your trip request—how exciting! Now you're wondering when you’ll find out if your trip is confirmed.
Hosts have 24 hours to accept or decline yourrequest, but the vast majority respond within 12 hours. Chances are, it’ll be even quicker: More than half of all trip requests are accepted within one hour of the host reading it.
You can checkthe status of your trip requestany time you like, but you’ll also receive email updates when things change.
Most hosts respond within a few hours, but time zones and lack of internet access might slow things down. In the meantime, you can try reaching out to other hosts in the area. Just be careful not to send more than one trip request for the same dates to ensure you don’t end up double booked.
Don’t want to wait for a host response? TryInstant Booklistings—they allow you to book immediately without needing to send a request to the host for approval, provided you’ve completed your account setup.
If the host declines your request or lets it expire, you won’t be charged and you’re free to book a different stay. If you’re still interested in booking the host’s place, you can learn more aboutwhat to do if your trip request is declined or expires.
Check a host’s response rate and time on desktop
Click the listing you want to check
Go toMeet your host
UnderHost details, find the response rate and how long it usually takes them to respond
Note:The response rate and time are averages based on when the host has responded to  inquiries and reservation requests over a recent period of time. It can help give you an indication of how quickly a host responds, but it’s not a guarantee.

title: How long a host has to respond to your inquiry
content:
How long a host has to respond to your inquiry
An inquiry is what guests can send to a host before they request to book—it’s usually a message from a guest wanting more info about certain dates for a stay, service, or experience.
If you've already sent a request to book, find outhow long a host has to respond to a request.
Most Hosts respond within a few hours, but time zones and lack of internet access might slow things down.
We recommend that hosts respond to guests within 24 hours, but keep in mind that inquiries don’t expire—you may find that it takes a host a bit longer to get back to you.
If you're waiting for a response, you can try reaching out to other hosts in the area. You can also send the host another message asking for a response as soon as possible because you're interested in booking a place, service, or experience right away.
Want to go ahead and book the host’s place? You cansend them a trip requestor book right away usingInstant Book, if it’s available.
Check a host’s response rate and time on desktop
Click the listing you want to check
Go toMeet your host
UnderHost details, find the response rate and how long it usually takes them to respond
Note:The response rate and time are averages based on when the host has responded to  inquiries and reservation requests over a recent period of time. It can help give you an indication of how quickly a host responds, but it’s not a guarantee.

title: Booking services and experiences
content:
Booking services and experiences
Wine tastings. Dinner cooked in-home by a private chef. Family photos to remember an event. Airbnb services and experiences offer something for everyone.
Want to enhance your trip? Check out the services and experiences that are available during your stay. You can choose a specific city and dates, or browse through all of the services and experiences available—just visit theServicesorExperiencessections on the Airbnb website or app.
Any home, service, or experience you book will show up in yourTripson the itinerary timeline along with your other reservations. From there, you can tap to quickly get directions, add co-travelers, or message your host.
You don’t need to have a home booked on Airbnb in order to book a service or experience. You can find a great staycation activity or ways to celebrate a special event.
If you want to book a service at your Airbnb, review your host’s house rules first. Some hosts allow all services, while others may limit specific types or not permit them at all. You can contact your home host if you have questions. Learn more aboutadding a serviceto your reservation.
If a service you’re interested in isn’t allowed at your place, you’ll need to book it at an offsite location instead. For instance:
For chefs, ask if they can drop off prepared meal
For trainers, ask if they can meet you at a nearby public space for the workout
For hair, makeup, or nails, ask if they can work out of a nearby salon
For experiences, take a minute to review theguest requirementsand any other guidelines set by the host. Not all experiences are appropriate for allage levelsand some require special skills. Got questions? Feel free tocontact the host.

title: Find out what’s included in an Airbnb Experience
content:
Find out what’s included in an Airbnb Experience
Every experience is different, so it’s a good idea to carefully review what is and isn’t included before you book.
For instance, you’d need to know if drinks are included in your tasting experience, or if you’ll need to pay for the special exhibit at a museum tour.
Hosts add this info to the experience page. Check theWhat’s includedorWhat I’ll providesection to find out what to expect.
Accommodations are typically not included with single-day experiences. Multi-day adventures do include lodging, meals, and activities.

title: Booking experiences for groups
content:
Booking experiences for groups
Organizing a work outing, family trip, or gathering? Private groups allow you to share experiences with a unique party. You can reserve all the spots in an Airbnb Experience on the specific date and time that it’s offered.
Just like when you’re searching for a place to stay, enter the location and find all the amazing experiences available. Next, enter your date and then the number of guests.
When you find the experience that you want to book, check if the Book for a private group option is available. You can alsocontact the hostto learn if they can host your group privately or customize the experience for your group. Just be aware that whether a host offers private groups is at the host’s discretion
If your group makes a reservation and the total amount that you’d pay for that reservation exceeds the host’s minimum price, then your group will pay the higher amount.
After your reservation is confirmed, you caninvite guests to your group reservationinTrips. Simply send the invite link for guests to accept access to the host’s profile, service details, helpful info about the experience, and all past and future messages with your host.
Use group threads to keep your group’s communications connected and organized. Once you’ve added your guests to your booked experience, guests, hosts, and co-hosts canMessageeach other in a single thread, making it much easier to coordinate plans and share information.
Booking or hosting an experience and need to message the host or a specific guest directly? Hosts and guests of experiences can alsodirectly message each other.

title: Search for listings with accessibility features
content:
Search for listings with accessibility features

We believe that anyone can belong anywhere—and we want to make it easier for people with disabilities and other accessibility needs to have a good time using Airbnb. You can search for a home, service, or experience that offers accessibility features such as a home with a step-free entrance or a service hosted in sign language.

If a listing that you’re interested in does not have any accessibility information, please contact the host to discuss your accessibility needs.

Listed below are some of the accessibility features that could be available for homes, services, and experiences. Contact your host for more detailed information about the specific accessibility features they offer and whether they meet your needs:

Home accessibility features:
Guest entrance and parking
Step-free access
Disabled parking spot
Guest entrance wider than 32 inches

Bedroom
Step-free bedroom access
Bedroom entrance wider than 32 inches

Bathroom
Step-free bathroom access
Bathroom entrance wider than 32 inches
Toilet grab bar
Shower grab bar
Step-free shower
Shower or bath chair
Adaptive equipment
Ceiling or mobile hoist

Experiences accessibility features:
Sign language options
Designated sighted guide
Step-free access
Entrances wider than 32 inches
Mainly flat or leveled ground
Step-free bathroom available
Disabled parking spot
No extreme sensory stimuli
Quiet retreat space available

Services accessibility features:
Sign language options

Filter by accessibility features on desktop
Click Homes, Services, or Experiences
Enter your destination, dates, and number of guests
Once your search results show, click Filters
Click Accessibility features
Select the features you want and click Show results

Here’s where you can find more details on its accessibility features and determine if they meet your needs.

Listing description: Hosts may add accessibility information in the description of their home, service, or experience listing.
Accessibility section: For homes, hosts will have added a detailed description of any accessibility feature they’ve selected in the Accessibility section.
Things to know section: Selected accessibility features for experiences will be displayed in the Things to Know section of the listing.
Contact the host: If you’d like more information on the accessibility features of a listing, or if you need to request a reasonable adjustment to access a home or participate in a service or experience, you can message the host directly.

As part of our commitment to inclusion and respect for everyone in our community, our Nondiscrimination Policy doesn't allow hosts to treat a guest differently or turn them down simply because they have a disability.
Our Accessibility Policy also sets expectations for hosts around accommodating requests for reasonable adjustments in order to allow someone with accessibility needs to use or enjoy a home, service, or experience.

title: Invite guests to your experience reservation
content:
Invite guests to your experience reservation
When you want to invite a guest to your experience reservation, share your reservation link and ask them to click or tapJoin experience. Once they do, they’ll receive an email confirming the reservation details—like where to meet and how to prepare for their upcoming experience.
After booking and up until 1 day before start time: you can share your invite link by clicking or tappingInvite guestson your experience reservation
Day before start time: go to your experience reservation and click or tapManage guests
To change the total number of guests (not just who’s joining), cancel and rebook with the right number. Refunds are subject to the host’s cancellation policy
Learn more aboutremoving or changing the number of guestsinvited to your experience reservation.
If you want to invite your group of friends, family, teammates, or colleagues to a private experience, you canbook an experience for a group, and then share your invite link.

title: Joining an experience
content:
Joining an experience
You’ve been invited to an experience as a guest. Lucky you! So, what’s next?
You’ll receive an invite link from the person who booked the experience. Click or tap the link to open Airbnb (or you’ll need to create anAirbnb accountif you don’t have one) and then tapJoin experience.
Once you’ve accepted the invite, here’s what happens next:
The person who booked the experience will be informed that you’re attending
You’ll be officially added to the reservation
You’ll get a confirmation email that includes:Details about the experience and what you can expectYour host’s contact information to answer any questionsA way to remove yourself from the reservation if you can no longer make it
Details about the experience and what you can expect
Your host’s contact information to answer any questions
A way to remove yourself from the reservation if you can no longer make it
You’ll have access to a direct message thread with the host
You’ll be able to leave your feedback in a review after the experience is over

title: Request a custom date or time for a service or experience
content:
Request a custom date or time for a service or experience
You’ve got your heart set on a service or experience, but you don’t see availability on the host’s calendar. You can always ask about a different time or date not listed and find out if they’re available.
Request availability on desktop
Select the service or experience you want and clickMessage [host’s name]
ClickRequest a specific date
Select a date and the number of guests
ClickSend request
The host will review the reservation details and decide if they can fit you into their schedule.
If they confirm, you can complete the booking in the message thread
If they can’t accommodate you, you can try to work together to find a mutually good time
If the host scheduled the experience and it’s on their calendar, it will be available for others to book, unless your request was fora private booking.

title: Interacting with Experience Co-Hosts and assistants
content:
Interacting with Experience Co-Hosts and assistants
Some Hosts choose to manage their listing with a team, so you might interact with an assistant or a Co-Host instead of the primary Host.
Name, photo, and bio will be on the Experience page
They can help lead Experiences, and may either be present with the primary Host or lead your group by themselves
They can respond to your messages and inquiries, and their role will be displayed to guests in the group message
Name, photo, and bio won’t be on the Experience page
They can help manage Experiences, but not lead any guests
They can respond to your messages and inquiries, and their role will be displayed to guests in the group message

title: Booking requirements for experiences
content:
Booking requirements for experiences
Experiences give you an insider view of the world. You might learn how to cook tamales in Mexico City, or hike to a mountain picnic outside of Marrakesh.
No matter the thrill, one thing's for sure—you must comply with local laws and regulations when you go on an experience. For example, you’ll need to meet the local drinking age laws to drink alcohol on your experience.
Curious if your activity requires a specific license, such as scuba certification or a driver’s license? Your host will provide that info in the guest requirements section of the listing.
Some activities require differentskill levels, such as a strenuous hike or a sailing adventure. This information should be included in the listing. And, of course, feel free to ask your host questions about what you should expect during the experience.

title: Age requirements
content:
Age requirements
We all love to wander the world, but it’s against ourTerms of Servicefor anyone under the age of 18 to create an account to travel or host.

title: Bringing extra guests to a service or experience
content:
Bringing extra guests to a service or experience
It’s a big “Nope” to bring a couple of extra friends unannounced on the day of a service or experience. The host can turn them away if they’re not included in your experience reservation, or they haven’t been accounted for in your service reservation.
To avoid disappointment,include your friendsin your plans early.
Already have your reservation for friends properly booked? Here how youinvite guests to your experiencereservation and how youinvite guests to a servicethat you’ve booked.

title: Buying services and experiences for other people
content:
Buying services and experiences for other people
Airbnb services and experiences are amazing when they are shared.
For experiences, you can book and pay for up to 10 guests, but you must be one of the participants. You may be notified by email that you and any guests you invite need to have an Airbnb account andprovide IDbefore they can attend.
Services are private bookings and they can’t be purchased separately by other guests, but you caninvite guests to a servicethat you’ve booked.

title: Children attending services or experiences
content:
Children attending services or experiences
From a tea party with sheep in Scotland to horseback riding in Spain, hosts offer the most incredible family experiences. Of course, some experiences may not be right for all age levels—such as when a strenuous hike is involved. Services may also be geared more toward adults than children.
Hosts should have detailed descriptions that specify if the service or experience is suitable for children orhow old children must be to attend.
You can always contact the host before you book to ask questions about the skill level needed for the experience or if the service is child-friendly.  And remember, all children or guests under 18 years old must be accompanied by their legal guardian.

title: Dietary restrictions on Airbnb Experiences
content:
Dietary restrictions on Airbnb Experiences
This content was created by Airbnb and is consistent withPan American Health Organization (PAHO)technical standards for food safety.
Churros. Ramen. Ceremonial cacao. Airbnb Experiences have something for every palate. Of course, sometimes you’d rather keep an itemoffthe menu.
Hosts can often find ways to accommodate a variety of dietary restrictions, so everyone gets to enjoy the experience.
Let your Host know about any dietary restrictions or preferences you or your co-travelers have before you book. You can always message your Host before submitting your reservation request to ask questions.
Make it clear if you have an allergy or dietary restrictions—don’t simply ask your Host if certain ingredients are used or assume the online menu will list all the ingredients. It’s very important that your Host be aware of any allergies or dietary restrictions that your party may have, as it could affect other stages of the experience, such as food preparation and serving. Learn more abouthow to handle food allergies when attending an Airbnb Experience.
Remind your Host when you arrive for the experience about your dietary restrictions or preferences.
Hosts may be able to accommodate the following:
Dairy-free:Avoid cheese, lactose, or milk products, and their derivatives
Egg-free:Avoid eggs and their derivatives
Fish allergy:Avoid fish and their derivatives
Gluten-free:Avoid gluten, that can be found in grains like wheat, rye, and barley (these grains can be found in certain breads, pastas, cereals, and even soy sauce and some cleaning materials)
Peanut allergy:Avoid peanuts and foods that contain peanuts
Vegan:Includes plant-based foods only, and excludes animal foods of any kind—EX: meat, fish, fowl, eggs, honey, and dairy products
Vegetarian:Includes eggs, milk, milk products, and plant foods. Excludes meat, fish, and fowl
Pescatarian:Includes fish, excludes all other meats
Shellfish allergy:Avoid marine animals in the shellfish category (crustaceans and mollusks, such as shrimp, crab, lobster, squid, oysters, scallops, and others)
Soy-free:Avoid soybeans and all foods containing by-products of soybeans
Tree nut allergy:Avoid almonds, cashews, walnuts, pine nuts, lychee nuts, and other tree nuts
For more information on some of the common dietary restrictions or preferences Hosts may be able to accommodate, visit thePan American Health Organization (PAHO).

title: Buying services and experiences for other people
content:
Buying services and experiences for other people
Airbnb services and experiences are amazing when they are shared.
For experiences, you can book and pay for up to 10 guests, but you must be one of the participants. You may be notified by email that you and any guests you invite need to have an Airbnb account andprovide IDbefore they can attend.
Services are private bookings and they can’t be purchased separately by other guests, but you caninvite guests to a servicethat you’ve booked.

title: What to do if your home host doesn’t allow services
content:
What to do if your home host doesn’t allow services
Some home hosts don’t allow services to take place at their property. If a service isn’t allowed at your place, you won’t be able to book that location.
If you still want the service, you have a few options:
Find another home: if you want the service, but it’s not allowed at your home, you’ll need to find a different home or location.
Check with the service host for alternative locations or options: Message the service host and ask if the service can be provided at another location. Some may have a physical location available (ex: If you booked a dinner, ask if they have a restaurant or if they can deliver the food to you).
Cancel your service reservation: If the service can’t take place where you’re staying and no alternative is available, you can cancel your service for a refund as long as you’re within thecancellation window.

title: Booking services and experiences
content:
Booking services and experiences
Wine tastings. Dinner cooked in-home by a private chef. Family photos to remember an event. Airbnb services and experiences offer something for everyone.
Want to enhance your trip? Check out the services and experiences that are available during your stay. You can choose a specific city and dates, or browse through all of the services and experiences available—just visit theServicesorExperiencessections on the Airbnb website or app.
Any home, service, or experience you book will show up in yourTripson the itinerary timeline along with your other reservations. From there, you can tap to quickly get directions, add co-travelers, or message your host.
You don’t need to have a home booked on Airbnb in order to book a service or experience. You can find a great staycation activity or ways to celebrate a special event.
If you want to book a service at your Airbnb, review your host’s house rules first. Some hosts allow all services, while others may limit specific types or not permit them at all. You can contact your home host if you have questions. Learn more aboutadding a serviceto your reservation.
If a service you’re interested in isn’t allowed at your place, you’ll need to book it at an offsite location instead. For instance:
For chefs, ask if they can drop off prepared meal
For trainers, ask if they can meet you at a nearby public space for the workout
For hair, makeup, or nails, ask if they can work out of a nearby salon
For experiences, take a minute to review theguest requirementsand any other guidelines set by the host. Not all experiences are appropriate for allage levelsand some require special skills. Got questions? Feel free tocontact the host.

title: Search for listings with accessibility features
content:
Search for listings with accessibility features
We believe that anyone can belong anywhere—and we want to make it easier for people with disabilities and other accessibility needs to have a good time using Airbnb. You can search for a home, service, or experience that offers accessibility features such as a home with a step-free entrance or a service hosted in sign language.
If a listing that you’re interested in does not have any accessibility information, please contact the host to discuss your accessibility needs.
Listed below are some of the accessibility features that could be available for homes, services, and experiences. Contact your host for more detailed information about the specific accessibility features they offer and whether they meet your needs:
Home accessibility features:
Guest entrance and parking
Step-free access
Disabled parking spot
Guest entrance wider than 32 inches
Bedroom
Step-free bedroom access
Bedroom entrance wider than 32 inches
Bathroom
Step-free bathroom access
Bathroom entrance wider than 32 inches
Toilet grab bar
Shower grab bar
Step-free shower
Shower or bath chair
Adaptive equipment
Ceiling or mobile hoist
Experiences accessibility features:
Sign language options
Designated sighted guide
Step-free access
Entrances wider than 32 includes
Mainly flat or leveled ground
Step-free bathroom available
Disabled parking spot
No extreme sensory stimuli
Quiet retreat space available
Services accessibility feature:
Sign language options
Filter by accessibility features on desktop
ClickHomes,Services, orExperiences
Enter your destination, dates, and number of guests
Once your search results show, clickFilters
ClickAccessibility features
Select the features you want and clickShow results
Here’s where you can find more details on its accessibility features and determine if they meet your needs.
Listing description: Hosts may add accessibility information in the description of their home, service, or experience listing
Accessibility section: For homes, hosts will have added a detailed description of any accessibility feature they’ve selected in theAccessibilitysection
Things to know section: Selected accessibility features for experiences will be displayed in theThings to Knowsection of the listing
Contact the host: If you’d like more information on the accessibility features of a listing, or if you need to request a reasonable adjustment to access a home or participate in a service or experience, you can message the host directly
As part of our commitment to inclusion and respect for everyone in our community, ourNondiscrimination Policydoesn't allow hosts to treat a guest differently or turn them down simply because they have a disability.
OurAccessibility Policyalso sets expectations for hosts around accommodating requests for reasonable adjustments in order to allow someone with accessibility needs to use or enjoy a home, service, or experience.

title: Add a service to your home reservation
content:
Add a service to your home reservation
Want an in-home gourmet dinner prepared by a private chef? Or maybe you and your guests want to work out with a personal trainer during your stay. Reserving a service can make your trip even more special.
Airbnb Services make your stay more special by connecting you with providers who have beenvetted for quality. You can choose the perfect service from a variety of categories including:
Chefs
Catering
Prepared meals
Photography
Massage
Spa treatments
Hair styling
Personal training
Nails
Makeup
Service hosts either charge a fixed total cost for an offering or charge per-guest. Some have a minimum price per booking, which you’ll be able to review when booking.
Find and book services through theServicessection on the Airbnb website or app:
Select your city and travel dates
Browse by service type
Detailed listing pages highlight each host’s qualifications, offerings, and pricing
Choose a location, date, time, and number of guests (up to the service hosts’ limit)
Book the service that fits your needs
Once you book, your service provider will either come to you or you’ll meet them at their location.
Keep in mind, availability is set by the service host.
If you want to book a service at your Airbnb, review your host’s house rules first. Some hosts allow all services, while others may limit specific types or not permit them at all. You can view this on their home listing or contact your home host if you have questions.
You can book as many services or experiences as you’d like–just be sure to check schedules to avoid overlaps. Any home, service, or experience you book will show up in yourTrips, and you can evenexport them to your calendar.

title: What to do if your home host doesn’t allow services
content:
What to do if your home host doesn’t allow services
Some home hosts don’t allow services to take place at their property. If a service isn’t allowed at your place, you won’t be able to book that location.
If you still want the service, you have a few options:
Find another home: if you want the service, but it’s not allowed at your home, you’ll need to find a different home or location.
Check with the service host for alternative locations or options: Message the service host and ask if the service can be provided at another location. Some may have a physical location available (ex: If you booked a dinner, ask if they have a restaurant or if they can deliver the food to you).
Cancel your service reservation: If the service can’t take place where you’re staying and no alternative is available, you can cancel your service for a refund as long as you’re within thecancellation window.

title: Who is eligible for emergency stays through Airbnb.org
content:
Who is eligible for emergency stays through Airbnb.org
Airbnb.orgoffers free or discounted temporary housing for people displaced by humanitarian crises. To do this, we work with nonprofit organizations and resettlement agencies that connect people to temporary housing, resources, and specialized support in times of crisis. We also provide access to free and discounted housing offered by the Airbnb host community.
Airbnb.org doesn’t currently provide a direct intake process for individuals or accept funding requests from organizations. We share information about our upcoming grant cycle directly with eligible nonprofits.
After we’ve verified eligibility of a nonprofit organization, referral organization, or service provider, guests may also be required toset up an Airbnb account, which may includeverifying their identity.
Individuals who are eligible for our program may include:
People affected by major disasters, including relief workers responding to those disasters in an official capacity
Refugees, asylum seekers, Special Immigrant Visa (SIV) holders, and other newcomers

title: About Airbnb.org
content:
About Airbnb.org
Airbnb.orgis a nonprofit organization that works with other nonprofits, governments,  and resettlement agencies to provide people with temporary housing in times of crisis.
Through current programs and former initiatives such as Frontline Stays, we’ve worked with communities to help people in times of crisis since 2012.
Airbnb.org is an independent nonprofit organization with its own board of directors, but Airbnb covers its operating costs so that community donations go even further to support people in times of crisis.
Airbnb.org is a tax-exempt public charity under Section 501(c)(3) of the US Internal Revenue Code. Contributions to organizations with 501(c)(3) status may be tax deductible.
You can support Airbnb.org by hosting ordonating. Hosts may choose tohost Airbnb.org stays for freeor at a discount ordonate a percentage of each payout. Airbnb does not make money on Airbnb.org stays and covers all Airbnb.org’s operating costs, which means 100% of donations go directly to fund emergency housing.
Participating hosts receive anAirbnb.org supporter badgeon their profiles to recognize their contributions and help spread the word to our community.
Airbnb.org offers free or discounted temporary housing for people displaced by humanitarian crises. To do this, Airbnb.org works with nonprofit organizations and resettlement agencies that connect people to temporary housing, resources, and specialized support in times of crisis.
Airbnb.org doesn’t currently provide a direct intake process for individuals.
Airbnb.org guests may be required toset up an Airbnb account, which may includeverifying their identity.
Learn more aboutwho’s eligible for emergency stays.

title: How to read and send messages
content:
How to read and send messages
It all starts with a message, whether you’re a guest who has aquestion about a listingor a host who wants toshare information about your home, service, or experience. You’ll just need to be logged in to your Airbnb account first to read or send messages in the Airbnb app or website.
YourMessageswill help you keep your communication organized for all your trips. Every reservation automatically creates a group thread where all bookers, co-travelers, hosts and co-hosts can connect.
Just click on a message thread to read an existing message or send a new one. By default, read receipts are enabled for all messages on Airbnb to help keep communication between guests and hosts transparent.
If you’re a host, just go to yourMessagesand click on a message thread to read an existing message or send a new one.
If you’re hosting an experience and need to chat with a guest privately, you have that option. Just head toDetailsand click or tap the messaging icon next to their name underWho’s going.
Guests canmessage a hostbefore a reservation, after a trip is confirmed, or after the trip is completed. They can always find their message threads by going toMessages.
Need to get in contact with the host of an experience? You can contact them privately by heading toDetailsand tapping or clickingMessage host(s).
You can filter, search, andarchiveyour messages to stay on top of your conversations. Made a mistake? You can edit a message within 15 minutes after sending it, or unsend your message up to 24 hours after it’s sent.
Guests and hosts can reply directly to specific messages anywhere in the thread, so it’s easier to keep track of responses as conversations grow busier.
Guests and hosts are limited to 25 messages within any 24-hour period or 10 messages per hour. They’ll see an error message if they’ve reached their message limit.

title: Contacting hosts
content:
Contacting hosts
Getting in touch with a host on Airbnb is easy—just send them aquick messageto let them know you’re interested in their home, service, or experience.
For your safety and privacy, guests and hosts only receive each other's phone numbers after a reservation is confirmed. Learn more about why it’s good to keepcommunication and transactionson Airbnb.
If you have questions about a listing, click or tap Contact host to send them a message. Pro tip: If you’re asking about specific reservation details, enter your travel dates and number of guests first—this is required before you can send a message.
For homes and services
Once your home or service booking is accepted, you canMessageyour host (and co-host, if applicable), and all guests in a group message. Alternatively, you can reach your host by going toTrips, finding the reservation, and contacting them on the same group message from there.
If you’ve reserved a home or service and you haveco-travelers, you can invite them to join the reservation.
For experiences
Once your experience booking is accepted, you canMessageyour host, co-host, and all other guests on the experience—including others outside your booking group—in a group message.
If you want to message the host and co-hosts privately, you can do that from your group message thread by selectingDetails, and thenMessage hosts. This message will be private between you, the host, and any co-hosts.
If you’ve reserved an experience, you caninvite your friendsto join you.
You can send follow-up messages to your group or a private message to the  host after a trip, service, or experience has ended.
If you have any payment issues that you need to resolve, you can do so in theResolution Center.

title: Manage all your Airbnb messages
content:
Manage all your Airbnb messages
However you use Airbnb, it’s good to know that all your messages are organized and searchable in one place.
You have anAllview that unifies all guest, host, and support messages in one place. This means that if you're a host who’s also often a guest (and many are!) you don’t have to switch between hosting and traveling to manage your messages
Once the reservation is made, guests, hosts, and co-hosts can message each other in a single thread, making it much easier to coordinate plans and share information.
Booking or hosting an experience and need to message the host or a specific guest directly? Hosts and guests of experiences can alsodirectly message each other.
You can easily find whatever you’re looking for by applying a quick filter to differentiate the type of thread you want to access. Ex: Hosting, Support, and Traveling.
Read receipts show whether other recipients have viewed your messages, unless they’veturned off this featurein their account settings.
Can't quite remember a person’s name? No problem, just start typing to see all possible results in real-time. Search results take into account your quick filters as well, helping you to refine searches even faster.
Give guests a better idea of what you provide or share memories from a trip, service, or experience by sending visual attachments that can be referenced in each message thread’s Gallery. Find out more aboutsending photos and videosand how they can enhance trips, services, and experiences.
You can access these features by holding and pressing on a message for 1-2 seconds on the Airbnb app or hovering over a message on your desktop.
Edit messages
Correct your grammar and typos. Both guests and hosts can edit their message as many times as they want within 15 minutes after they send it.
Emoji reactions
Emojis let you acknowledge a message with a smiley face, heart, thumbs up, clapping hands, or laughing face.
Threaded replies
When you reply to a specific message as a thread, your response gets nested under the original message. Others can reply in the same way, creating a string of related messages.
Unsend messages
You can unsend a message within 24 hours of sending it—whether you’re a guest or a host.
No notifications are sent when a message is edited or unsent.
Others in the thread may have already seen the message before you unsent or edited it.
Hosts using third-party apps to access their messages may still be able to see previous versions of edited messages or unsent messages.
Booking threads that are over a year old will be closed. After that time, the message thread will read: 'This conversation is closed.'
We mayreview, scan, or analyze messages, including a record of unsent and edited messages, for purposes such as legal compliance, trust, safety, and customer support, and as described in ourPrivacy Policy.
Any messages you send, including any photos or videos you share, should comply with ourContent Policyand ourCommunity Standards. If you believe that a message you’ve received violates our policies, you can report the content directly through the app or bycontacting us.
You can stay in touch with guestconnectionsyou’ve traveled with–simply visit their profile and press Message to start a direct message thread. You can also stay in touch with other guests you met on an experience by visiting their profile and pressing Message, which starts a message request with them. If they accept, you can continue to communicate using direct messages.
If you decide to remove a connection for any reason, your direct message will remain open. To close a direct message, you canblockthe person from their profile.
Hosting starts with a warm welcome, and the first message with guests sets the tone for future conversations. But hosts can also save time by usingquick replies, which allow them to send personalized messages quickly and automatically to answer frequently asked guest questions. Learn more aboutmanaging guest messages.

title: Why we review messages on Airbnb
content:
Why we review messages on Airbnb
We care about the safety of our hosts and their guests. That’s why we’re careful when it comes to how everyone communicates with each other.
We may review or analyze your messages on Airbnb for safety, support, and to provide and improve our services, as we describe in ourPrivacy Policy. This includes:
Identifying risks to our users
Regulatory compliance
Investigation
Enforcing ourTerms of Serviceandcommunity policies, including ournon-discrimination policy
Providing customer support
Research and analytics
Personalizing and customizing your experience
Improving or expanding our products and services, including by developing and deploying certain AI features
Communicating or paying outside of Airbnbcan put you at risk, so one of the main reasons we analyze messages is to help protect againstonline fraud. That’s why it’s so important that you communicate only through the Airbnb message thread.
By communicating and booking directly on Airbnb, you’ll have access to our secure payments platform, 24/7 customer support, andHost Damage Protectionif you’re a host.
Sometimes our system may block messages from being sent. We may also suggest or require text to be removed from messages in certain circumstances, such as when our systems detect words or numbers that include contact information, references to other sites, external links, or content that violates ournon-discrimination policyor ouroff-platform and fee transparency policy.
We are a community built on trust. A fundamental part of earning that trust means being clear about how we use your information and protecting your human right to privacy. We never review, scan, or analyze your messaging communications to send you third-party marketing messages, and we don’t sell reviews or analyses of these communications.
You can also contact us toexercise your rightsover how we process your message data, including for certain AI features.

title: Messaging with suspended or deactivated users
content:
Messaging with suspended or deactivated users
Airbnb maylimit, suspend, or deactivatean account, as outlined in ourTerms of Service. If a host or guest you've messaged is suspended or their account deactivated, messages they sent you will be hidden in the message thread.
If a user is suspended or deactivated, that person’s messages to you will be replaced with text indicating that the message has been hidden because the person no longer has access to Airbnb.
Depending on the nature of the situation, the suspended user and their messages may eventually be restored.
Your messages with the person will show as they did before the suspension or deactivation.

title: Archive messages
content:
Archive messages
Archiving your messages allows you to tidy up yourMessagesbut keep the message threads available for you to access later.
Keep in mind that if an archived message thread receives a new message from a host or guest, it will reappear in yourAll messagesfolder.
Only full message threads between a host, co-host, or guest can be archived—not individual messages.
Archive a conversation on desktop
ClickMessages
Click thethree dotsnext to the message thread you want to archive
ClickArchive
At this time, it’s not possible to delete individual messages or entire message threads on Airbnb. Archiving removes conversations from your messages, but they can still be accessed.
Individual messages that you've sentcan be unsent within 24 hoursafter sending.
To manage message volume, old transactional and support message threads are automatically archived. Threads that are over 1 year old are read-only, and messages older than 5 years are deleted.
Restore your archived messages on desktop
ClickMessages
ClickSettingsand then clickArchived
Click thethree dotsnext to the message thread you want to restore
ClickUnarchive

title: Turn off message read receipts
content:
Turn off message read receipts
Read receipts can help keep communication transparent by letting hosts and guests know when a message they've sent has been read. By default, read receipts are turned on for all messages on Airbnb, but they can be turned off.
Turn off read receipts on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Privacy
UnderRead receipts, click to turn them off (the checkmark means on)
When you turn your read receipts off, other people can’t tell when you’ve read their messages. If the other person has their read receipts turned on, you’ll still be able to check if they’ve read your message.
The name (or names if there’s more than one person in the message thread) of who has read your message shows below the individual message next to “Read by.” If anyone in the message thread has their read receipts turned off, that person’s name won’t show, even if they’ve read your message.

title: Turn on notifications for host and guest messages
content:
Turn on notifications for host and guest messages
Responding to messages in a timely manner is a great way to build rapport between hosts and guests. It’s easy to set up your preferences so that you receive email, browser, and/or SMS (text) notifications when a host or guest sends you a message.
Set up your notifications so you know when a host or guest messages you.
Turn on notifications on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Notifications
ClickAccount
UnderGuest and host messages, clickEdit
EnableEmail,SMS,and/orPushnotifications

title: Send documents in a message
content:
Send documents in a message
Attaching and sending documents, like a PDF, directly in messages isn't possible—however, you can share a link to your document if it’s hosted on a file-sharing platform such as Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, or WeTransfer.
Once you’ve uploaded your document to your preferred file-sharing platform, you can share the link in messages with a host or guest.
Send document links on desktop
ClickMessagesand select the thread you want
Type or paste in the link to your document that’s been uploaded to a file-sharing platform
ClickSend
Most file-hosting platforms allow you to adjust privacy settings, so you can control who has access to your file—you may also be able to restrict access or require a password. Make sure to check the settings to ensure that your host or guest can access your document.
Please keep in mind that using email and messaging must comply with ourOff-Platform Policy. You shouldn’t use email or messaging to ask for guests’ contact information before they book a stay, or misuse their contact information.

title: Send photos and videos in a message
content:
Send photos and videos in a message
Once your reservation for a home, service or experience is confirmed, you can share photos and videos up to 60 seconds in length in messages with your host or guests.
All of the photos and videos sent between guests, hosts, and co-hosts can be viewed in theGallerysection of yourDetails.
Photos and videos can be a valuable part of the communication process. For instance, you can send attachments to:
Show guests a video of where your service or experience will take place
Share photos of a walking tour with other guests
Share a video of a catered meal service that you booked
Show a guest how the hot tub works
Share photos or video at checkout of how you left your host’s home
Photos and videos can only be sent after a reservation is confirmed.
Send photos and videos on desktop
ClickMessagesand select the message thread
ClickAdd +
ClickAdd photo or videoand select what you want to send
Review and clickSend
Having trouble sharing the photo or video you want? Make sure you have the latest Airbnb app installed.
Any messages you send, including any photos or videos you share, should comply with ourContent Policyand ourCommunity Standards. If you believe that a message you’ve received violates our policies, you can report the content directly through the app or bycontacting us.

title: When to message your host
content:
When to message your host
You’re more than welcome tocontact a hostat any time, whether you’re booking a home, experience, or service.
Before sending a trip request to reserve a home: You can double-check details about the place
If you have a question: You should be clear about what to expect. Just check the listing description to make sure the answer isn’t already there
If you want to work out the details of a service: If you’re interested in a service that can be customized, you can message the host to work out the details
If you’re already booked a home, service, or experience, but you need more info: You can use your message thread with your host or you can go to yourTrips
If you need to share important info: You can let your host know if you have a food allergy or if you’ll need specific help during your reservation
Unless you’ve found anInstant Booklisting, you’ll have to ask that the host accept your request to book. At theConfirm and paystage of your request, you’ll need to include a brief message about why you’re traveling and when you’ll check-in.
If you're booking a home, just be careful not to send more than one trip request for the same dates to ensure you don’t end up double booked.
Most hosts respond within a few hours, but time zones and lack of internet access might slow things down.

title: Travel insurance for residents of the European Union and the United Kingdom
content:
Travel insurance for residents of the European Union and the United Kingdom
Travel is rewarding, but sometimes things don’t go as expected. People can get sick, flights can get canceled, and bags can go missing. To help protect you financially, there’s travel insurance.
Airbnb guests who live in certain countries in the European Union and the United Kingdom can purchase travel insurance on the checkout page. The policy is underwritten by Europ Assistance, a leading travel insurer.
Don’t live in the EU or the United Kingdom? Travel Insurance is also available toresidents of the United Statesandresidents of certain Canadian provinces.Stay Protectionis available to residents of Australia. And guests everywhere can also purchase travel insurance outside of Airbnb, directly through a travel insurance company’s website or by shopping around on a comparison site.
If eligible, you’ll be given the option to add travel insurance on the checkout page. Before you do, be sure to review the details of the insurance policy to make sure it’s right for you.
Shortly after your stay is confirmed, you’ll get an email confirming your travel insurance purchase. That email will include your insurance certificate as well as a link to your policy details page, where you can cancel your policy, start a claim, and more.
If you change the dates of your stay, your travel insurance premium will be automatically updated. If the change impacts the total cost of your stay, your travel premium will be adjusted, and you’ll be charged or refunded accordingly.
Learn more about changing an Airbnb stay.
Travel insurance through Europ Assistance covers:
Reservation cancellation:Get reimbursed (up to 100% of the cost of your stay on Airbnb) if you cancel your stay for a covered reason. Covered reasons include serious illness or injury; travel carrier cancellation or delays of 12 hours or more due to mechanical breakdown; and a natural disaster at your home or destination.
Flights, tours, activities, and more:Get reimbursed up to the applicable policy limits if you  or a guest in your group have to cancel any transportation or activities, because your stay was cancelled. All transportation and activities must be pre-booked and non-refundable to be covered. Transportation is covered as long as it’s within 48 hours before and after your stay.
Medical assistance:Get help paying for emergency and necessary medical expenses that you or a guest in your group needs while traveling, such as hospitalisation costs, medication prescribed by a doctor, or repatriation back to your home. Coverage will vary depending on where you’re located and if you’re travelling domestically or internationally.
Personal liability:Get reimbursed if you or a guest in your group accidentally damage property or  cause an injury to someone during your trip and are legally required to pay them. This benefit doesn’t cover legal expenses, damage to the host’s property, or damage caused by professional activity or employment.
Trip interruption:Get reimbursed for nights your group can’t stay at your Airbnb because the trip is delayed or cut short.This benefit also covers the cost of pre-booked, non-refundable activities and some additional transportation costs if you trip is delayed or cut short.
Delayed departure:Get reimbursed for reasonable expenses (such as meals, accommodations, and phone calls) you incur because your trip is delayed for 4 hours or more for a covered reason. Covered reasons include a storm, fire, or flood at the departure point; mechanical breakdown of your vehicle; and loss of travel documents or money.
Luggage:Get reimbursed if your luggage is stolen, or lost or damaged by your travel carrier.
Luggage delay:Get reimbursed for necessary purchases you make because your luggage is delayed by your carrier for more than 24 hours. Covered expenses may include clothing, food, and toiletries.
Sporting equipment:Get reimbursed if your sporting equipment is damaged by your carrier, stolen, or lost permanently.
Sporting equipment delay:Get reimbursed for the cost of sporting equipment rentals if your sporting equipment is delayed for more than 24 hours.
Additional benefits:Get covered if you or a guest in your group need any additional benefits for a personal accident.
Coverage and limit amounts may vary by country, so make sure to check the travel insurance  you’ve purchased for exact information.
Travel outside of your destination zone or original trip dates:You are not covered in countries outside of your destination zone or if you choose to travel outside of the original dates on your reservation.
Cancellation and interruption benefits for other accommodation:If your stay is cancelled and you choose to rebook, this policy won’t provide cancellation and interruption benefits if you book a different accommodation.
Pre-existing medical conditions (UK only):If you’re traveling with medical conditions, you may need additional or alternative coverage. For information, contact the Money and Pensions Service by calling +44 (0) 80 0138 7777 or searching their medical cover firm directory.
To check your destination zone and understand your benefits, review the Insurance Terms & Conditions.
Every policy covers the guest who purchased it and the people, up to 21 in total, who are staying with them at the accommodation you’ve booked on Airbnb. These people don’t have to be added to your stay or named on the policy in order to be covered, and there are no age restrictions.
You can cancel your policy for a full premium refund if you cancel within 14 days of purchase if you are in the EU (excluding France) and UK, and within 30 days of purchase if you’re in France. Please note that you can’t cancel your policy after you’ve checked-in to your accommodation or started a claim, even if you’re within the time period.
To cancel your policy for the EU, France, and UK,  login to your Airbnb account, go toTripsand click or tapYour Reservation, then go toTravel insuranceandCoverage overview.You’ll find a section to cancel your travel insurance.
Europ Assistance has dedicated support for Airbnb guests.
If you have questions about your policy or need medical assistance, send an email toinfoairbnb@roleurop.com, or call the phone number available in your policy.
If you need help with a claim, send an email toclaimsairbnb@roleurop.com.

title: Travel insurance through Europ Assistance
content:
Travel insurance through Europ Assistance
Note:This article is relevant if you purchased travel insurance with Europ Assistance before March 31, 2025 (for Customers in the Netherlands), April 17, 2025 (for Customers in Ireland, Italy, Austria, Spain, Portugal and Germany) and May 15, 2025 for Customers in the UK.  If you purchased reservation insurance through Europ Assistance before April 17, 2025 (for Customers in France), please refer tothis articlefor coverage information.
As of September 2022, guests who live in the United Kingdom, Germany, Spain, Italy, Ireland, Netherlands, Austria, and Portugal can purchase travel insurance on the checkout page.
The travel insurance policies are offered by Generali's Europ Assistance Group, trading under the Europ Assistance name in the EU and UK.
Adding insurance while booking
You’ll be given the option to add travel insurance on the checkout page when making your Airbnb booking. Before you do, be sure to review the details of the insurance policy being offered and what’s covered to make sure it’s right for you.
Shortly after your reservation is confirmed, you’ll get an email confirming your insurance purchase and providing a copy of your insurance policy. That email will also include a link to your policy details page, which is where you can cancel your policy, start a claim, and more.
Making changes to your reservation
Your travel insurance policy will be automatically updated if the dates of your reservation or guest count change and there is no claim on the existing policy. If the change impacts the total price of your reservation, you’ll be charged or refunded an adjusted amount for your travel insurance.
What the policy covers
The Europ Assistance travel insurance policy includes:
Travel cancellation: Get reimbursed for up to 100% of your non-refunded Airbnb reservation cost if you cancel for a covered reason, such as: serious illness or injury, a flight delay due to mechanical breakdown, or a natural disaster at your home or destination.
Delayed departure: Get reimbursed for certain additional expenses you incur because your trip is delayed for 12 hours or more for a covered reason. Covered reasons include a storm, fire, or flood at the departure point; mechanical breakdown of your vehicle; and loss of travel documents or money.
Medical assistance: Get reimbursed for medical expenses you incur while traveling abroad, such as: hospitalisation costs, medication prescribed by a doctor, or ambulance journeys ordered by a doctor.
Luggage: Get reimbursed if your luggage is lost definitively or damaged by your carrier.
What’s not covered
Pre-existing medical conditions: If you’re traveling with medical conditions, you may need additional or alternative coverage. For information, contact the Money and Pensions Service by calling +44 (0) 80 0138 7777 or searching theirmedical cover firm directory.
Certain travel costs: You can’t get reimbursed for the cost of planned transport (including flights, trains, and ferries) to and from your Airbnb listing if you need to cancel your reservation before you depart on your trip.
Is COVID-19 covered?
Although pandemics aren’t covered by travel insurance, COVID-19 is covered in some situations because it’s considered to be a serious illness. Unexpected illness is a covered reason under the travel cancellation, medical assistance, and travel curtailment benefits. You’re not covered for travel disruptions caused by COVID-19, like border closures and quarantine mandates.
Please note that if you file a claim due to COVID-19, you’ll be asked to provide documentation showing that you, a family member (for example, your child), or someone staying with you at the Airbnb accommodation tested positive while under the virtual or in-person supervision of a qualified medical professional.
Who’s covered
Every policy covers the guest who purchased it and the people who are staying with them at the Airbnb accommodation. These people don’t have to be added to the Airbnb reservation or named on the policy in order to be covered, and there are no age restrictions.
Canceling your policy
UK residents can cancel their policy within 14 days of purchase for a full premium refund. Please note that you can’t cancel your policy after you’ve departed on your trip or started a claim, even if you’re within the 14-day cooling-off period.
To cancel your policy, just go to your coverage overview page and selectCancel policy.
Starting a claim
Claims are handled by Europ Assistance from start to finish. You can start a claim by going to your coverage overview page and selecting Start a claim or by contacting Europ Assistance directly.
How to get help
If you have questions about your policy, need help with a claim, or want travel assistance contact Europ Assistance by calling +44 (0) 203 7888 656 or emailinginfoairbnb@roleurop.com.
For UK residents, travel insurance is underwritten by Europ Assistance S.A. UK Branch.
Europ Assistance S.A. is supervised by the French Supervisory authority (ACPR), 4, place de Budapest, CS92459 - 75436 Paris Cedex 09, France. Europ Assistance S.A. UK Branch is authorized by the Prudential Regulation Authority. Subject to regulation by the Financial Conduct Authority and limited regulation by the Prudential Regulation Authority. Details about the extent of our regulation by the Prudential Regulation Authority are available on request.Europ Assistance S.A. UK Branch FCA register number is 203084.
For UK residents, this travel insurance is arranged by Airbnb UK Services Limited. Airbnb UK Services Limited is an appointed representative of Aon UK Limited, who is authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority. Aon UK Limited’s FCA register number is 310451, you can check this by visiting theFinancial Services Register | FCAor calling 0800 111 6768. Travel Insurance is available to guests aged 18 and over. Full terms and conditions apply. Medical assistance: Get reimbursed for medical expenses you incur while traveling abroad, such as: hospitalisation costs, medication prescribed by a doctor, or ambulance journeys ordered by a doctor.
Luggage: Get reimbursed if your luggage is lost definitively or damaged by your carrier.

Unexpected illness is a covered reason under the travel cancellation, medical assistance, and travel curtailment benefits.You’re not covered for travel disruptions caused by COVID-19, like border closures and quarantine mandates.
Please note that if you file a claim due to COVID-19, you’ll be asked to provide documentation showing that you, a family member (for example, your child), or someone staying with you at the Airbnb accommodation tested positive while under the virtual or in-person supervision of a qualified medical professional.

EU residents (excluding France) can cancel their policy within 14 days of purchase for a full premium refund. Please note that you can’t cancel your policy after you’ve departed on your trip or started a claim, even if you’re within the 14-day grace period.
To cancel your policy, just go to your coverage overview page and selectCancel policy.
Starting a claim
Claims are handled by Europ Assistance from start to finish. You can start a claim by going to your coverage overview page and selectingStart a claimor by contacting Europ Assistance directly.

If you have questions about your policy, need help with a claim, or want travel assistance:

title: Travel insurance and assistance services for US residents
content:
Travel insurance and assistance services for US residents
Travel is rewarding, but sometimes things don’t go as expected. People can get sick, flights can get canceled, and bags can go missing. To help protect you financially, there’s travel insurance and assistance services.
Airbnb guests who live in the United States can add travel insurance and assistance services on the checkout page or after booking. The policy is underwritten by Generali US Branch and offered by Airbnb Insurance Agency LLC.
Adding insurance to your reservation
If your reservation is eligible, you’ll be given the option to add travel insurance and assistance services on the checkout page. If your reservation is eligible, you’ll also be given the option to add travel insurance and assistance services after you book—just look for theAdd to your tripbutton on your booking confirmation email and reservation details page.
Before adding travel insurance and assistance services to your reservation, be sure to review all the details to make sure it’s right for you.
Shortly after your travel insurance and assistance services purchase is confirmed, you’ll get an email that includes a copy of your plan, as well as a link to your plan details page, where you can cancel your plan, start a claim, and more.
Making changes to your reservation
Your travel insurance and assistance services plan will be automatically updated if the dates of your reservation or guest count change and there is no claim on the existing policy. If the change impacts the total price of your reservation, you’ll be charged or refunded an adjusted amount for your travel insurance and assistance services.
What’s covered
Travel insurance through Generali covers:
Trip cancellation: Get reimbursed for up to 100% of your Airbnb reservation cost if you need to cancel for a covered reason, such as illness or a flight cancellation due to weather. Note that events that have already happened or are foreseeable when you purchase the policy, such as a named hurricane, aren’t covered.
Travel delay:Get reimbursed up to $750 per person if your trip is delayed for 24 hours or more for a covered reason, such as a flight delay or stolen passport. Covered expenses may include meals, a place to stay, and parking costs.
Medical: Get reimbursed up to $50,000 per person for hospital and ambulance services, prescription drugs, and certain other doctor-ordered medical services. Note that pre-existing medical conditions may not be covered, as outlined below.
Baggage: Get reimbursed up to $1,000 for luggage, personal items, and travel documents that are lost, stolen, or damaged. Note that portable electronics like laptops and cell phones aren’t covered.
Baggage delay: Get reimbursed up to $250 per person for clothing, toiletries, and certain other expenses if your baggage is delayed for 24 hours or more.
Sporting equipment: Get reimbursed up to $1,000 per person for sporting equipment that’s lost, stolen, or damaged. Note that normal wear and tear isn’t covered.
Sporting equipment delay: Get reimbursed up to $1,000 per person for rentals and certain other expenses if your sporting equipment is delayed for 24 hours or more.
Trip interruption: Get reimbursed up to 150% of your Airbnb reservation cost for unused nights if your trip is delayed or cut short for a covered reason, such as illness or injury. Certain transportation expenses may also be covered.
Emergency transportation: Get reimbursed up to $100,000 per policy for medical evacuation, and certain other expenses if you get sick or injured while on your trip.
Coverage for COVID-19
Although pandemics aren’t covered by travel insurance, COVID-19 may be covered in some situations because it’s an illness.
Please note that if you file a claim due to COVID-19, you’ll be asked to provide documentation showing that you, a family member (for example, your child), or someone staying with you at the Airbnb accommodation tested positive while under the virtual or in-person supervision of a qualified medical professional.
Coverage for pre-existing conditions
A pre-existing medical condition is an illness or injury that first manifested itself, got worse, or required treatment in the 180-day period before the insurance was purchased.
Pre-existing conditions are covered as long as you buy insurance within 15 days of booking, and while you’re medically able to travel. (Note that the 15-day requirement doesn’t apply to New York residents, who must only be medically able to travel at the time of purchasing insurance.)
Consult your policy for further details.
Assistance services
Each plan comes with assistance services like a 24-hour emergency hotline, concierge services, and roadside assistance. The amount you’ll be charged for travel protection includes a fee for these services.
Who’s covered
Each plan covers you and up to 9 people staying with you at the Airbnb accommodation. These people don’t have to be added to the Airbnb reservation or named on the policy in order to be covered, and there are no age restrictions.
Canceling your plan
US residents can cancel their travel insurance and assistance services plan within 10 days of purchase for a full premium refund. Please note that you can’t cancel your plan after you’ve departed on your trip or started a claim, even if you’re within your cancellation period.
To cancel your plan, just go to your plan details page and selectCancel plan.
Filing a claim
Claims are handled by Generali from start to finish. You can start a claim by going to your policy details page and selectingStart a claim, or by contacting Generali directly.
How to get help
If you have questions about your travel insurance and assistance services, need help with a claim, or want travel assistance, contact Generali by calling (866) 757-0010 or emailinghelp@generalitravelinsurance.com.
For medical and travel emergencies, call (877) 243-4135 or collect worldwide at (240) 330-1529.
Travel insurance coverages are underwritten by: Generali U.S. Branch, NY (NAIC #11231) and offered by Airbnb Insurance Agency LLC, CA Lic. No. 6001912.

title: Reservation insurance for France residents
content:
Reservation insurance for France residents
Note:This article applies to reservation insurance purchased prior to April 17, 2025. If you purchased travel insurance after April 17, 2025, please refer tothis article.
Travel is rewarding, but sometimes things don’t go as expected. People can get sick, flights can get canceled, and bags can go missing. To help protect you financially, there’s reservation insurance.
Airbnb guests who live in France can purchase reservation insurance on the checkout page. The policy is underwritten by Europ Assistance, a leading travel insurer.
If eligible, you’ll be given the option to add reservation insurance on the checkout page. Before you do, be sure to review the details of the insurance policy to make sure it’s right for you.
Shortly after your reservation is confirmed, you’ll get an email confirming your insurance purchase. That email will include your insurance certificate as well as a link to your policy details page, where you can cancel your policy, start a claim, and more.
If the dates of your reservation change (or if the guest count changes), your reservation insurance will be automatically updated. If the change impacts the total cost of your reservation, your reservation insurance premium will be adjusted, and you’ll be charged or refunded accordingly.
Learn more about changing an Airbnb reservation.
Reservation insurance through Europ Assistance covers:
Reservation cancellation:Get reimbursed (up to 100% of your Airbnb reservation cost) if you cancel your Airbnb reservation for a covered reason. Covered reasons include serious illness or injury; a flight delay due to mechanical breakdown; and a natural disaster at your home or destination.
