Medical assistance:Get help paying (up to €50,000 per person) for emergency and pre-authorised medical services you need while travelling abroad, such as hospitalisation costs, medication prescribed by a doctor, and ambulance journeys ordered by a doctor. (Please note that the €50,000 per person limit applies to residents of all EU countries where reservation insurance is offered except Germany, where medical assistance coverage is unlimited.)
Delayed departure:Get reimbursed (up to €750 per person) for reasonable expenses you incur because your trip is delayed for 12 hours or more for a covered reason. Covered reasons include a storm, fire, or flood at the departure point; mechanical breakdown of your vehicle; and loss of travel documents or money.
Luggage:Get reimbursed (up to €1,000 per person) if your luggage is damaged by your carrier, stolen, or permanently lost.
Luggage delay:Get reimbursed (up to €250 per person) for necessary purchases you make because your luggage is delayed by your carrier for more than 24 hours. Covered expenses may include clothing, food, and toiletries.
Sporting equipment:Get reimbursed (up to €1,000 per person) if your sporting equipment is damaged by your carrier, stolen, or lost permanently.
Sporting equipment delay:Get reimbursed (up to €1,000 per person) for the cost of sporting equipment rentals if your sporting equipment is delayed for more than 24 hours.
Reservation curtailment:Get reimbursed (up to 125% of your Airbnb reservation cost) for nights you can’t stay at your Airbnb accommodation, and additional transportation expenses you incur, because your trip is delayed or cut short for a covered reason. Covered reasons include serious illness or injury; delay of a flight, train, or boat; and passport theft.
Personal accident:Get reimbursed (up to €100,000 per person) if you’re injured in an accident while you’re on your trip, resulting in permanent total disability or the loss of your life, limb, or sight.
Travel documents:Get reimbursed (up to 10 days at €80 per night) for additional travel and accommodation expenses you incur because your passport or ID are lost or stolen while you’re traveling abroad.
Although pandemics aren’t covered by reservation insurance, COVID-19 is covered in some situations because it’s considered to be a serious illness. Serious illness is a covered reason under the reservation cancellation, medical assistance, and reservation curtailment benefits.
Please note that if you file a claim due to COVID-19, you’ll be asked to provide documentation showing that you, a family member (for example, your child), or someone staying with you at the Airbnb accommodation tested positive while under the virtual or in-person supervision of a qualified medical professional.
As is the case with all insurance policies, there are terms, conditions, and exclusions that apply. Generally, foreseeable events and events that aren’t named in the policy aren’t covered.
Specifically, EU residents should know that the Europ Assistance policy doesn’t include certain travel costs: You can't get reimbursed for the cost of planned transport (flights, trains, ferries, etc.) to and from your Airbnb accommodation if you need to cancel your reservation before you depart on your trip.
Every policy covers the guest who purchased it and the people who are staying with them at the Airbnb accommodation. These people don’t have to be added to the Airbnb reservation or named on the policy in order to be covered, and there are no age restrictions.
Your reservation insurance will be automatically updated if you change the dates of your Airbnb reservation, or the guest count.
If the change impacts the cost of your reservation, your reservation insurance premium will be adjusted and you’ll be charged or refunded accordingly.
Residents of France can cancel within 30 days of purchase. Please note that you can’t cancel your policy after you’ve departed on your trip or started a claim, even if you’re within the 30-day period.
To cancel your policy, just go to your policy details page and select Cancel policy. Alternatively, you can send an email toreservation-insurance-cancellation-help@airbnb.com.
Claims are handled by Europ Assistance from start to finish. You can start a claim by going to your policy details page and selecting Start a claim. Alternatively, you can contact Europ Assistance by sending an email toclaimsairbnb@roleurop.comor calling +33 1 57 32 73 04 (France residents only).
You can file a complaint if you have issues with reservation insurance.
If you have issues related to the distribution of your policy, your coverage, medical assistance, or a claim, contact Europ Assistance by sending an email tocomplaints_eaib_fr@roleurop.comor calling +33 157-327-304 (France residents only).
Europ Assistance has dedicated support for Airbnb guests.
If you have questions about your policy or need medical assistance, send an email toinfoairbnb@roleurop.comor call +33 157 327 304 (France residents only).
If you need help with a claim, send an email toclaimsairbnb@roleurop.comor call +33 157 327 304 (France residents only).

title: Travel insurance and assistance services for residents in certain Canadian Provinces
content:
Travel insurance and assistance services for residents in certain Canadian Provinces
Travel is rewarding, but sometimes things don’t go as expected. People  get sick, flights  get canceled, and bags  go missing. To help protect you financially, there’s travel insurance and assistance services.
Airbnb guests who live in certain Provinces in Canada can add travel insurance and assistance services on the checkout page. The insurance policy is underwritten by Europ Assistance S.A. Canada Branch and offered by Airbnb Canada Insurance Services Inc.
Adding insurance to your reservation
If your reservation is eligible, you’ll be given the option to add travel insurance and assistance services on the checkout page.
Before adding travel insurance and assistance services to your reservation, be sure to review all the details to make sure it’s right for you.
Shortly after your travel insurance and assistance services purchase is confirmed, you’ll get an email that includes a copy of your insurance policy, as well as a link to your insurance details page, where you cancel your insurance, start a claim, and more.
Making changes to your reservation
Your travel insurance and assistance services plan will be automatically updated if the dates of your reservation or guest count change and there is no claim on the existing policy. If the change impacts the total price of your reservation, you’ll be charged or refunded an adjusted amount for your travel insurance and assistance services.
What's covered
Travel insurance through Europ Assistance covers:
Trip cancellation:Get reimbursed for up to 100% of your Airbnb reservation cost if you need to cancel for a covered reason, such as illness, injury, flight cancellation due to weather or mandatory evacuation due to weather. Note that events that have already happened or are foreseeable when you purchase the policy, such as a named hurricane, aren’t covered.
Emergency medical:Get reimbursed up to CAD $5,000,000 (for comprehensive plans covering international trips) or up to CAD $25,000 (for domestic plans covering domestic trips) for approved emergency medical services during your trip such as hospital and ambulance services or dental services. Note that pre-existing medical conditions may not be covered, as outlined below.
Trip interruption:Get reimbursed for up to 150% of your Airbnb reservation cost for unused nights if your trip is delayed or interrupted after departure for a covered event such as illness, injury or flight cancellations or delays due to severe weather. Note that airport taxes, port taxes, insurance premium, services fees and booked activities during the trip aren’t covered.
Missed departure:Get reimbursed for up to CAD $1,000 per person for the costs of extra accommodation and transport to get to your destination or back home if your transport does not follow its original scheduled time or the vehicle you are traveling has a breakdown.
Delayed departure:Get reimbursed up to CAD $1,500 per person if your trip is delayed for 4 hours or more for a covered reason, such as a fire, storm, strike, bad weather. Covered expenses may include meals, accommodations, and parking costs.
Baggage:Get reimbursed up to CAD $3,000 per person if your baggage is stolen by violent robbery or is lost or damaged caused by your travel carrier.
Baggage delay:Get reimbursed up to CAD $500 per person for clothing, toiletries, and certain other expenses if the delivery of your checked-in baggage is delayed for 24 hours or more.
Sporting equipment:Get reimbursed up to CAD $1,000 per person for sporting equipment that’s lost, stolen, or damaged. Note that normal wear and tear isn’t covered.
Sporting equipment delay:Get reimbursed up to CAD $1,000 per person for rentals and certain other expenses if your sporting equipment is delayed for 24 hours or more.
Passport and travel documents:Get reimbursed up to CAD $500 per person for expenses incurred if your passport is lost or stolen outside your home country during your trip.
Flight accident:Get compensated up to CAD $100,000 if you suffer death, loss of limb, loss of sight or permanent total disability as a result of injury sustained in a flight accident during your trip
Travel Accident:Get compensated or up to CAD $50,000 if you suffer death, loss of limb, loss of sight or permanent total disability as a result of injury sustained in a travel accident.
Coverage for COVID-19
Although pandemics aren’t covered by travel insurance, COVID-19 may be covered in some situations because it’s an illness.
Please note that if you file a claim due to COVID-19, you’ll be asked to provide documentation showing that you, a family member (for example, your child), or someone staying with you at the Airbnb accommodation tested positive while under the virtual or in-person supervision of a qualified medical professional.
Coverage for pre-existing conditions
A pre-existing medical condition is a disease, illness or injury that exists before the insurance was purchased.
Pre-existing conditions are covered but certain stability conditions apply for comprehensive plans covering international trips. Consult your policy for further details.
Assistance services
Each plan comes with assistance services like a 24-hour emergency hotline and concierge services. The amount you’ll be charged for travel insurance includes a fee for these services.
Who’s covered
Each travel insurance and assistance services plan covers you and up to 9 people staying with you at the Airbnb accommodation. These people don’t have to be added to the Airbnb reservation or named on the policy in order to be covered, and there are no age restrictions.
Canceling your plan
Canada residents can cancel their travel insurance and assistance services plan within 10 days of purchase for a full premium refund. Please note that you are not able to cancel your plan after you've departed on your trip or started a claim, even if you’re within your cancellation period.
To cancel your plan, just go to your plan details page and selectCancel plan.
Filing a claim
Claims are handled by Europ Assistance from start to finish. You  start a claim by going to your policy details page and selectingStart a claim, or by contacting Europ Assistance directly.
How to get help
If you have questions about your travel insurance and assistance services, need help with a claim, or want travel assistance, contact Europ Assistance by calling 1 (888) 352-1311 or send an email to:service@europ-assistance.ca.
For medical and travel emergencies, call 1 (888) 352-1311 or local collect call at 1 (416) 874-1311.
Travel insurance coverages are underwritten by: Europ Assistance S.A. Canada Branch and offered by Airbnb Canada Insurance Services Inc.

title: Stay Protection for residents in Australia
content:
Stay Protection for residents in Australia
Travel is rewarding, but sometimes things don’t go as expected. People get sick, flights get canceled, and bags go missing. To help protect you financially, there’s Stay Protection.
Airbnb guests who live in Australia can add Stay Protection for their Airbnb reservation on the checkout page. The stay protection policy is underwritten by Chubb Insurance Australia Limited and offered by Airbnb Australia Insurance Services Pty Ltd.
Adding Stay Protection to your reservation
If your reservation is eligible, you’ll be given the option to add Stay Protection on the checkout page.
Before adding Stay Protection to your reservation, be sure to review the combinedProduct Disclosure Statement & Financial Services Guideand theTarget Market Determinationto make sure it’s right for you.
Shortly after your Stay Protection purchase is confirmed, you’ll get an email that includes a copy of your Stay Protection policy, as well as a link to your plan details page, where you cancel your Stay Protection, start a claim, and more.
Making changes to your reservation
Your Stay Protection policy will be automatically updated if the dates of your Airbnb reservation change and there is no claim on the existing policy. If the change impacts the total price of your reservation, you’ll be charged or refunded an adjusted amount for your Stay Protection. You will not receive a refund if you've already started your trip, or you've made a claim against the policy.
What's covered
Stay Protection through Chubb Australia covers:
Trip cancellation and amendment:Get reimbursed for up to 100% of your Airbnb reservation cost if you need to cancel or cut short your trip for a covered reason, such as unforeseen illness, injury, flight cancellation due to weather, or you can't stay at the listing you booked due to a natural disaster.
Emergency overseas medical:Get unlimited cover per person (for international trips), subject to certain benefit limits, for approved overseas emergency medical services during your trip, such as hospital and ambulance services. Note that pre-existing medical conditions are not covered, as outlined below.
Emergency medical evacuation:Unlimited per person. In case of medical emergencies, get covered for evacuation or transportation and other expenses to get to a hospital.
Flights and other transport:Get up to $1,000 AUD per person (domestic) or up to $5,000 AUD per person (international) if you or a guest in your group has to cancel transportation (such as flights or ferries) to or from your Airbnb reservation for a covered reason. To be covered, the original travel itinerary must have been within 48 hours from your Airbnb reservation. All transportation has to have been pre-planned and non-refundable.
Missed connecting flight:For international plans, get reimbursed up to $10,000 AUD per person if you or a guest in your group missed a flight connection due to your incoming connecting flight arriving late. To be covered, your original travel itinerary must be within 48 hours of your Airbnb reservation.
Rental vehicle excess:Get reimbursed up to $5,000 AUD if you or a guest in your group rents a vehicle, gets into an accident, and has to pay the rental vehicle excess.
Flight delay:Get reimbursed up to $250 AUD per person if your flight is delayed for 6 hours or more, or if you are denied boarding due to overbooking and not offered an alternative for 6 hours or more. You may be eligible for more if your flight is delayed by another 24 hours, after the first delay, up to a total of $500 AUD per person (domestic) or $2,000 AUD per person (international). To be covered, your original travel itinerary must be within 48 hours of your Airbnb reservation.
Luggage delay:Get reimbursed up to $500 AUD per person for clothing, toiletries, and certain other expenses if the arrival of your checked-in baggage is delayed for 24 hours or more. You may be eligible for another $500 AUD per person if your luggage is delayed for a further 24 hours.
Lost, stolen, or damaged personal possessions:Get reimbursed up to $5,000 per person for loss, theft or damage of baggage and other personal items, and personal electronic devices (up to $4,000).
Passport and travel documents:Get reimbursed up to $500 AUD per person for expenses you incur if your passport is lost or stolen outside your home country during your trip.
Permanent disability:Get $25,000 AUD per person (domestic) or $100,000 AUD per person (international) if you or a guest in your group suffers an injury that causes a permanent disability. The disability must be diagnosed within 12 months of being injured.
Accidental death:Get up to $25,000 AUD per adult (domestic) or up to $100,000 AUD per adult (international) if you or a guest in your group dies due to injury or disappears while traveling on public transportation. This benefit has a $250,000 AUD (domestic) and $1,000,000 AUD (international) policy limit.
What’s not covered
Any event that occurs more than 48 hours before or after your Airbnb stay, except for events leading to a cancellation claim.
Travel cancellation or delay where your original travel itinerary was more than 48 hours from your Airbnb stay.
Pre-existing medical conditions, change of mind, traveling against medical advice, participation in excluded sports and activities.
For full details about what is and isn’t covered, review theProduct Disclosure Statement (PDS).
Coverage for COVID-19
Although pandemics aren’t covered by travel insurance, COVID-19 may be covered in some situations because it’s an illness. However, your policy does not cover trip cancellation, curtailment or change due to border closures caused by COVID-19, or mandatory quarantine.
Refer to theProduct Disclosure Statementfor more details.
Who's covered
Stay Protection covers you and anyone you are traveling with who meets the eligibility criteria and will stay, or who intends to stay, at least one night at the Airbnb reservation. These people don’t have to be added to the Airbnb reservation or named on the policy in order to be covered, and there are no age restrictions. For full details, review theProduct Disclosure Statement (PDS).
Canceling your plan
Australian residents can cancel their Stay Protection within 14 days from the policy issue date for a full premium refund. Premium is not refundable after this 14 day cancellation period, unless the Airbnb Accommodation is canceled by the host or by Airbnb. Please note that you can’t cancel your stay protection after you’ve departed on your trip or started a claim, even if you’re within your cancellation period.
To cancel your plan, just go to your plan details page and selectCancel plan.
Making a claim
Claims are handled by Chubb from start to finish. You start a claim by going to your plan details page and selectingStart a claim, or by contacting Chubb directly.
How to get help
If you have questions about your stay protection or need help with a claim, contact Chubb by calling1-800-425-586or send an email to:airbnb.au@chubb.com.
For overseas medical and travel emergencies, call+61-2-8907-5666.
Chubb Insurance Australia Limited ABN 23 001 642 020 AFSL No. 239687 (Chubb) is the issuer of this product and Airbnb Australia Insurance Services Pty Ltd ABN 66 681 023 389 (Airbnb) distributes this product. Airbnb is an Authorised Representative of Chubb (AR number: 001311886). Chubb and Airbnb do not consider your objectives, financial situation or needs; any advice provided is general only. To decide if the product is right for you or for information on how to contact Chubb and Chubb’s dispute resolution process, read theCombined Product Disclosure Statement (PDS) and Financial Services Guide (FSG)andTarget Market Determination (TMD). Please also see theChubb Privacy Policy. Cover is subject to terms, conditions, exclusions and limits apply.

title: Find your reservation status as a guest
content:
Find your reservation status as a guest
As a guest, it’s always good to know the status of your reservation. Your status lets you know if your reservation has been confirmed, or if anything else is needed, like verifying your ID or leaving a review.
If you’re unsure about your status, you can always find outwhat other statuses mean.
Check reservation status on desktop
ClickMessages
Find the reservation message from your host
The reservation status is shown within the message thread
A confirmed status means you’re good to go! You’ll receive an email saying so, along with a text (SMS) and a push notificationif you have those set up. Learn where tofind your confirmation code.
If you sent the host of a place to stay areservation request, they have 24 hours to respond. If they decline or don’t respond, no worries: You won’t be charged and are free to book a different stay. Either way, be sure your account is up to date with validpayment informationandyour verified ID. (Some hosts require it.) For Instant Book listings, your reservation is automatically confirmed.
Not sure about your status? Find outwhat other statuses mean.

title: Understanding your reservation status
content:
Understanding your reservation status
Your reservation status lets you know how things are going as a host or guest.
Arriving today
The guest will arrive within 24 hours.
Arriving tomorrow
The guest will arrive soon, but not within 24 hours.
Arrives in __ days
The guest will be checking within said number of days. Now’s a good time toprint reservation detailsandcoordinate check-inif you haven’t already.
Awaiting guest ID
The host is requiring the guest toverify their identitybefore accepting the trip request. They have 12 hours to do so; otherwise, the request will expire.
Awaiting guest review
The guest has checked out and has 14 days towrite a reviewof their stay.
Awaiting payment
The trip request was accepted, but the guest's payment hasn’t gone through. The reservation can’t be confirmed until payment is complete, so they have 24 hours to update their payment information. Otherwise, the reservation will be canceled, and the guest won't be charged.
Learn more aboutreservations that are awaiting payment.
Canceled
The reservation was cancelled, probably because:
The guest didn’tverify their identitywithin the 12-hour time period
Their payment didn’t go through and they didn't update their payment info within 24 hours
Canceled by you/guest/host/Airbnb
Either thehost, theguest, or Airbnb canceled a confirmed reservation. Sometimes, Airbnb may cancel on behalf of a host or guest.
Checking out today
The guest will check out within 24 hours.
Change pending
Either the host or guest has initiated a trip change.
Confirmed
The trip request was accepted, either by the host or automatically viaInstant Book. Airbnb has collected payment.
Currently hosting
The guest is on their trip at this very moment. Nice!
Inquiry
The guest has asked a question about specific dates, but hasn't yet sent a trip request. In order to maintain their response rate, hosts are encouraged to reply within 24 hours and invite guests to book, decline, or send the guest a special offer before the inquiry expires.
If the status isInquiry—expires soon, hosts can check theirTodaydashboard to find out how many hours are left to respond. Learn more aboutcontacting hosts.
Invite expired
The guest did not accept their pre-approvedinvitation to bookwithin 24 hours of receiving it from the host. They can still book the place, but they’ll need to send a new trip request.
Invite sent
The host hasinvited the guest to bookthe dates shown with automatic confirmation. The guest has 24 hours toacceptby choosingBook Now.
Not possible
The requested dates are no longer available. This could happenfor several reasons, including reservation overlap or a recent update to the host’s calendar.
Offer expired
The guest received a special offer from the host but didn’t accept it within the 24-hour window.
Past guest
The guest as completed their trip.
Request declined
The hostdeclinedthe guest’s trip request, so they won’t be charged.
Request expired
Either the host or the guest took longer than 24 hours to accept or decline the request. If the guest is still interested, they’ll need to send a new request. Learn more aboutdeclined or expired requests.
Request withdrawn
The guest sent a trip request but then decided to cancel it.
Review guest
The trip is over, and the host has 14 days to leave a review for the guest.
Special offer sent
The host has invited the guest to book the dates shown at a different price from what’s listed. Hosts often do this to offer a discount or add an additional amount, such as a pet fee, to the reservation. The guest has 24 hours toacceptby choosingBook Now—at which point, the reservation is automatically accepted.
Trip changed by Airbnb
Our customer support team has changed the trip details on behalf of the host or guest.
Trip change declined
The host has declined the guest’s request to change details about their trip.
Trip change requested
The guest has requested to change details about their trip. If the host doesn't accept, they’ll have to go with the original plan or cancel.
Trip change sent
The host has requested to change details about their trip. If the guest doesn’t accept, they’ll have to go with the original plan or cancel.
Trip declined
Guest asked a trip question and the host declined it, which means the guest is not able to send a trip request. This may be due to a change in their availability.
Note: Times are based on the listing’s local time zone.

title: How long a host has to respond to your trip request
content:
How long a host has to respond to your trip request
You’ve found a great place to stay and sent your trip request—how exciting! Now you're wondering when you’ll find out if your trip is confirmed.
Hosts have 24 hours to accept or decline yourrequest, but the vast majority respond within 12 hours. Chances are, it’ll be even quicker: More than half of all trip requests are accepted within one hour of the host reading it.
You can checkthe status of your trip requestany time you like, but you’ll also receive email updates when things change.
Most hosts respond within a few hours, but time zones and lack of internet access might slow things down. In the meantime, you can try reaching out to other hosts in the area. Just be careful not to send more than one trip request for the same dates to ensure you don’t end up double booked.
Don’t want to wait for a host response? TryInstant Booklistings—they allow you to book immediately without needing to send a request to the host for approval, provided you’ve completed your account setup.
If the host declines your request or lets it expire, you won’t be charged and you’re free to book a different stay. If you’re still interested in booking the host’s place, you can learn more aboutwhat to do if your trip request is declined or expires.
Check a host’s response rate and time on desktop
Click the listing you want to check
Go toMeet your host
UnderHost details, find the response rate and how long it usually takes them to respond
Note:The response rate and time are averages based on when the host has responded to  inquiries and reservation requests over a recent period of time. It can help give you an indication of how quickly a host responds, but it’s not a guarantee.

title: What happens if your home reservation request is declined or expires
content:
What happens if your home reservation request is declined or expires
If your reservation request is declined by the host or expires (hosts have 24 hours to respond), no reservation is made and you’re free to book another place to stay.
If you’re unsure of the status of your reservation request, learnhow to find your reservation status as a guest.
The host took longer than 24 hours to accept or decline the request, and it expired.
The host declined your reservation request. Find out more aboutwhy hosts may decline reservation requestsand what you can do before booking to help them get to know you.
Things come up, and the host may have missed your reservation request. If it expired without a response from the host and you're still interested in booking their place, you can simply send them another reservation request.
You can also message the host directly and let them know about your interest in booking their place. It often helps to let the host know why you like their place, who will be staying with you, and generally why you’re traveling to their area.
Often, hosts let guests know why they declined a reservation request, but they may not always provide that info.
If your reservation request was declined and the listing for the stay shows that it's still available, the host’s calendar may not be up-to-date, they may want reservations of a different length or time, or they may have another reason for declining.
Feel free to reach out to the host directly. You can send them a message expressing your continued interest and ask if there's any flexibility to accommodate your stay. This communication can open the door to potential adjustments or clarification that could make your reservation possible.
The authorization placed on your payment method will be removed. Or, if your payment method was already charged, you’ll be refunded. Find out more aboutwhat happens to your payment if a reservation request is declined or expires.
Instant Book listings allow you to book homes immediately without needing to send a request to the host for approval. You can find a stay you can book instantly by using theInstant Book search filter.

title: What is the "pending" status for a reservation?
content:
What is the "pending" status for a reservation?
Here are some reasons why your reservation might have a "Pending" status:
When you make a request to book and complete your payment, a reservation request is automatically sent to the host, who has 24 hours to accept or decline the request. The status for your reservation will be "Pending" until the host responds. During this time, you can also contact the host to approve your request.
If the host declines your request or doesn't respond within 24 hours, the booking status will be updated as "Declined" or "Expired," and your payment will berefunded to your original payment method.
Sometimes a reservation will be "Pending" if you've booked a place to stay but you haven't completed the identity verification process. In this case, you'll have 12 hours from the time you booked tocomplete identity verification, otherwise the reservation will be automatically canceled.

title: Change the dates of your home reservation
content:
Change the dates of your home reservation
If you need to change the dates of a confirmed home reservation, you can submit a trip change request to your host. Once you send the request, your host will receive a notification to approve or decline your request.
Changing your reservation dates on desktop
ClickTripsand select the trip you want to change
UnderReservation details, clickChange reservation
Change the dates of your stay and clickSave
Review your changes and then clickSend request
Depending on your host’s pricing, changing the dates of your reservation may affect the total cost of your trip. The original total and the new total will be shown before you submit your trip change request. If the cost of your trip changes, you’ll be charged or refunded, if necessary.
If the cost of your trip increases, in most cases, the additional cost will be charged to your original payment method as soon as the host accepts your trip change request. For some payment methods, guests will need to submit the additional payment within 48 hours of the host accepting the trip change request.
If the cost of the trip decreases, you’ll be refunded to the payment method you used when you booked the home reservation. Find outwhen you’ll get your refund.
If you booked using a pre-approval that your host sent, any change to the home reservation means that the price of the reservation will be recalculated as if it were a new booking.
Your host may be willing to offer you the same price as your original booking. Just message them to ask. If they agree, they can send you a trip change request with a new price.
When a trip change request is accepted, the taxes for a home reservation are always recalculated to apply the most recent and accurate tax requirements for the home's location.
If the host declines your trip change request, or doesn’t respond, your reservation will stay the same.
You can’t make changes if your checkout has already passed. But you can always book a new stay with the host.
For some hotel stays, you may need to contact the hotel directly to change your reservation.
Once you’ve submitted a trip change request, any later changes must go through the process again as a new request.
If your host hasn’t responded to the request and you no longer want to make changes, you can alwayswithdraw your trip change request.
If a reservation is for 28 nights or more, themonthly stays cancellation policy applies. When a trip change request is submitted for a monthly stay, the change may happen automatically without requiring approval if:
It’s before the trip and the reservation is still 28 nights or longer
The trip has started, but it’s 30 nights or more until the checkout date
If a monthly stay is shortened to a length of 27 nights or less (shorter stay), you’ll be responsible for the following 30 days of payment from the day of the alteration, or until the end of the reservation if less than 30 days remain.
Once a home reservation is altered from a monthly stay to 27 nights or less (shorter stay), the host’s standard cancellation policy will apply to future alterations or cancellations made to the updated reservation.

title: Checking the status of your trip change request
content:
Checking the status of your trip change request
After making a reservation change request, you can easily check the status of your trip change.
Go toTripsand select the reservation you have requested to change. The request will show as pending, accepted or declined. You can also check yourMessages.

title: Withdraw a trip change request
content:
Withdraw a trip change request
Plans change, and that's ok. If you sent a host a trip change request for a confirmed home reservation, you can withdraw the request and keep the original reservation.
Withdraw a trip change request on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to change
UnderChanges pending, clickView
ClickCancel request
To check the status of your reservation, click or tapTripsand select the home reservation you have requested to change. The request will show as pending, accepted or declined. You can also check yourMessages.

title: Change the number of guests on your home reservation
content:
Change the number of guests on your home reservation
If you need to make changes to your booking, like adding an extra guest or removing a guest that can’t make the trip, you can do so.
To invite specific guests in your travel group to join your trip, find outhow to add your guests to your reservation.
Change the number of guests on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to change
UnderReservation details, clickChange reservation
Add or remove guests
Review your changes and any price difference
ClickSend request
Depending on your host’s pricing, changing the number of guests on your home reservation may affect the total cost of your trip. If your host requires a fee for any extra guests, in most cases, the additional cost will be charged to your original payment method as soon as the host accepts your trip change request.
For some payment methods, guests will need to submit the additional payment within 48 hours of the host accepting the trip change request.
If you’re due a refund, it’ll be sent to the original payment method that you used when you booked the reservation. How long it takes for you to receive the money depends on your bank or financial institution. Find theaverage refund timelines.
After submitting a trip change request to add any guests, it's important to wait for your host's approval before having your guests show up—your host will need to first confirm that they can accommodate a different number of guests.
Once your host approves the change, you'll receive a confirmation, and the reservation details will be updated to reflect the changes.
If you submitted a trip change request and your host hasn’t responded to it, try sending them a message reminding them to review your request.
It's not possible to transfer a reservation to someone else. If you can't make a trip, you’ll need tocancel the bookingand ask the other guest to rebook.
Depending on the host's cancellation policy, there may be a cancellation cost. Also, the cost of the rebooked stay may be different from that of the original booking.
For some hotel stays, you may need to contact the hotel directly to modify your reservation.

title: Invite guests to your group reservation
content:
Invite guests to your group reservation
You’re all booked! Before you begin your trip and stay in a home, or take part in a service or experience, it’d be a good idea to give guests in your travel group access to your reservation.
When your guests join your reservation, they can access the reservation details, review group messages, share their profile, and stay in touch with you and the group on Airbnb after the trip throughConnectionsand messaging.
Inviting guests to join or joining a reservation is optional. If you prefer not to share your profile with other guests and remain connected on Airbnb afterwards, you don’t have to. You can alwaysreview and update your profileto make sure that you show up how you want to for your connections, hosts and other guests on Airbnb.
When invited guests join an experience reservation, they have a choice to share their profile with other guests outside of their travel group on the Experience just like the primary booking guest. If they choose to share their profile they will appear on the public and post-bookingWho’s goingguest list for the experience.
Once your reservation is confirmed, you can invite other guests directly from the reservation details inTripsby sending an invite link by SMS, email, WhatsApp, Messenger, WeChat, and more.
To accept the invitation, your guests will need to log in to their Airbnb account or create one, if they don’t already have one.
After joining your travel group, guests will have access to information such as:Home stays: Check-in details, including the address of the host’s place and check-in instructions.Services: Info including details about the booked service and the host’s background.Experiences: Helpful info about the experience, like the duration of the experience and what they should bring or wear (ex: comfortable shoes), and the list of other guests going on the experience (who have chosen to share their profile).
Guests on the reservation will also get access to all past and future messages with your host, and can read and send messages to the host and everyone else in the group conversation.
After joining, guests will appear as travelers on the reservation and everyone on the reservation will be able to view each other's profiles, as Connections. Guests can remove themselves from the reservation, or be removed, before a trip ends. Learn more about how tostay in touch with Connections.
Remove a connection after a trip on desktop
ClickProfile>Connections
UnderConnections, click the profile of the person you want to remove
At the bottom of the page, clickRemoveConnection
ClickRemove
Tip: On the Connections page, you can find features like reviews, stamps, and interests, to highlight where you've been. Additionally, you can adjust who can view your profile with features like removing connections after a trip and byreportingandblockingother users.
Invitations to join a reservation expire at checkout for home stays, upon completion of a service or experience, or if the reservation is canceled. You can also disable the shared link at any time from the reservation inTrips.
The number of guests can be changed for home stays and service reservations that are in progress or haven’t yet started. If you need to change your guest count, find out how to add or remove guests from yourhomeorservicereservation.
Forexperiencereservations, the number of guests can’t be changed after the reservation is confirmed. You’ll need to either cancel and rebook or have any additional guests book a separate reservation.

title: Remove guests from your home reservation
content:
Remove guests from your home reservation
If someone in your travel group can’t make the trip, you can remove them from your reservation any time before the reservation ends.
Need to invite someone new? Find outhow to invite a different guest to join your trip.
Remove a guest from your travel group on desktop
Go toTripsand select the trip you want to change
UnderReservation details, clickManage guests
Next to the guest you want to remove, clickRemove
ClickRemoveto confirm
If you remove a specific guest from your reservation or they remove themselves, they’ll lose access to the trip details, including the address of the host’s place and check-in instructions. They’ll also be removed from the group message thread with your host and can no longer access or send messages.
You can remove specific guests from your reservation any time before the trip ends—this means before check-in or while your reservation is in progress.
Guests can’t be removed or remove themselves from past reservations.
The number of guests can be changed for reservations that are in progress or haven’t yet started. If you need to change your guest count, find outhow to add or remove guests on your home reservation.

title: Remove an invited guest from your service or experience reservation
content:
Remove an invited guest from your service or experience reservation
You can remove an invited guest from your reservation if you no longer want them to have access to your service or experience reservation details. You can then invite someone else to take their place by sharing your reservation link.
Remove guests from your reservation on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation
UnderReservation details, clickManage guests
Find the guest you want to remove and clickRemove
ClickConfirm
To change the total number of guests (not just who’s joining),cancel your reservationand rebook with the right number. Refunds are subject to the host’s cancellation policy.
If you want to bring fewer people to a service than the number of guests you’ve booked, you’ll need tocancel your reservationand rebook with the correct guest count.
If the reservation is for a group, there’s a minimum price. If you’re willing to continue the reservation at that price, you can message your service host to inform them you will have fewer guests coming. The host won’t be able to adjust the minimum price, however, unless youcancel your reservationand rebook.
Refunds are subject to your host’s cancellation policy.

title: Extend your stay
content:
Extend your stay
Changing your reservation dates on desktop
ClickTripsand select the trip you want to change
UnderReservation details, clickChange reservation
Change the dates of your stay and clickSave
Review your changes and then clickSend request
If your Host accepts your change request, your reservation will be updated and you’ll be charged for the extra time. The original total and the new total will be shown before you submit your trip change request.
In most cases, the additional cost will be charged to your original payment method as soon as the Host accepts your trip change request.
For some payment methods, guests will need to submit the additional payment within 48 hours or by their original checkout time, whichever is sooner.
If you submitted a trip change request and your Host hasn’t responded to it, try sending them a message reminding them to review your request.
If your checkout has passed, it’s too late to make a change request. What you can do, though, is send a new trip request to your Host.

title: Changing a reservation paid for with Klarna
content:
Changing a reservation paid for with Klarna
With aKlarna payment plan, you can still make reservation changes, like changing the arrival and departure dates, or adding extra guests. However, you’ll need tosubmit a trip change requestto your host on Airbnb—Klarna can’t change reservations in any way.
Need to cancel? Find outhow canceling a reservation paid for using Klarna works.
To change a confirmed reservation, the first step is tosubmit a change requestto your host on Airbnb. If there's a difference in cost for the reservation, you can compare the original total and the new total before you submit your request.
If the host accepts the request, the change is processed.
Note: Klarna can’t change reservations in any way, and hosts don’t have any information on any Klarna payment plans that you have chosen to cover the cost of the trip.
Even with a Klarna payment plan, you can change your booking (for example, you can change your reservation dates or add extra guests). You can make these changes directly through Airbnb.
If the new reservation costs less after a change request, Airbnb will send the refund to Klarna for the difference. This refund may take up to 14 business days to reflect in your Klarna account.
If the new reservation change costs more than the original cost, you can use an existing payment method in your Airbnb account oradd a new payment methodto cover the difference. Additionally, you may be able to apply for a new payment plan with Klarna for an additional amount.
To check your refund status on desktop
ClickTrips
UnderCanceled trips, click the trip you want to check
ClickTrack refund
UnderRefund status, review your refund amount
Airbnb will send the refund to Klarna for the difference. This refund may take between 5-7 business days to reflect in your Klarna account.
However, once we send the refund to Klarna, we can’t provide additional information on the Klarna refund status. For any details of refunds issued by Klarna, contact theircustomer service.
If you’ve completed all scheduled installments with Klarna, then Klarna would issue the refund to your original method of payment used with Klarna. If your payment plan is still active, then Klarna would issue refunds as a balance to adjust to the remainder of their scheduled installments.
In the case of a significant delay in receiving the amount due from Klarna, you'll need to check the status with Klarna directly. If after 14 days you have not received a refund, contactKlarna's customer service.
The refund amount refers to the full or partial cost of your reservation and doesn't include any fees or interest included in a Klarna payment plan.
Paying in 3 or 4 payments with Klarna loans are interest-free, but fees may be applied by Klarna for late payments. If you pay monthly with Klarna, interest may be applied.
Once a booking is confirmed and you have set up a Klarna payment plan, if you are unable to make a payment to Klarna on time, Klarna may try again. If that payment attempt doesn’t work, the missed payment will be added to the amount of the next scheduled payment, and late fees may apply.
Learn more aboutwhat happens if you can’t pay on time using Klarna.
Note: If you are late with payment to Klarna, this information won’t be shared with Airbnb and has no effect on your Airbnb profile or personal information. All financial agreements with Klarna are strictly between Klarna and you.

title: What to do if you accidentally made a booking or booked the wrong dates
content:
What to do if you accidentally made a booking or booked the wrong dates
If you made a mistake when you booked a home, service, or experience—maybe you hit reserve before you checked the details, or you got the right place, but the wrong dates—there are steps you can take to address it.
Your host’s cancellation policy determines if you’re eligible for a refund if you need to cancel. Some bookings allow free cancellations if you act within a certain time frame, so your first step is to check the cancellation policy for your home, service, or experience reservation.
You can find info about your reservation in themessage threadwith your host, or by checking yourTrips. The cancellation policy is located underReservation details.
If you’re within the free cancellation window, no problem. You can go ahead and cancel your reservation and make other plans.
If you want to keep your home reservation but need different dates, you can send atrip change requestto your host. The host decides whether to approve the change. If the dates you need aren’t available, try messaging your host—they may be able to adjust their calendar or suggest alternatives.
If you booked a service or experience, but you made a mistake with the date or time, you may be able tochange your reservation, depending on your host’s availability and theircancellation policy.
In situations where you’re not eligible to cancel for a full refund, communicating directly with the host can often help. Explain the situation and ask if they can offer flexibility. Many hosts are understanding and may help with a refund or date change, especially if you reach out quickly.
Keep in mind: A refund outside of the cancellation policy for the reservation is always at the discretion of the host.

title: Remove yourself as a guest on a home reservation
content:
Remove yourself as a guest on a home reservation
You accepted an invitation to join a trip, and now your plans have changed. You can remove yourself as a guest on the reservation.
Remove yourself from a reservation on desktop
ClickTripsand select the trip you want to remove yourself from
UnderReservation details, clickManage guests
Next toYou, clickLeave
ClickYes, leave
If you remove yourself as a guest on a reservation, you’ll lose access to the trip details, including the address of the host’s place and check-in instructions. You’ll also be removed from the group message thread with the host and other guests, and can no longer access or send messages.
You can remove yourself as a guest on a reservation any time before the trip ends—this means before check-in or while the reservation is in progress.
Guests can’t remove themselves or be removed from past home reservations.

title: Change the date or time of your service or experience reservation
content:
Change the date or time of your service or experience reservation
If you booked a service or experience, but the date or time no longer works for you, don’t worry, you don’t have to miss out! You may be able to change your reservation, depending on your host’s availability and theircancellation policy.
If you’re within the free cancellation period of your service or experience reservation, you can cancel and instantly rebook for any other available time and date on the host’s calendar.
The price might vary depending on the new date you select, but you’ll be able to review any changes in the total cost before you finalize the change.
If you’re outside your host’s cancellation window, you can send your host a request to change your reservation. They’ll review it and either accept or decline the request.
If they accept it, then you’ll have a new reservation and you’re good to go. If your host rejects it, and you can’t make the original date and time, you won’t receive a refund—unless one of the circumstances outlined in ourguest cancellation policy for services and experiencesapplies to your reservation, or if the host, at their discretion, agrees to give you a full refund.
If the options don’t work for you, you canrequest to book a date or time that’s not on your host’s calendaror you cancancel your reservation.

title: Add a service to your home reservation
content:
Add a service to your home reservation
Want an in-home gourmet dinner prepared by a private chef? Or maybe you and your guests want to work out with a personal trainer during your stay. Reserving a service can make your trip even more special.
Airbnb Services make your stay more special by connecting you with providers who have beenvetted for quality. You can choose the perfect service from a variety of categories including:
Chefs
Catering
Prepared meals
Photography
Massage
Spa treatments
Hair styling
Personal training
Nails
Makeup
Service hosts either charge a fixed total cost for an offering or charge per-guest. Some have a minimum price per booking, which you’ll be able to review when booking.
Find and book services through theServicessection on the Airbnb website or app:
Select your city and travel dates
Browse by service type
Detailed listing pages highlight each host’s qualifications, offerings, and pricing
Choose a location, date, time, and number of guests (up to the service hosts’ limit)
Book the service that fits your needs
Once you book, your service provider will either come to you or you’ll meet them at their location.
Keep in mind, availability is set by the service host.
If you want to book a service at your Airbnb, review your host’s house rules first. Some hosts allow all services, while others may limit specific types or not permit them at all. You can view this on their home listing or contact your home host if you have questions.
You can book as many services or experiences as you’d like–just be sure to check schedules to avoid overlaps. Any home, service, or experience you book will show up in yourTrips, and you can evenexport them to your calendar.

title: Changes guests can make to their service reservation
content:
Changes guests can make to their service reservation
Guests may want to make changes to their reservation for a service, and hosts can decide if and how they accommodate these requests.
Change the date or time of the service by requesting toreschedule a service.
Customize the service by messaging the host to request additional offerings (ex: An additional course for a private dinner). If the changes can be accommodated, the host can request payment from the guest directly via messages. The guest can accept the payment request to confirm the change.
If a booker wants to add more guests to a service they’ve booked, they can message their service host to ask if it’s possible.
The host will decide if they’re able to accommodate additional guests and if there’s a price change. Once an agreement is reached, payment can be made in the message thread and the reservation will be updated.
If a booker wants to bring fewer people to a service than the number of guests they’ve booked, they’ll need to cancel the reservationand rebook with the correct guest count.If the reservation is for a group, there’s a minimum price. If the booker is willing to continue the reservation at that price, they can message their service host to let them know they'll have fewer guests coming. The host won’t be able to adjust the minimum price, however, unless thereservation is cancelledand then rebooked.
Refunds are subject to your host’s cancellation policy.

title: Invite guests to your service reservation
content:
Invite guests to your service reservation
Booked an in-home private dinner with your own personal chef and want to invite your friends? You can invite guests to your reservation as soon as it’s confirmed by selectingInvite guestsand then choosing how you want to invite them.
Invite guests on desktop
ClickTripsand select the service you want invite a guest to
UnderReservation details, clickManage guests
ClickInvite guestsand select how you want to send the invite
Send invite
If you want to add more guests to a service you’ve booked, you can message your service host to ask if it’s possible.
They’ll let you know if they’re able to accommodate additional guests and if there’s a price change. Once you’ve reached an agreement, you’ll be able to pay in the message thread and your reservation will be updated.
If you want to bring fewer people to a service than the number of guests you’ve booked, unless you booked the reservation as a private group with a minimum price, you’ll need tocancel your reservationand rebook with the correct guest count.If the reservation is for a private group, there is a minimum price, and you are willing to continue the reservation at that price, you can message your service host to inform them you will have fewer guests coming. They will not be able to adjust the minimum price, however, unless youcancel your reservationand rebook.
Refunds are subject to your host’s cancellation policy.
To accept an invitation to join a service, you’ll need to create an Airbnb account or log into your Airbnb account if you already have one. You’ll also need tocomplete your profilebefore accepting the invitation. Once accepted, you’ll find the reservation details inTrips.

title: If a host offers you a different place to stay
content:
If a host offers you a different place to stay
Sometimes a host might offer you an alternative listing if the one you wanted has become unavailable—and it’s completely up to you if you want to accept or decline it.
If you don’t have aconfirmed home reservationyet and the host offers you a different listing from the one you discussed, you can book their suggested stay or simply look for a new place.
Please make sure the host’s suggested alternative would work for your trip before confirming.
If your host is asking you to stay in a different listing than you originally booked and you’re okay with a switch, your host canchange the home reservation.
If you don’t want to change your reservation, or if your host is asking you to switch without officially changing the reservation on Airbnb,ask the host to cancel your home reservationso you can get a full refund or find another place to stay.
If you’ve already checked in and you believe that the host’s place is not the listing you booked on Airbnb,document the issue and message your hostwithin 72 hours of discovering the issue. Your host may be able to fix it right away.
If the listing is significantly different than advertised and your host can’t resolve the issue, we’ll help you find a similar place, depending on availability at comparable pricing. If a similar place isn’t available or you’d prefer not to rebook, we’ll give you a full or partial refund. Read more about ourRebooking and Refund Policy.

title: Declining a change request
content:
Declining a change request
Wondering if you’re allowed to say no to proposed changes for a confirmed reservation? The answer is yes! Both guests and hosts can decline service, experience, or trip change requests. If you decline, the proposed changes don’t take effect, and the existing reservation details remain as is.
There are no consequences for declining, but if you’re a host, there are a few issues to considerwhen responding to a trip change requestfrom a guest.

title: How to change a home reservation during a trip
content:
How to change a home reservation during a trip
If your trip has already started and you want to change the length of your home reservation, you can submit a change request to your host. However, there are a few things to bear in mind before you do:
You can change the number of guests after check-in, if agreed upon with the host
Any additional payments can be made through theResolution Center
The cancellation policy will remain the same as the originally booked policy for short-term reservations
If you've booked additional services or experiences for your stay, make sure their scheduling aligns with the revised length of your home reservation
Submit a change request on desktop
ClickTripsand select the trip you want to change
ClickChange reservation
Change the dates of your stay or the number of guests and clickSave
Review your changes, then clickSend request
Depending on the host’s policy, changes to the number of guests may affect the total cost of your stay. This will be automatically reflected in your bill once your host confirms the changes before you finalize.
If the request is declined, or you don’t receive a response from your host, your reservation will stay the same.

title: Monthly stay cancellations by guest
content:
Monthly stay cancellations by guest
For home reservations 28 nights or longer, a long-term cancellation policy applies.
Your host may have a firm or strict long-term cancellation policy. To check it, go toTripsand find it underReservation details.
You cancancel your reservationanytime before or during your trip. However, depending on your host’s policy guidelines, keep in mind:
Your first payment might be nonrefundable, depending on the home listing and how far in advance the reservation is made
If you cancel after the trip has started, the following month of the reservation is nonrefundable
Your host can choose to offer an additional refund
If you don’t need to cancel your monthly stay and only want tochange the dates of your confirmed home reservation, you can submit a trip change request to your host.

title: Cancel your home reservation as a guest
content:
Cancel your home reservation as a guest
Your plans have changed and now you need to cancel your home reservation. No problem.
Cancel a reservation on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to cancel
UnderReservation details, clickCancel reservation
Choose a reason for canceling
ClickCancel reservation
For home reservations, cancellation policies are set by the host and vary by listing. If a host has selected a policy with a fully refundable option, you can cancel the reservation for free—just make sure you cancel before the time and date listed. Cancellation deadlines for receiving refunds are measured from the check-in time for the listing in its local time zone, or 3:00 PM if no check-in time is specified.
Keep in mind that some listings may be non-refundable, or only partially refundable, after certain dates and times. In these cases, you won’t be able to cancel your reservation for free. In the rare circumstances that a large-scale event at your destination prevents completion of your reservation, you may be eligible for a refund under Airbnb’sMajor Disruptive Events Policy.
If you have other services booked at the home you’re cancelling, you'll need to cancel them separately or make other arrangements with your service host.
Learn more aboutwhat's refunded when you cancel a home stay reservation. Remember that the amount refunded will never be more than the amount you have actually paid at the time you cancel. You canfind your refund amount before or after canceling your reservation.
If you’re experiencing an issue during your stay, you can ask your host to fix it, request a partial refund, or request to cancel your reservation for a full refund.
Before canceling, find outwhat your options are if there’s an issue during your stay
Cancelling a service or experience? You can usually cancel for free up to 1 day (24 hours) before the start time of the service or experience, but certain services and experiences allow you to cancel for a full refund up to 3 days (72 hours) before the start time. Find out how tocancel your service or experience reservation as a guest.

title: Canceling a reservation paid for using Klarna
content:
Canceling a reservation paid for using Klarna
If you have a Klarna payment plan andcancel a reservation for a stay, your refund is subject to thecancellation policyfor the reservation. Additionally, your Klarna payment plan will be canceled and if applicable, a refund will be issued to Klarna and then back to you based on the balance of your payment plan.
If you are eligible for a refund, it may take between 5–7 business days.
To check your refund status on desktop
ClickTrips
UnderCanceled trips, click the trip you want to check
ClickTrack refund
UnderRefund status, review your refund amount
Airbnb will send the refund to Klarna for the difference. This refund may take between 5-7 business days to reflect in your Klarna account.
However, once we send the refund to Klarna, we can’t provide additional information on the Klarna refund status. For any details of refunds issued by Klarna, contact theircustomer service.
If you're unable to cancel a trip within the cancellation policy refund time frame, you may still owe an additional amount, based on the cost of the reservation and the cancellation policy for the reservation. This means that you may still need to make additional payments to Klarna, depending on the current balance of your payment plan.
Every booking made on Airbnb comes withAirCoverprotection for guests against significant issues, including host cancellations within 30 days of check-in.
In the unlikely event that a host needs to cancel your booking, we automatically issue a refund to Klarna for the amount paid. Klarna will then issue you a refund based on the balance of your payment plan.

title: Cancel or withdraw a trip request
content:
Cancel or withdraw a trip request
Need to add another guest or change the dates of your reservation before it’s confirmed? Here are a few things to know.
First, check thestatus of your request. If the host has already accepted it, then it’s subject to theircancellation policy. If it’s still pending, you can cancel, and you won’t be charged for the reservation or the service fees.
The simplest way is to find themessage threadwith your host and selectWithdraw request.
Cancel a reservation request on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to change
For a reservation that saysPending, clickWithdraw request
ClickCancel request

title: Canceling a trip due to a Major Disruptive Event
content:
Canceling a trip due to a Major Disruptive Event
If you need to cancel because a  large-scale event at your destination prevents completion of your reservation, we can help:
Read ourMajor Disruptive Events Policyto understand what's covered.
Check the reservation details page to see if your home, service, or experience is eligible.  In some cases, you may need tocontact usto determine eligibility.

title: What to do if you accidentally made a booking or booked the wrong dates
content:
What to do if you accidentally made a booking or booked the wrong dates
If you made a mistake when you booked a home, service, or experience—maybe you hit reserve before you checked the details, or you got the right place, but the wrong dates—there are steps you can take to address it.
Your host’s cancellation policy determines if you’re eligible for a refund if you need to cancel. Some bookings allow free cancellations if you act within a certain time frame, so your first step is to check the cancellation policy for your home, service, or experience reservation.
You can find info about your reservation in themessage threadwith your host, or by checking yourTrips. The cancellation policy is located underReservation details.
If you’re within the free cancellation window, no problem. You can go ahead and cancel your reservation and make other plans.
If you want to keep your home reservation but need different dates, you can send atrip change requestto your host. The host decides whether to approve the change. If the dates you need aren’t available, try messaging your host—they may be able to adjust their calendar or suggest alternatives.
If you booked a service or experience, but you made a mistake with the date or time, you may be able tochange your reservation, depending on your host’s availability and theircancellation policy.
In situations where you’re not eligible to cancel for a full refund, communicating directly with the host can often help. Explain the situation and ask if they can offer flexibility. Many hosts are understanding and may help with a refund or date change, especially if you reach out quickly.
Keep in mind: A refund outside of the cancellation policy for the reservation is always at the discretion of the host.

title: Rebook a canceled home reservation
content:
Rebook a canceled home reservation
Change of plans? Not a problem! While canceled home reservations can’t be restored, you can contact the host and book a new reservation.
We recommend that you message the host to let them know your plan for rebooking.
You canbook a home reservationfor the host’s place again as you did before. Or, the host can send you aspecial offerwith the booking details that you’ve agreed on. You’ll have 24 hours to accept the special offer, and once you do, your reservation will be automatically confirmed.
If you didn't get a full refund for the original canceled home reservation, you cancontact usto request a full refund after you rebook.
Message the host to book again on desktop
ClickTrips
UnderCanceled trips, click the canceled reservation you want to book again
ClickMessage host

title: Cancel your service or experience reservation as a guest
content:
Cancel your service or experience reservation as a guest
Have your plans changed? You may be able to reschedule for another date or time, or you can cancel your reservation.
Cancellation policies vary by service or experience listing. Most services and experiences on Airbnb have a 1-day cancellation policy, allowing you to cancel until 24 hours before the service or experience start time (local time zone) for a full refund. Certain services and experiences may have a 3-day (72-hour) cancellation policy.
If you cancel outside of the stated free cancellation period, you won’t receive a refund, unless one of the circumstances outlined in ourguest cancellation policy for services and experiencesapplies to your reservation, or if the host, at their discretion, agrees to give you a full refund.
Once you start the cancellation process, you’ll be able to review any refund amount before you finalize the cancellation.
Cancel a reservation on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to cancel
UnderReservation details, clickCancel reservation
Choose a reason for canceling and clickContinue
ClickCancel reservation
You’ll get a message to confirm the cancellation. If you’re eligible for a refund, you’ll get it within 10 business days. Find out more aboutwhen you’ll get your refund.
Learn more about how tocancel your home reservation.

title: If your host cancels your home reservation
content:
If your host cancels your home reservation
While it’s rare, sometimes a host may need to cancel a reservation. We understand this can impact your plans in a big way. Rest assured, if your host cancels your reservation for their home before check-in, you’ll receive a full refund.
If your host cancels your reservation before check-in, you’ll receive a full refund, including service fees, if you choose not to rebook.
Based on eligibility, you may receive Airbnb booking credit that can be used to make another reservation right away. If you don’t use it within 72 hours, you will automatically receive a full refund instead. If you are ineligible to receive booking credit, your refund will be issued immediately. Find out more abouthow booking credit works.
Your refund will be sent to the payment method you used when you booked. How long it takes for you to receive the money depends on your bank or financial institution. Find the commonrefund timelines.
Every booking made on Airbnb comes withAirCover for guests; support for guests who experience issues with their booking. If your host cancels, we’ll help you rebook a similar place to stay, considering location and amenities, based on availability at comparable pricing.
When a cancellation occurs, you’ll get an email with full details, including your refund information.
If your host lets you know that they can’t accommodate your stay anymore, don’t cancel for them. Instead, send them a message asking them to cancel. That way, you’ll be eligible to receive a full refund.

title: If your host asks you to cancel
content:
If your host asks you to cancel
Things happen, and a host may need to cancel a reservation, whether it’s for a home, service, or experience. But a guest shouldn’t cancel on behalf of their host.
If your host lets you know that they can't accommodate your reservation anymore, don't cancel for them. Instead, send them a message asking them to cancel. That way, you'll be eligible to receive a full refund.
If your host cancels your reservation for a home, service, or experience before check-in or the start time, you’ll receive a full refund.
For homes, if check-in is within 30 days, we’ll help you rebook a similar place to stay, depending on availability at comparable pricing. Find out more aboutwhat happens when your host cancels your home reservation.
If you have a service booked at a home and the home host cancels your reservation, you'll need to contact your service host and make other arrangements, or cancel your service.

title: If your host cancels your service or experience
content:
If your host cancels your service or experience
While it’s rare, sometimes a host may need to cancel your service or experience reservation. We understand this can impact your plans in a big way.  If this happens, you’ll get a full refund and you can rebook the service or experience if you want. Sometimes a host may need to cancel an ongoing service or experience because an issue prevents the activity from continuing; if this happens you may be entitled to a refund under theGuest Refund Policy for services and experiences.
When a cancellation occurs, you’ll get an email with details, including your refund information.

title: Find the cancellation policy for any home, service, or experience
content:
Find the cancellation policy for any home, service, or experience
Maybe you just want to know what your options are, or perhaps you need to cancel right now. Here’s where to find the cancellation policy for any home, service, or experience.
You can find cancellation details on the listing, and during the booking process—before you pay.
Find the cancellation policy on desktop
Click the listing you want to check
Go toThings to know
UnderCancellation policy, find if there’s a free cancellation period
ClickShow moreto get more details about what’s refunded if you cancel
Find the cancellation policy for a reservation on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to check
UnderReservation details,go toCancellation policy
ClickRead more
If you’ve checked out the host’s cancellation policy and you're ready to cancel, find out how tocancel your home reservationorcancel your service or experience reservation.

title: Understanding home cancellation policies as a guest
content:
Understanding home cancellation policies as a guest
For home reservations, cancellation policies are set by the host and vary by listing.
If your reservation has a fully refundable policy, you can cancel for free before the specified date and time. Keep in mind that some listings might have non-refundable or only partially refundable policies after certain dates, so free cancellation wouldn't be an option.
Find out more abouthow to cancel your home reservation as a guest.
You can find the applicable cancellation policydisplayed on the listing page, when you confirm and pay, and in your confirmation email, so you'll know exactly what to expect if your plans change.
Hosts can adjust cancellation policies for specific dates.
The cancellation policy for your stay is set by the check-in date, not by how many nights you book
If you request to alter your check-in date, the cancellation policy may change

title: What guests need to know about a service or experience cancellation policy
content:
What guests need to know about a service or experience cancellation policy
We know clear cancellation policies are important to provide flexibility and peace of mind when booking a service or experience. Here’s how to find the cancellation policy for your service or experience reservation, whether you simply want to understand more or if you need to cancel.
Before you book: You can find cancellation details on the service or experience listing page underThings to know, and during the booking process—before you pay.
For a confirmed reservation: You can find info about a reservation you booked in the message thread with your host, or by checking yourTrips. The cancellation policy is located underReservation details.
Cancellation policies vary by service or experience listing. Most services and experiences on Airbnb will have a 1-day cancellation policy, allowing you to cancel until one day (24 hours) before the service or experience start time for a full refund. Select services and experiences will have a 3-day cancellation policy, allowing you to cancel until three days (72 hours) before the service or experience start time for a full refund.
If you cancel outside of the stated free cancellation period, you will not receive a refund, unless one of the circumstances below applies to your reservation, or if the host, at their discretion, agrees to provide you a full refund.
The times and dates we display for cancellation policies are based on the local time zone of the service or experience listing.
Major Disruptive Events: In the rare circumstance that a large-scale event at the location of your service or experience prevents completion of the reservation, you may be able to cancel for a refund underAirbnb's Major Disruptive Events Policy.
Issues while on your service experience: If you cancel because your service or experience is materially different than what was described at booking, you may be entitled to a refund under ourrefund policy for services and experienceseven if you’re outside the free cancellation period.
Issue at an Airbnb home: If an issue at an Airbnb home prevents the service or experience from taking place at the home, you may be entitled to a refund under ourrefund policy for services and experienceseven if you’re outside the free cancellation period.
Please note that poor weather is not considered an issue that would entitle a guest to a refund outside of the free cancellation period, unless the weather prevents the activity from taking place.
Our final decisions relating to cancellations and refunds do not affect other contractual or statutory rights that may be available to you. Any right that guests or hosts may have to initiate legal action remains unaffected.
If you're a host or want to learn more about what cancellation policies are available, please refer tocancellation policiesfor your listing

title: Major Disruptive Events Policy
content:
Major Disruptive Events Policy
In general, cancellations and refunds for Airbnb reservations are governed by the listing’scancellation policy. In the rare circumstances that large-scale events prevent or legally prohibit completion of a reservation, the Major Disruptive Events Policy (the “Policy”) may apply. When this Policy applies, guests can cancel their reservation and receive a refund, travel credit, and/or other consideration regardless of the reservation’scancellation policy, and hosts can cancelwithout fees or other adverse consequences, although their listing’s calendar will be blocked for the dates of the canceled reservation.
This Policy applies to reservations for homes, services, and experiences and is applicable to reservations that are ongoing or that have a check-in on or after the effective date, except as otherwise notified by Airbnb to users. The Major Disruptive Events Policy is not an insurance policy.
The following events are covered under this Policy if they impact your reservation location, occur after the time of booking, and prevent or legally prohibit completion of a future or ongoing reservation (referred to in this Policy as “Events”):
Declared public health emergencies and epidemics.Government-declared epidemics, pandemics, and public health emergencies.
