title: Contacting hosts
content:
Contacting hosts
Getting in touch with a host on Airbnb is easy—just send them aquick messageto let them know you’re interested in their home, service, or experience.
For your safety and privacy, guests and hosts only receive each other's phone numbers after a reservation is confirmed. Learn more about why it’s good to keepcommunication and transactionson Airbnb.
If you have questions about a listing, click or tap Contact host to send them a message. Pro tip: If you’re asking about specific reservation details, enter your travel dates and number of guests first—this is required before you can send a message.
For homes and services
Once your home or service booking is accepted, you canMessageyour host (and co-host, if applicable), and all guests in a group message. Alternatively, you can reach your host by going toTrips, finding the reservation, and contacting them on the same group message from there.
If you’ve reserved a home or service and you haveco-travelers, you can invite them to join the reservation.
For experiences
Once your experience booking is accepted, you canMessageyour host, co-host, and all other guests on the experience—including others outside your booking group—in a group message.
If you want to message the host and co-hosts privately, you can do that from your group message thread by selectingDetails, and thenMessage hosts. This message will be private between you, the host, and any co-hosts.
If you’ve reserved an experience, you caninvite your friendsto join you.
You can send follow-up messages to your group or a private message to the  host after a trip, service, or experience has ended.
If you have any payment issues that you need to resolve, you can do so in theResolution Center.

title: What to do if a host isn’t responding
content:
What to do if a host isn’t responding
Hosts have 24 hours to respond to guests. Most hosts respond within a few hours, but time zones and lack of internet access might slow things down.
Unless you’ve chosen a home withInstant Book, hosts have 24 hours to accept or decline a request to book. Find more abouthost response time.
When you have a confirmed reservation, your host’s phone number will show in the reservation details for your trip so you can open the Airbnb app and call them. The host's phone number won't show up after your stay (or if the home reservation is canceled), but you can still send them amessage. Find out more aboutcontacting hosts.
If you’re wondering aboutcheck-in details and instructions, you’ll find them 48 hours before the check-in date on your reservation’s itinerary page.
If all communications fail, and you can’t contact your host,contact us.
Service and experience hosts have 24 hours to respond to guests. If you have messaged your host and haven’t received a response. Please try again.
If all communications fail, and you can’t contact your host,contact us.

title: Getting charged for damage
content:
Getting charged for damage
Accidents are rare, but they happen. If you, someone you invite, or a pet are responsible for damage during a home stay, service, or experience, let your host know right away.
Your host can send you a reimbursement request in ourResolution Centerto cover the cost of the damage, and you’ll have 24 hours to respond.
The best way to let your host know about damage is to send them amessagethrough the Airbnb website or app. This will be helpful in case our Community Support team needs to refer to it later. If you can provide photos, that’s great, too.
Being upfront about damage gives your host as much time as possible to fix it before their next guest arrives. It also gives you and your host a chance to find a resolution without Airbnb getting involved.
If you break something, just bring it to your host’s attention and use the Resolution Center to make it right.
If your host believes that you’re responsible for damage, missing items, or unexpected cleaning costs, they may send you a reimbursement request through our Resolution Center. You’ll have 24 hours to respond.
If you pay the full amount, the request will be closed
If you pay a partial amount, decline the request, or don’t respond to the request, your host may involve Airbnb or our insurer as a next step
If your host involves Airbnb, a member of our Community Support team will determine if you’re responsible for the damage and if the amount your host is requesting is reasonable. They’ll do this by reviewing evidence provided by your host, as well as any notes you included in your initial response to your host’s request. Someone may also follow up with you or others who may have information about the host’s damage claim.
You’ll be given the opportunity to voluntarily submit a timely payment or an appeal. If you don’t submit a timely payment or appeal, or your appeal is unsuccessful, your payment method will be charged. (This doesn’t apply to stays in China, Japan, or India.) You’ll never be charged without advance notice or a chance to appeal before and after you’ve been charged. You’ll have 60 days to appeal after you’ve been charged.
Dealing with damage is never fun, but this process is designed to be as fair as possible. For more info, review ourTerms of ServiceandPayments Terms of Service.

title: How to read and send messages
content:
How to read and send messages
It all starts with a message, whether you’re a guest who has aquestion about a listingor a host who wants toshare information about your home, service, or experience. You’ll just need to be logged in to your Airbnb account first to read or send messages in the Airbnb app or website.
YourMessageswill help you keep your communication organized for all your trips. Every reservation automatically creates a group thread where all bookers, co-travelers, hosts and co-hosts can connect.
Just click on a message thread to read an existing message or send a new one. By default, read receipts are enabled for all messages on Airbnb to help keep communication between guests and hosts transparent.
If you’re a host, just go to yourMessagesand click on a message thread to read an existing message or send a new one.
If you’re hosting an experience and need to chat with a guest privately, you have that option. Just head toDetailsand click or tap the messaging icon next to their name underWho’s going.
Guests canmessage a hostbefore a reservation, after a trip is confirmed, or after the trip is completed. They can always find their message threads by going toMessages.
Need to get in contact with the host of an experience? You can contact them privately by heading toDetailsand tapping or clickingMessage host(s).
You can filter, search, andarchiveyour messages to stay on top of your conversations. Made a mistake? You can edit a message within 15 minutes after sending it, or unsend your message up to 24 hours after it’s sent.
Guests and hosts can reply directly to specific messages anywhere in the thread, so it’s easier to keep track of responses as conversations grow busier.
Guests and hosts are limited to 25 messages within any 24-hour period or 10 messages per hour. They’ll see an error message if they’ve reached their message limit.

title: Send photos and videos in a message
content:
Send photos and videos in a message
Once your reservation for a home, service or experience is confirmed, you can share photos and videos up to 60 seconds in length in messages with your host or guests.
All of the photos and videos sent between guests, hosts, and co-hosts can be viewed in theGallerysection of yourDetails.
Photos and videos can be a valuable part of the communication process. For instance, you can send attachments to:
Show guests a video of where your service or experience will take place
Share photos of a walking tour with other guests
Share a video of a catered meal service that you booked
Show a guest how the hot tub works
Share photos or video at checkout of how you left your host’s home
Photos and videos can only be sent after a reservation is confirmed.
Send photos and videos on desktop
ClickMessagesand select the message thread
ClickAdd +
ClickAdd photo or videoand select what you want to send
Review and clickSend
Having trouble sharing the photo or video you want? Make sure you have the latest Airbnb app installed.
Any messages you send, including any photos or videos you share, should comply with ourContent Policyand ourCommunity Standards. If you believe that a message you’ve received violates our policies, you can report the content directly through the app or bycontacting us.

title: Find your reservation info
content:
Find your reservation info
Looking for your reservation info? You can find details about a reservation you’ve booked by going to the go to themessagethread with your host, or find it inTrips. For info about past reservations, go toProfile.
Find reservation details on desktop
ClickTripsand select your reservation
Click the reservation you want to check
Find all your booking information
Trying to find your confirmation code? Follow the steps above and you’ll find the code underReservation details.
Find past reservations on desktop
ClickProfile>Past trips
Select the past reservation you want to check
Find all your booking information
Make sure you’re logged in to the same Airbnb account you used to book the reservation. If you booked it using a friend’s or family member’s account, log in to their account first, or ask them to invite you as an additional guest.
If you’re still having trouble finding your reservation,get help.

title: Finding the booking confirmation for an Experience
content:
Finding the booking confirmation for an Experience
When you book an Airbnb Experience, we'll send you a booking confirmation via email.
Having trouble finding it? Try checking your spam folder. You can also find your itinerary by visiting yourTripson our website or app.

title: If you can’t find the place you booked on the site
content:
If you can’t find the place you booked on the site
Hosts can choose to hide their listing at any time from search results, usually to prevent new bookings.
Hosts are encouraged toconfirm reservations quickly. But if you’ve sent a trip request and the listing is now hidden, you can contact the host to find out if there’s a problem.
If you have aconfirmed trip, it's still active, even if your host has hidden the listing. To find your itinerary, go toTrips.
You and your host can also stillmessage through Airbnbto confirm details about check-in, checkout, and any additional information.

title: Add business travel details to your reservation
content:
Add business travel details to your reservation
If you’veadded your work emailto your profile orenrolled in Airbnb for Work, you can add business travel notes to a trip. Being a “verified” business traveler means you’ve added your work email to your Airbnb account, though it’s your choice whether to add business details to a trip.
You must be a verified business traveler to add business travel details to your reservation.
Add business travel details on desktop
ClickTripsand select the trip you want to edit
Go toPayment info
SelectThis is a business trip
ClickAdd details for expensing your trip
Enter your billing address or any other relevant details, then clickSave
Adding VAT details to your notes won’t affect the VAT charged on your reservation.VAT invoicescannot be changed. Learn more abouthow VAT may apply to you.
Once a reservation is confirmed, what’s shown on the receipt won’t change, regardless of any updates that you make to your business details. You can still add business details or notes to your trip, but changes cannot be made to the receipt.
If you need to add information to your receipt, you can have it reissued bycancelingandrebookingyour trip. Important: Canceling your reservation will be subject to your Host’s cancellation policy, so make sure youcheck your Host’s cancellation policyfirst to find out if you’ll receive a refund.

title: Change the number of guests on your home reservation
content:
Change the number of guests on your home reservation
If you need to make changes to your booking, like adding an extra guest or removing a guest that can’t make the trip, you can do so.
To invite specific guests in your travel group to join your trip, find outhow to add your guests to your reservation.
Change the number of guests on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to change
UnderReservation details, clickChange reservation
Add or remove guests
Review your changes and any price difference
ClickSend request
Depending on your host’s pricing, changing the number of guests on your home reservation may affect the total cost of your trip. If your host requires a fee for any extra guests, in most cases, the additional cost will be charged to your original payment method as soon as the host accepts your trip change request.
For some payment methods, guests will need to submit the additional payment within 48 hours of the host accepting the trip change request.
If you’re due a refund, it’ll be sent to the original payment method that you used when you booked the reservation. How long it takes for you to receive the money depends on your bank or financial institution. Find theaverage refund timelines.
After submitting a trip change request to add any guests, it's important to wait for your host's approval before having your guests show up—your host will need to first confirm that they can accommodate a different number of guests.
Once your host approves the change, you'll receive a confirmation, and the reservation details will be updated to reflect the changes.
If you submitted a trip change request and your host hasn’t responded to it, try sending them a message reminding them to review your request.
It's not possible to transfer a reservation to someone else. If you can't make a trip, you’ll need tocancel the bookingand ask the other guest to rebook.
Depending on the host's cancellation policy, there may be a cancellation cost. Also, the cost of the rebooked stay may be different from that of the original booking.
For some hotel stays, you may need to contact the hotel directly to modify your reservation.

title: Download or print your itinerary for visa purposes
content:
Download or print your itinerary for visa purposes
You can download or print your travel itinerary for visa purposes, with the option to add your guests' full names or simply display the total number of guests. Plus, you have the option to select the language in which your itinerary will be displayed.
Get a PDF for visa purposes on desktop
ClickTripsand select the trip you’d like an itinerary for
UnderReservation details, clickGet a PDF for visa purposes
UnderWho’s coming?, add the names of all guests, if needed
ClickNextorSkip
Choose a language for your itinerary to be displayed in
ClickSave
Itineraries can show your guests’ full names or simply show the number of guests on the reservation—it’s up to you. It can be helpful to add the full names of guests if you need to print out your itinerary for a travel visa.
The itinerary includes:
Main listing photo
Check-in and checkout dates and times
Number of guests (including guest names if you’ve added them)
Confirmation code
House manual
House rules
Who’s hosting your stay (Host’s first name)
Amount paid
You can generate the itinerary in a language other than your preferred language. When downloading or printing your itinerary, you'll have the option to choose from a list of common languages.
If the language you need isn’t available, you can try translating the itinerary as a photo or document using Google Translate or another translation app or service.
The number of guests can be changed for reservations that are in progress or haven’t yet started. If you need to change your guest count before printing or downloading your itinerary for visa purposes, find outhow to add or remove guestson your reservation.

title: Helpful tips for traveling to Cuba
content:
Helpful tips for traveling to Cuba
Since Airbnb is just getting started in Cuba, here are some extra tips and guidelines to make sure your trip goes as smoothly as possible.
If you need anything for your Cuba trip, call us at one of these numbers:
+1-888-404-6895 (US toll-free)
+1-305-203-0879 (Miami)
+1-437-888-3133 (Toronto)
Internet access in Cuba can be sporadic. We suggest printing your itinerary, contact numbers and addresses, and any maps you’ll need before leaving.
If you’re a US citizen, consider enrolling in theSmart Traveler Enrollment Program (STEP). This will help local embassies andUS Citizen Servicesprovide assistance faster, in the unlikely event of a problem.
If you have an emergency while in Cuba, dial 106 to contact local police, fire department, or medical services.
Your Host in Cuba is legally required to record every guest's passport details at check-in. Your Host won’t need to hold your passport—they'll only need to copy down a few details to bring to their local immigration office.
Airbnb is new to Cuba, but your Host isn't. Don't be afraid to ask questions, get directions, or share expectations. Also, be sure to check with your Host before bringing visitors to your place. Many Hosts don't accept visitors, but if they do, they'll need your visitors' passport details as well.

title: How the Resolution Center helps you
content:
How the Resolution Center helps you
Need to send or request money for things related to your home stay, service, or experience? No problem! Go to theResolution Centerto open a refund or payment request.
You may need to add a payment method before sending or requesting money through the Resolution Center.
You have up to 60 days after your reservation’s checkout date or the end of the service or experience to submit a Resolution Center request.
Refunds
Refunds agreed upon by both host and guests
Requests from guests for refunds
Optional fees
For example:
Host offering paid transportation service to their home, business, or experience location
Guest access to elected amenities or upgrades
Security deposits
Security deposits can only be charged byselect software-connected hostsand must be properly disclosed in the appropriate fee field, so that it is disclosed to guests at checkout.
Reservation alteration
You can’t use the Resolution Center to collect payment for additional costs or provide refunds for modifications to nights, guest count, or pet count. You must useChange reservationforreservation alterations.
Mandatory fees and security deposits
All mandatory fees must be disclosed in theappropriate fee fieldor in the nightly price if there is no applicable fee field. Including these fees in the listing description at the time of booking is insufficient. Limited exceptions exist:
Select software-connected hostsmay collect payment for disclosed mandatory fees and security deposits separately in the Resolution Center. These mandatory fees include:Utilities (water, heat, electricity, air conditioning)Resort feesLinen feesManagement feesHOA feesCleaning feesSecurity deposits
Utilities (water, heat, electricity, air conditioning)
Resort fees
Linen fees
Management fees
HOA fees
Cleaning fees
Security deposits
In locations where Airbnb does not collect taxes or where hosts are legally required to collect them directly from guests, hosts must disclose taxes in the listing description.
Hotel stays
The Resolution Center may not be available for use with some hotel stays. Hotels may also collect payment off of the Airbnb platform for optional fees (ex: parking) where it is in the course of normal business practices.
If a host or guest requests money for extra services or a refund, you’ll find their request in your email associated with your Airbnb account or in theResolution Center.
Every now and then, hosts and guests aren’t able to make it work. If you haven’t come to an agreement, you’ll see the option to ask Airbnb to help you mediate.
Issues must be reported to Airbnb within 72 hours of discovery to be eligible under ourrebooking and refund policy for homesor ourrefund policy for services and experiences. From there, a dedicated team member will review the info provided by everyone and ask questions (if necessary) before making a final decision.
Sometimes accidents happen, which is why there’sAirCover for Hosts.
AirCover for Hosts is top-to-bottom protection for hosts. It includes $3 million inHost damage protection, in addition to $1 million liability insurance, guest identity verification, reservation screening and a 24-hour safety line. It’s always included and always free whenever you host.
If a guest causes damage to your place or belongings during an Airbnb stay, you can file a reimbursement request through our Resolution Centre. You’re reimbursed for certain damage caused by guests to your home and belongings if the guest does not pay for the damage.Learn about the process.
Host damage protection isn’t an insurance policy. Host damage protection andExperiences & Services liability insurancedon’t cover Hosts who offer stays through Airbnb Travel, LLC or Hosts who offer Stays or Experiences in Japan – whereJapan Host InsuranceandJapan Experience Protection Insuranceapply. For listings in Washington state, Airbnb’s contractual obligations under Host damage protection are covered by an insurance policy purchased by Airbnb. For hosts whose country of residence or establishment is outside of Australia,these Host Damage Protection Termsapply. For Hosts whose country of residence or establishment is within Australia, Host damage protection is subject to theHost Damage Protection Terms for Australian Users. Keep in mind that all coverage limits are shown in USD.
Host liability insurance and Experiences & Services liability insurance are underwritten by third-party insurers. If you’re hosting in the UK, the Host liability insurance policy and Experiences & Services liability insurance policy are underwritten by Zurich Insurance Company Ltd and are arranged and concluded at no additional cost for UK Hosts by Airbnb UK Services Limited – an appointed representative of Aon UK Limited, authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority. Aon’s FCA register number is 310451. You can check this on theFinancial Services Registerby visiting the FCA’s website or contacting the FCA on 0800 111 6768. The Host liability and Experiences & Services liability policies within AirCover for Hosts are regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority; the remaining products and services are not regulated products arranged by Airbnb UK Services Limited. FP.AFF.476.LC
In the EU, these policies have been arranged and concluded at no additional cost for the benefit of EU Hosts by Airbnb Marketing Services SLU, an external collaborator of Aon Iberia Correduría de Seguros y Reaseguros, SAU, which is authorised and regulated by Autoridad de Control de laDirección General de Seguros y Fondos de Pensiones, registered by the serial number of J0170. Aon Iberia Correduría de Seguros y Reaseguros SAU is acting as intermediary for the EU, participating in the distribution of insurance in EU countries under the freedom of services regime to provide services under the Spanish Insurance Distribution Law, the Insurance Distribution Directive, and other legal or regulatory provisions. Without prejudice to the authority of the Host country where insurance distribution services are provided, the member state responsible for the supervision of Aon is the Kingdom of Spain and the Supervisory Authority, the General Directorate of Insurance and Pension Funds, at Paseo de la Castellana 44, 28046 – Madrid.

title: After your home stay, service, or experience: Handling disagreements
content:
After your home stay, service, or experience: Handling disagreements
Disagreements don’t have to be disagreeable when you have a way to address the issues. Here’s how we can help:
You can use ourResolution Centerwithin 60 days of your reservation's checkout date or end time to request or send money for things related to your home stay, service, or experience. It helps to have any photos or other relevant evidence ready when you submit a Resolution Center request. Learn more abouthow the Resolution Center helps you.
Staying at a hotel? The Resolution Center may not be available for use with some hotel stays. In those cases, you will need to resolve issues directly with the hotel. You may also contact us to help mediate issues where helpful.
You canrespond to a reviewwith your perspective of the situation, or to address feedback given. It will post directly below the review and will be visible to the rest of the Airbnb community. If the review you received isagainst our policies, you can report it.
Learn more aboutreviews for homesandreviews for services and experiences.

title: Request or send money in the Resolution Center
content:
Request or send money in the Resolution Center
A host sending their guest a partial refund for a missing amenity, a guest sending their host a payment for breaking a coffee mug, and much more—the Resolution Center makes it easy to handle money-related requests for an Airbnb home, service, or experience.
Just keep in mind that:
You must use Airbnb’s Change reservation tool for reservation alterations. You can’t use the Resolution Center to collect payment for additional costs or provide refunds to nights, guest count, or pet count.
With few exceptions, hosts cannot charge guests for mandatory fees through the Resolution Center. Exceptions include:Select software-connected hoststhat may collect payment for mandatory fees and security deposits separately in the Resolution Center. These fees would be detailed in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout.In locations where Airbnb does not collect taxes or where hosts are legally required to collect them directly from guests, hosts must disclose taxes in the listing description and may collect them over the Resolution Center.
Select software-connected hoststhat may collect payment for mandatory fees and security deposits separately in the Resolution Center. These fees would be detailed in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout.
In locations where Airbnb does not collect taxes or where hosts are legally required to collect them directly from guests, hosts must disclose taxes in the listing description and may collect them over the Resolution Center.
Learn more about what theResolution Center can and cannot be used for.
Request money from your host or guest on desktop
ClickResolution Centerand then clickRequest money
Select the reservation and clickChoose
UnderWhat is this request for?, choose the reason that best describes why you’re requesting money
Enter the amount you’re requesting
Upload optional attachments and add a note
ClickNextand thenRequest
Send money on desktop
ClickResolution Centerand then clickSend money
Select the reservation and clickChoose
UnderWhat is this payment for?, choose the reason that best describes why you’re sending money
Enter the payment amount and add a note
ClickNext
Choose a payment method and then clickConfirm and pay
You can only submit a new Resolution Center request up to 60 days after a completed reservation (except for damage-related claims, which need to be filed 14 days within checkout).
You can respond to an existing Resolution Center request—like choosing to pay the full amount, paying a different amount, or declining the request—more than 60 days after checkout, as long as the request was submitted within the 60 days after completion.
Hosts can use the Resolution Center to request money from a guest before their confirmed reservation starts, but they can't send money to a guest until the reservation has started or has been canceled. Guests can send money to a host before their reservation starts, but they can’t request money from a host until their reservation has started.
If a guest cancels their reservation before it starts and their host has agreed to a full refund, either the guest or host can initiate it:
The guest can request a full refund from the host in the Resolution Center
The host can contact Airbnb to have our Community Support team refund the guest in full
Hosts and guests can both use the Resolution Center to send or request money from one another after the reservation starts.
If a guest cancels their reservation and it’s past the start date or time, their host can send a full refund to the guest in the Resolution Center.
The Resolution Center may not be available for use with some hotel stays. In those cases, guests of hotel stays and their hosts will need to resolve any money-related requests directly.

title: Find your Resolution Center requests
content:
Find your Resolution Center requests
Want to check on the status of a request you sent, respond to one that you received, or review a past request? Learn how to find Resolution Center requests.
Find a request in the Resolution Center on desktop
ClickResolution Center
Locate the request you want and click anywhere in the row to open it
Find outhow to send or request money in the Resolution Center.

title: What to do if you forgot something during a home stay, service, or experience
content:
What to do if you forgot something during a home stay, service, or experience
If you left something behind at a place you stayed, or during a service or experience,message your hostdirectly for help.
If you need to have an item sent to you, you can send your host money to cover the shipping cost directly through ourResolution Center.

title: Paying and communicating through Airbnb
content:
Paying and communicating through Airbnb
It'sagainst Airbnb policyfor hosts to ask you to take your booking off of Airbnb or to communicate outside the Airbnb platform through a messaging service (ex: WhatsApp, Facebook, etc.), text, or any platform other than Airbnb prior to booking. If you choose to pay for your booking outside of Airbnb, you will lose theAirCoverprotection that comes with booking through our platform for no extra cost.
WithAirCover, you’ll have access to our global Community Support team who are on hand 24/7 to support with booking assistance, refunds and reimbursements—and to prevent fraud. AirCover also supports you if there’s a serious issue with your reservation that you can’t resolve with your host.
In some cases, hostsmay ask you to payoutside of the Airbnb platform, but all mandatory charges must be included in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout. Any payment for optional amenities should be sent on Airbnb using theResolution Center.
Leaving Airbnb also makes it harder for us to protect your information and puts you at greater risk of fraud and other security issues, such asphishing. If you pay via wire or bank transfer it may be a fraudulent reservation. To get help,let us know immediately. You can always check if yourreservation was booked through Airbnb.
If a host suggests you pay for the reservation outside of the Airbnb platform or communicate outside of the Airbnb platform prior to booking, pleasereport it to usimmediately.
We do our best to keep you safe by reviewing messages on the Airbnb platform. We’ll blockpotentially dangerous messagesthat contain words or numbers that might include contact information or references to other sites, including external links.
If you get a suspicious message, let us know byreporting itorflagging it.
Learn more about ourPrivacy Policy.

title: When you’ll pay for your reservation
content:
When you’ll pay for your reservation
Want to know when you’ll be charged for a reservation? It all depends on the type of reservation and how you pay.For most reservations, your payment method will be charged as soon as your reservation is confirmed.
No matter how far in advance you book, payment will not be issued to your host until 24 hours after your scheduled check-in for a home reservation. This gives you and the host time to make sure everything is as expected. Experience and service hosts are paid after the experience or service has been completed.

title: How the Resolution Center helps you
content:
How the Resolution Center helps you
Need to send or request money for things related to your home stay, service, or experience? No problem! Go to theResolution Centerto open a refund or payment request.
You may need to add a payment method before sending or requesting money through the Resolution Center.
You have up to 60 days after your reservation’s checkout date or the end of the service or experience to submit a Resolution Center request.
Refunds
Refunds agreed upon by both host and guests
Requests from guests for refunds
Optional fees
For example:
Host offering paid transportation service to their home, business, or experience location
Guest access to elected amenities or upgrades
Security deposits
Security deposits can only be charged byselect software-connected hostsand must be properly disclosed in the appropriate fee field, so that it is disclosed to guests at checkout.
Reservation alteration
You can’t use the Resolution Center to collect payment for additional costs or provide refunds for modifications to nights, guest count, or pet count. You must useChange reservationforreservation alterations.
Mandatory fees and security deposits
All mandatory fees must be disclosed in theappropriate fee fieldor in the nightly price if there is no applicable fee field. Including these fees in the listing description at the time of booking is insufficient. Limited exceptions exist:
Select software-connected hostsmay collect payment for disclosed mandatory fees and security deposits separately in the Resolution Center. These mandatory fees include:Utilities (water, heat, electricity, air conditioning)Resort feesLinen feesManagement feesHOA feesCleaning feesSecurity deposits
Utilities (water, heat, electricity, air conditioning)
Resort fees
Linen fees
Management fees
HOA fees
Cleaning fees
Security deposits
In locations where Airbnb does not collect taxes or where hosts are legally required to collect them directly from guests, hosts must disclose taxes in the listing description.
Hotel stays
The Resolution Center may not be available for use with some hotel stays. Hotels may also collect payment off of the Airbnb platform for optional fees (ex: parking) where it is in the course of normal business practices.
If a host or guest requests money for extra services or a refund, you’ll find their request in your email associated with your Airbnb account or in theResolution Center.
Every now and then, hosts and guests aren’t able to make it work. If you haven’t come to an agreement, you’ll see the option to ask Airbnb to help you mediate.
Issues must be reported to Airbnb within 72 hours of discovery to be eligible under ourrebooking and refund policy for homesor ourrefund policy for services and experiences. From there, a dedicated team member will review the info provided by everyone and ask questions (if necessary) before making a final decision.
Sometimes accidents happen, which is why there’sAirCover for Hosts.
AirCover for Hosts is top-to-bottom protection for hosts. It includes $3 million inHost damage protection, in addition to $1 million liability insurance, guest identity verification, reservation screening and a 24-hour safety line. It’s always included and always free whenever you host.
If a guest causes damage to your place or belongings during an Airbnb stay, you can file a reimbursement request through our Resolution Centre. You’re reimbursed for certain damage caused by guests to your home and belongings if the guest does not pay for the damage.Learn about the process.
Host damage protection isn’t an insurance policy. Host damage protection andExperiences & Services liability insurancedon’t cover Hosts who offer stays through Airbnb Travel, LLC or Hosts who offer Stays or Experiences in Japan – whereJapan Host InsuranceandJapan Experience Protection Insuranceapply. For listings in Washington state, Airbnb’s contractual obligations under Host damage protection are covered by an insurance policy purchased by Airbnb. For hosts whose country of residence or establishment is outside of Australia,these Host Damage Protection Termsapply. For Hosts whose country of residence or establishment is within Australia, Host damage protection is subject to theHost Damage Protection Terms for Australian Users. Keep in mind that all coverage limits are shown in USD.
Host liability insurance and Experiences & Services liability insurance are underwritten by third-party insurers. If you’re hosting in the UK, the Host liability insurance policy and Experiences & Services liability insurance policy are underwritten by Zurich Insurance Company Ltd and are arranged and concluded at no additional cost for UK Hosts by Airbnb UK Services Limited – an appointed representative of Aon UK Limited, authorised and regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority. Aon’s FCA register number is 310451. You can check this on theFinancial Services Registerby visiting the FCA’s website or contacting the FCA on 0800 111 6768. The Host liability and Experiences & Services liability policies within AirCover for Hosts are regulated by the Financial Conduct Authority; the remaining products and services are not regulated products arranged by Airbnb UK Services Limited. FP.AFF.476.LC
In the EU, these policies have been arranged and concluded at no additional cost for the benefit of EU Hosts by Airbnb Marketing Services SLU, an external collaborator of Aon Iberia Correduría de Seguros y Reaseguros, SAU, which is authorised and regulated by Autoridad de Control de laDirección General de Seguros y Fondos de Pensiones, registered by the serial number of J0170. Aon Iberia Correduría de Seguros y Reaseguros SAU is acting as intermediary for the EU, participating in the distribution of insurance in EU countries under the freedom of services regime to provide services under the Spanish Insurance Distribution Law, the Insurance Distribution Directive, and other legal or regulatory provisions. Without prejudice to the authority of the Host country where insurance distribution services are provided, the member state responsible for the supervision of Aon is the Kingdom of Spain and the Supervisory Authority, the General Directorate of Insurance and Pension Funds, at Paseo de la Castellana 44, 28046 – Madrid.

title: How to get to the Resolution Center
content:
How to get to the Resolution Center
You can use theResolution Centerif you need to send or request money for things related to your Airbnb stay or experience.
You have up to 60 days after your reservation’s checkout date to submit a Resolution Center request.
If you’re within the 60 days allowed, there will be a link on your Help Center homepage to take you to the Resolution Center. It will be underRecommended for youand you’ll be able to click or tap it toGo to the Resolution Center.
You can always use the Help Center search and follow links to theResolution Center.

title: Pay for your reservation with Airbnb scheduled payments
content:
Pay for your reservation with Airbnb scheduled payments
Here are your options for scheduled payments when making your reservation.
Pay Part Now, Part Later
You can pay part of the reservation when your reservation is confirmed, and the rest at a later time prior to check-in, as long as your trip is eligible and Pay Part Now, Part Later is available when you pay on the checkout page.
Reserve Now, Pay Later
You may reserve a listing without paying anything at the time of booking and pay the full amount of your reservation at a later time prior to check-in, as long as your trip is eligible and Reserve Now, Pay Later is available on the checkout page. Reserve Now, Pay Later is available only for listings with certain cancellation policies.
Note: If you choose Reserve Now, Pay Later and select travel insurance, climate contribution, or other optional add-ons, the costs for those options will be charged at the time of booking.
When you choose Airbnb scheduled payment options, you can always pay earlier than the scheduled due date and your original payment method will be charged. You also have the option to update your payment method.
How the total payment is split depends on the reservation and can’t be changed. When making a reservation, you’ll be shown a payment split and schedule based on the dates and thecancellation policyof your reservation. Once the reservation is confirmed, scheduled payment dates will be communicated in confirmation emails and inyour payments.
If your reservation meets all of the following criteria, you may be eligible for one of Airbnb scheduled payments options:
You pay with a credit card, debit card, gift card, Airbnb credit, or PayPal—or, if you are a US traveler, when you pay bybank account
The check-in date is at least 14 days away (this also depends on yourhost’s cancellation policy)
The reservation is a short-term stay (less than 28 nights)
After any coupons and credits are applied, the reservation has a remaining total of $50 USD or more, including taxes and fees
Reservations for business travel booked usingAirbnb for Work
Reservations for Airbnb Services and Experiences
Reservations that are monthly stays (28 nights or more)
If you’re a guest in:Brazil that has selected Brazilian Reais as currency of paymentIndia paying in Indian Rupees
Brazil that has selected Brazilian Reais as currency of payment
India paying in Indian Rupees
If you’re eligible to pay with Pay Part Now, Part Later, your payment method will be charged as soon as your reservation is confirmed for the first payment, and the later payment will be automatically charged on the scheduled date listed at checkout.
If you choose to pay with Reserve Now, Pay Later, your payment method will be charged on the scheduled date listed at checkout.
To remind you of any upcoming payments, you’ll receive an email confirmation that includes information on future charges 3 days prior to the charge, along with a link to manage your payment settings orpay earlyif you’d like. In case the scheduled charge is unsuccessful, you’ll be contacted by email and push notification to complete the payment to avoid cancellation of the reservation.
Learn more aboutwhen you’ll be charged for a reservation paid forwith Airbnb scheduled payments.
If you decide to cancel your reservation, your refund or amount due will depend on how much you’ve paid at the time of cancellation. Please note that any coupons used and climate contributions are non-refundable.
Examples:
If you make a reservation using Pay Part Now, Part Later and pay 50% when your reservation is confirmed, and then cancel within the free cancellation period, you will be eligible for a refund of what you’ve already paid.
If you make a reservation using Reserve Now, Pay Later and cancel before the 100% payment is due, your scheduled payment will be cancelled and your payment method will not be charged for the reservation.
If your host cancels your reservation before check-in, you’ll be fully refunded any payments that you’ve made for the reservation but any applied coupons will not be refunded. Find out more aboutwhat happens if your host cancels your reservation before check-in.
When booking a reservation with Airbnb scheduled payments, your credit, coupon or gift card is automatically applied before the scheduled payment amount(s) are calculated. You can always remove the credit, coupon or gift card before you pay, and the price details and total amount will be recalculated.
For Pay Part Now, Part Later: If you want to use credits, coupons  or gift cards for your  second payment, you can manually complete the second payment any time before its scheduled payment date by visitingyour payments. Find outhow to pay your Airbnb scheduled payments  early. If you don’t make the second payment before its scheduled payment date, the full second payment amount will be charged to the payment method you chose when making the reservation.
For Reserve Now, Pay Later: If you want to use credits, coupons or gift cards earned after your reservation is confirmed, you can manually complete the payment any time before the scheduled payment date by visitingyour payments. Find outhow to pay your Airbnb scheduled payment early. If you don’t make the payment before its scheduled payment date, the full amount will be charged to the payment method you chose when making the reservation.
Keep in mind: Only one coupon can be applied per reservation you book.
If you reside in Brazil, you may have the option to pay for your reservation over the course oftwo or more monthly installments.
You may be eligible to pay for a reservation over a number of weeks or months with Pay Over Time with Klarna:Pay for your reservation over time with Klarna.

title: Payment in installments in Brazil
content:
Payment in installments in Brazil
If you reside in Brazil, you can pay in up to 12 monthly installments as long as your trip is eligible and the installments option is available when you pay on the checkout page.
If your reservation meets all of the following criteria, you may be eligible for payment in installments:
Your currency is Brazilian real (BRL).
You’re paying with a credit card issued in Brazil.
The billing country for your payment method is Brazil (ex: The billing address you provided to your credit card company is in Brazil).
You’re not booking with an Airbnb gift card.
You’re booking a stay, not an Airbnb Experience or Service.
You’re not booking a long-term reservation of 28 days or more.
The payment isn’t for an alteration to a previous payment or for a Resolution Center request.
You are not paying using credits as your payment option.

title: If a host asks for more money
content:
If a host asks for more money
Hosts are not permitted to collect any fees related to Airbnb reservations outside of our platform, with limited exceptions:
In select cases, certainmandatory feesor security deposits may show in a listing’s price breakdown at checkout that are due to the host at property.
Hosts may collect taxes at check-in, but only if these are stated in the listing description.
Hotels may collect an incidental hold, but only if this is stated in the listing description.
Hosts may charge for optional add-ons such as upgrades or access to certain amenities, but only if they are clearly stated in the listing description prior to booking and collected using the Resolution Center. Hotels may collect payment for optional add-ons off of the Airbnb platform where it is part of their standard procedures.
Your hostfiles an AirCover for Hosts requestfor damage that occurred during your stay, experience, or service. AirCover claims must be paid on Airbnb.
Adding additional guests, pets, or nights to your reservation may impact costs. For home stays, guests and hosts must use thechange reservationtool to make these changes.
With few exceptions, we prohibit hosts from:
Failing to disclose reservation fees at the time of booking
Requesting, sending, or receiving payment for any reservation fee off of Airbnb
Please review ourOff-platform and Fee Transparency Policyfor the list of what is prohibited and any exceptions. If a host tries to charge you for something that violates our policies or attempts to charge you off the Airbnb platform instead of using theResolution Center, pleasereport it to usimmediately.
We don't allow hosts to charge a pet fee or cleaning fees for animal hair or dander to guests who are traveling withService Animals anywhere or Emotional Support Animalsin places where applicable laws prohibit the extra fees, such as New York State and California.
If you agree with the charge, pay the host directly through theResolution Center. Be sure tokeep all payment transactions on Airbnb—otherwise, we can’t help with issues related to off-site or cash payments.
In limited cases, your host can request payments outside of the Airbnb platform for a fee included in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout or a tax that’s allowed to be collected outside Airbnb and was included in the listing description (or house rules). In either case, you can pay your host in person, using a payment app, or using theResolution Center.
Hotels may also collect payment off of the Airbnb platform for optional fees where it is in the course of normal business practices (ex: parking). Other hosts must collect payment for optional fees using theResolution Center.
Payments for optional fees (ex: parking, amenity upgrades) must be collected on our platform using theResolution Center.

title: What happens to your payment when a home reservation request is declined or expires
content:
What happens to your payment when a home reservation request is declined or expires
If your home reservation request isdeclined by the host or expires, the authorization placed on your payment method will be removed. Or, if your payment method was already charged, you’ll be refunded.
When you submit a home reservation request, we may place ahold on your payment method, called an authorization, for the full amount of the reservation.
When your home reservation request is declined or expires, the authorization will disappear or appear as a refund on your payment method. The release of the authorization may take up to 5 business days, depending on the processing time of your bank or financial institution.
Some payment methods or locations require the full amount of the home reservation to be charged when you submit a reservation request.
When your home reservation request is declined or expires, we’ll send your refund right away. How long it takes for you to receive the money depends on your bank or financial institution. Find the commonrefund timelines.
If the host accepts your home reservation request, your reservation is confirmed. Any authorization on your payment method will become a real charge and the amount will be deducted from your payment method. If you were already charged, the charge on your payment method will remain.

title: Who’s eligible to use pay over time with Klarna
content:
Who’s eligible to use pay over time with Klarna
Interested in using a payment plan withKlarna? Find out if you're eligible to get started.
To be eligible for a Klarna payment plan, you must:
Be at least 18 in your country of residence
Have a valid card/bank account
Have full legal capacity to enter into a contract
Be able to receive security verification codes via SMS text message
Please visit the Klarna website for specific requirements based on your country of residence.
Klarna verifies your personal information and performs an eligibility check to determine if they can offer you a payment plan. If paying over time with Klarna is available, you’ll have the option on the checkout page.
Klarna’s payment plans availability and requirements vary by country.
Klarna is available in the US, but pay monthly is not supported in Iowa, West Virginia, or Massachusetts.. Klarna payment plans are also available to guests who are residents of Canada, along with Australia, New Zealand, the UK, and select countries in Europe—and are being introduced in a range of countries.
The eligibility check is conducted by Klarna, and Airbnb does not influence the outcome of this assessment. If your application is declined by Klarna, you can still complete your reservation using an alternative payment method.
It’s important to note that an offer of a Klarna payment plan for one trip is not a guarantee of being offered a Klarna payment plan for other trips—or for any additional charges because of a reservation change request.
Klarna isn’t currently available for Airbnb Experiences or monthly stays.
However, if you have a reservation of 28 nights or more, check outpayment plan options for monthly stays.
Need more help? ContactKlarna's customer serviceto get immediate support.

title: When you'll be charged for a reservation using a payment plan
content:
When you'll be charged for a reservation using a payment plan
Using a pay part now, part later payment plan to book your reservation on Airbnb may make budgeting easier, but it’s important to know when you’ll be charged. Find out when to expect charges and what happens if your payment doesn’t go through.
If you choose to use apay part now, part later payment plan, you’ll pay a portion of the total when you book the reservation, and the remaining balance will be automatically charged on the date listed when your reservation is confirmed.
You’ll receive an email confirmation with information about your upcoming payment to help keep any charges on schedule, and you can always check when your upcoming payment is scheduled in your Airbnb account.
If the second payment of your payment plan doesn’t go through, we’ll let you know and ask you to make the second payment right away. If you’re unable to complete the second payment within 72 hours, your reservation will be canceled—any refund for the first payment will be determined by thecancellation policyfor your reservation.
To check upcoming payments on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to check
UnderReservation details, clickPayment info
ClickGet receipts and manage payments
UnderUpcoming, you'll find your scheduled payment date and amount
You always have the option to pay the second installment any time before the scheduled payment date. Find outhow to make a payment early.

title: Pay your scheduled payment early
content:
Pay your scheduled payment early
Whether you want to pay ahead of schedule to better manage your finances or simply check it off your to-do list, find out how to pay your scheduled payment early.
If you booked your reservation using Pay part now, part later, Reserve now, pay later, or have scheduled payments for a Monthly Stay, you always have the option to complete a payment or change the payment method any time before the scheduled payment date.
Make a payment early on desktop
ClickTripsand select your reservation
UnderReservation details, clickGet receipts and manage payments
Select the payment underUpcoming
UnderPay with, choose a new payment method or keep the payment method selected
UnderWhen do you want to pay?, clickPay now
ClickConfirm and pay
Note: If you booked a monthly stay, early payments can only be made within the 7 days before your payment is due.
When you book a reservation paid for using Klarna, you may be able to make installment payments early through your Klarna account. Log into your Klarna account to manage your payment plan.

title: Pay for a monthly stay
content:
Pay for a monthly stay
If you book a monthly stay (28 nights or more), you’ll be charged a first month payment, and then you’ll pay the rest in monthly installments.
If you want to book a place for 28 nights or more, here’s how payments work:
First-month payment: You'll be charged this up front, but we’ll hold off on releasing it to the host until 24 hours after check-in
Monthly installments: For reservations longer than a month, we will charge you 10 days in advance for each payment period after the first payment
Your price is locked in at the beginning of the reservation. You can track your payment details inTrips, or from your confirmation email.
To check upcoming payments on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to check
UnderReservation details, clickPayment info
ClickGet receipts and manage payments
UnderUpcoming, you'll find your scheduled payment date and amount
If you're based in the US, you can save even more money when youpay with your bank accountfor eligible reservations of 28 nights or more.
Thelong-term cancellation policyis automatically applied to all reservations of 28 nights or more, and generally requires a 30-day notice of changes to the reservation.
While it’s not required, you always have theoption to pay upcoming monthly installments a little early, as long as it’s within 7 days before your payment is due.
For example, if your payment is due on July 11, you’ll be able to pay as early as July 4 at 12am UTC.
For domestic stays over 28 nights in India, you’ll be charged in full when your reservation is confirmed. Learn more aboutpaying for a monthly stay in India.

title: Submit statements for payment verification
content:
Submit statements for payment verification
After you've finished addingPayment Methods, we may ask you to verify it with a credit or debit card statement. Need a recent statement? Try downloading it through your card issuer's website or mobile app.
We’ll email you within 24 hours of submission to let you know if you can use the credit or debit card on Airbnb.

title: Tipping service and experience hosts
content:
Tipping service and experience hosts
Acknowledgment for a job well done is always appreciated, whether it’s a good review or a tip. Guests often choose services and experiences because ofin-depth reviews, so you would help the entire Airbnb community if you share your thoughts. Just make sure to followour review policywhen leaving your review.

title: Paying for reservations in India
content:
Paying for reservations in India
Indian credit cards require two-factor authentication, so guests residing there are charged for the full cost of the reservation when submitting a trip request. We’re unable to change the payment method once you’re booked.
If your request expires or is declined by the Host, we’ll immediately refund you in full, which can take up to 15 days for you to receive, depending on your bank or payment provider.
Be careful submitting multiple trip requests for the same dates—if they’re all confirmed, you’ll be double-booked and the reservations will be subject to the Host’s cancellation policy.

title: If a host asks you to pay outside Airbnb
content:
If a host asks you to pay outside Airbnb
The full cost of your reservation iscollected by Airbnb, with few exceptions.
In some cases, hosts may collect additional payments outside of the Airbnb platform, but all mandatory charges must be included in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout.
On occasion, a host may collect certain fees and taxes directly from guests using a payment method outside Airbnb. These fees can only be collected by certain authorized hosts. Such fees are usually charged before the stay, at check-in, or within 48 hours of checkout.
In some areas, hosts are legally required to collect local taxes in person. These taxes must be detailed in the listing description or house rules if they aren’t included in the price breakdown at checkout. Find out more abouthow taxes work for guests.
Additionally, some hosts also offer additional optional amenities to enhance their guest's stays. Any payment for optional amenities should be sent on Airbnb using theResolution Center.
Learn more aboutwhat fees hosts can collect outside Airbnb.
Your host can request payments outside of the Airbnb platform for a fee detailed in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout or a tax that’s allowed to be collected outside Airbnb and was included in the listing description (or house rules). In either case, you can pay your host in person, using a payment app, or using theResolution Center, where you can securely send or request money for things related to your home stay, service, or experience.
Hotels may also collect payment off of the Airbnb platform for optional fees where it is in the course of normal business practices (ex: parking).
Other hosts must collect payment for optional fees using theResolution Center.
Payments for optional fees (ex: parking, amenity upgrades) must be collected on our platform using theResolution Center.
If you're asked on Airbnb, via email, or through another method of communication to make a payment outside Airbnb without prior notice, it may be fraudulent. If any of the following occur in connection with any such payment requests, pleasereport it to usimmediately:
You suspect fraud
You are caught off-guard by an unfamiliar request to pay outside Airbnb
You would like to dispute a host’s payment request
You receive an email requesting an unauthorized payment offsite, which includes payments requests from anyone claiming to represent Airbnb
You believe a host is attempting to collect a mandatory fee that was not included in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout (ex: pet fees, utilities, security deposit) or the host should not be attempting to collect the fee outside Airbnb.
Please note that signs of fraud may include wire or bank transfer requests, cashier’s check, money order, and PDF or paper invoices.
Report a message on desktop
ClickMessagesand select the conversation you want to report
In the message, click the three dots
ClickReport this message
Select the reason you're reporting the message and clickOK
Give further details (if required), and then clickSubmit

title: Payment methods accepted
content:
Payment methods accepted
Airbnb accepts a variety of payment methods. However, your available options will vary by region.
Depending on the country your guest account is located in, Airbnb supports a variety of payment methods. You’ll see all available payment options at checkout. If you don’t see a payment method, it’s not available in your locale.
Residents of the USA and Canada have the option to pay with Klarna, allowing you to pay over time, rather than all at once. Klarna accepts all major debit and credit cards–like Visa, Discover, Maestro, and Mastercard. Prepaid cards are not accepted. Learn more aboutPaying with Klarna.
