United States
Discover
Bank account
Offline or cash payments are a violation of ourTerms of Service, and can result in removal from Airbnb.Off-site paymentsmake it harder for us to protect your information, and put you at greater risk of fraud and other security issues.

title: Add or remove a payment method
content:
Add or remove a payment method
If an existing payment method on your account is incorrect (ex: an expired credit card), you can update it or add a new payment method.
You can add a credit or debit card or another payment method to your account.
Add a payment method on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Payments
ClickAdd payment method
Complete payment information and clickDone
Guests with payment methods based in some countries—like India and Brazil—may not be able to add a payment method to their account. In this case, you’ll need to add your payment details at checkout when you’re booking the reservation. Find out more aboutwhat payment methods are acceptedin certain countries.
You can remove a payment method from your account unless it’s being used on a pending or active reservation, or a past reservation that ended less than 14 days ago. You also can't remove your payment method if your host has requested a reimbursement from you and Airbnb is reviewing their request.
Remove a payment method on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Payments
ClickPayment methods, and then click the three dots next to the payment method
ClickRemove
You can choose which payment method appears first on your list of available payment methods.
Set a default payment method on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Payments
UnderPayment methods, click the 3 dots next to the payment method you want to set as default
ClickSet default

title: When you can change your payment method
content:
When you can change your payment method
Before booking a reservation, you canadd a payment methodat any time. However, once a payment is made using a certain payment method, the charge can’t be changed to a different payment method.
If you have any scheduled future payments, you canchange the payment method for an upcoming paymentbefore it’s processed.
If your reservation is confirmed and paid for, you can’t change the payment method.
If you've requested to book a listing but the host hasn't responded yet (the reservation is not confirmed), you cancancel your reservation requestand make another request with a different payment method.
Find outhow to add or remove a payment methodon your account.
If you paid for your stay using a pay part now, part later payment plan, learn how tochange your payment method for upcoming payments.
If your stay is for 28 nights or longer, learn how tochange your payment method for upcoming payments.

title: Paying for your reservation using multiple payment methods
content:
Paying for your reservation using multiple payment methods
Let’s keep things simple. Multiple payment methods can’t be used when you’re booking a reservation and paying for it in full.
The exceptions are when:
You’re paying part of the cost with a coupon, gift card, or travel credit
You book a monthly stay or pay using an Airbnb payment plan—for these reservations, you can always change your payment method for future scheduled payments
You can’t split the total cost of your Airbnb stay or experience across multiple payment methods. For example, you can’t pay using 2 different credit cards, or pay using a credit card and Paypal.
For these reservations, you’ll have one or more future scheduled payments, and you can use a different payment method for upcoming payments. Find outhow to change your payment method for upcoming payments.
If you have acoupon,gift card, ortravel creditthat covers part of the cost of your reservation, you’ll use another payment method for the remaining cost.

title: Confirm a payment method
content:
Confirm a payment method
Before completing a transaction with a new payment method, you’ll need to confirm it first. This process won’t charge your new payment method, it just verifies that your details are correct to ensure a smooth experience in the future.
Confirm a payment method on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Payments
ClickPayment methods
ClickConfirm cardfor the payment method that needs to be confirmed
Follow the directions to complete the confirmation
When your payment method isn’t confirmed, you won’t be able to finish booking a reservation or make new reservations with that payment method.
Any upcoming reservations that are already confirmed won’t be affected.

title: Supported currencies and currency fees
content:
Supported currencies and currency fees
Airbnb offers a number of payment options in various currencies, allowing you toselect your default currencyandchange it at any time.
Note:Some currencies may not be available for all payment methods. Some currencies may also come with additional fees.
Airbnb displays prices in the currency you selected. If you change the currency at any point while booking, the price will automatically update in the new currency.
Even though guests can choose their payment currency, they must select a compatible payment method. If the chosen payment method doesn’t support the selected currency, the guest will need to change either the payment method or the currency.
If a guest pays in a different currency than the host’s listing currency, an additional fee is charged. Find out more about how theservice fee may change for cross-currency bookings.
If you choose to use a currency that doesn’t match your default payment method, your bank or credit card company may apply currency conversion fees to your payment. Airbnb isn’t responsible for these fees, so please contact your bank or credit card company to learn more.
If your bank or credit card company classifies Airbnb's processing entity as international, it may charge cross-border fees—even if the charge was processed in the standard currency of your payment method.
Airbnb doesn’t manage and can’t refund these fees, so please contact your bank or credit card company for more information.

title: Set your default payment currency
content:
Set your default payment currency
Airbnb supports a number of different currencies to give you more flexibility when you stay with us. The prices on Airbnb will show in your default currency, which you canchange at any time.
Note:We only show the currencies in which we can process payments.
Set your default currency on desktop
ClickMenu
ClickLanguages & currency
ClickCurrency, and then choose your currency

title: Change your payment currency
content:
Change your payment currency
Airbnb shows your default currency, which can be easily changed if needed for scheduled future payments or when booking a reservation.
Note:We only show the currencies in which we can process payments.
Change your default currency on desktop
ClickMenu
ClickLanguages & currency
ClickCurrency, and choose your currency
If you have an upcoming reservation with payments scheduled for future dates, you can change the currency for the scheduled future payments.
Change the currency for upcoming payments on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation
UnderReservation details, clickGet receipts and manage payments
UnderUpcoming, click the payment you want to change
Click the currency next to your total to change your selection
ClickConfirm and pay
You can also change your currency whenbooking a reservation. UnderPrice details, click or tap the currency next to your total to change your selection.
Note:If there are restrictions on the currency at the time of payment, you may need to update your currency. Learn more aboutsupported currencies.
If a payment has already been made, the currency can’t be changed for that payment.

title: Automatic updates to cards on file
content:
Automatic updates to cards on file
Don’t remember updating your payment information with us? We took care of it.
We receive real-time account updates from card networks so your payment details can be updated automatically when they change. If your bank has reported changes for a card you have on file, the update service sends us the new card details securely. Then, your existing payment is updated, allowing you to book your stay without any issues.
Updates can include:
Expiration date changes
Reissued cards that were reported as lost, stolen, or expired
Account closures
You can find the new details saved to your existing payment methods in yourPayments & payouts. Card information updated as part of a booking will appear on your receipt and transaction history.
If your card hasn’t updated automatically, you can always manuallyadd or remove a payment method.

title: Pay for your reservation over time with Klarna
content:
Pay for your reservation over time with Klarna
With pay over time withKlarna, you can pay for a reservation over a number of weeks or months—giving you greater flexibility on how and when you want to pay.
If you’re eligible and have the ability to pay for your reservation in installments over time, you can use Klarna as an alternative way to pay to give yourself more flexibility.
You’ll find the available Klarna payment plans on the checkout page. Based on your location, you may be able to choose one of the following plans:
Pay monthly with Klarna (where interest may apply). Please note this option is not available in the United Kingdom.
Pay in 4 payments with Klarna (interest-free).
Pay in 3 payments (interest-free).
Pay in 30 days (only available in Europe).
Learn more about howKlarna’s eligibility for payment plans work.
You can pay in 3 or 4 payments, depending on the availability and specific options for the country you’re based in. You’ll always be charged the first payment when your reservation is confirmed. For the remaining payments, you’ll be charged:
If you choose 3 payments: Every month across 2 months, after the first payment.
If you choose 4 payments: Every 2 weeks for 6 weeks, after the first payment.
You can also pay monthly with a short-term loan. (This is not available in the United Kingdom)
Klarna accepts most major debit and credit cards such as Visa, Discover, Maestro, and Mastercard, and may differ depending on the country where you’re based. You can also choose to pay with Apple Pay or your associated bank account.
For Klarna pay monthly plans, guests can’t use a credit card to pay the reservation amount. Additionally, Klarna doesn’t allow prepaid cards to be used as a payment method.
You can apply an Airbnb coupon, credit, or gift card to your reservation booking before setting up a Klarna payment plan. The coupon, credit, or gift card will apply to the total amount of the reservation before the Klarna payment plan is calculated
Please note that any coupons used are non-refundable.
Before you accept a Klarna payment plan, you can find the following info in the Klarna pop-up:
The payment amount due immediately.
Any scheduled upcoming payment amounts and due dates.
A breakdown of fees and taxes.
Klarna terms and conditions and any applicable payment plan disclosures.
Once you accept a Klarna payment plan and are approved by Klarna, the booking will be confirmed and you’ll receive a receipt and booking confirmation notification from Airbnb. You'll also get a receipt from Klarna via email.
You can check and manage your payment plan in yourKlarna account.
Declining a Klarna payment plan doesn’t affect your trip planning—you can still select one of the available payment methods from Airbnb.
If you don’t have the option to use Klarna when booking, it may mean Klarna isn’t an option available for your trip, or Klarna isn’t available in your country.
Key factors in determining payment plan availability are reservation value and how close the check-in date is to when you’re booking. Klarna isn’t available to pay for reservations that are monthly stays (reservations that are 28 nights or longer) or Airbnb Experiences.

title: How to pay with PayPal
content:
How to pay with PayPal
If PayPal is available for your currency, you’ll see it as an option at checkout.
Once you select PayPal as your payment method, a new browser window will pop-up and you’ll be taken to the login screen on the PayPal site:
SelectPayPalat checkout, if available
A PayPal login screen will open in a new window
Log in to your PayPal account
Choose your payment method (ex: credit card or bank account)
ClickContinue, review PayPal’s policies, and clickAgree & Continue
You’ll return to Airbnb’s checkout page to confirm and complete your payment
Once the payment for the full amount of the reservation is authorized by PayPal, your reservation request will be sent (or your Instant Book reservation will be confirmed). You'll only be charged once your reservation is confirmed. If your reservation request is declined, canceled, or expires, your PayPal authorization will be voided and you won't be charged.
You may need to link a bank account or credit card to your PayPal account to complete your Airbnb reservation. PayPal will use this backup if your primary method fails. Check out the PayPal website formore information about selecting a preferred payment method.
You can transfer money securely from your bank account to Airbnb using PayPal Instant Transfer. Once set up, select your bank account as your payment source during checkout when prompted to Choose a way to pay. Visit PayPal’s website for more details aboutlinking a bank account.

title: How to pay using Alipay
content:
How to pay using Alipay
You can use the Alipay mobile app or website to pay. If you use Alipay, we recommend that you download the mobile app to complete the payment.
When you submit a reservation request and use Alipay, you'll be redirected to the Alipay website. You'll need to log in to Alipay to make your payment.
If you cancel your reservation, or if your payment is withdrawn, declined, or expired, the payment will be refunded to the card or account you selected when you paid via Alipay. The refund may take up to 5 business days to be reflected on the Alipay account.

title: Using a Gift Card for a Pay Part Now, Part Later Reservation
content:
Using a Gift Card for a Pay Part Now, Part Later Reservation
The “Pay Part Now, Part Later” (PPNPL) option makes it easy to book a reservation and pay in two smaller installments. While Airbnb gift cards can be used with this option, here are some important things to keep in mind.
You can always choose to use a gift card for your first payment of a PPNPL reservation. Just make sure the balance has beenredeemedand that credits have been added to your account.
Since a reservation can be canceled if a second payment is not received, the PPNPL option uses a single, preferred payment method by default. Here’s how to change your payment method for the second part of a PPNPL reservation.
Add a gift card to an existing PPNPL reservation on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to apply gift card credits to
ClickGet receipts and manage payments
UnderUpcoming, click the payment you want to adjust
Go toPay with
Next toCredits applied, clickManage
Select the gift card credits you want to apply to your payment
ClickConfirm and pay

title: How do I use Sofort Überweisung to pay?
content:
How do I use Sofort Überweisung to pay?
Sofort Überweisung (Germany) is an online payment method that enables you to log in with your personal online banking credentials and transfer a specified amount of your bank funds to pay for a reservation.
You do not need to register or create an account. Simply select Sofort Überweisung (or Sofort Banking) as the payment method and you’ll be guided through the payment process.
Sofort is an option to pay for your reservation if your plans meet the following conditions:
Book with a host in Europe
Select Germany as the payment country
Your reservation check-in date is at least 4 days after payment date
Your reservation doesn't include long-term payment installations
Sofort completes the payment within 24 hours after your reservation is accepted. You’ll receive an email and a push notification (if enabled on your mobile device) that the payment has been completed. If your payment doesn’t complete, your reservation will status will change to Canceled.
More info:
Sofort payments can take up to 5 days to complete
Sofort can’t be saved on your Airbnb account, so you'll need to go through the same process every time you opt to pay with Sofort

title: Pay with your bank account to save money on a monthly stay
content:
Pay with your bank account to save money on a monthly stay
If you’re a US-based traveler, you can save money when you pay with your bank account for reservations of 28 nights or more.
You’ll find the option to pay with your bank account when you’re ready to confirm and pay for your reservation. For monthly stays, the amount you’ll save will be highlighted.
You’ll be able to pay with your bank account when:
Your country of residence is the United States
Your default currency is USD
You’re booking a listing with a flexible or moderate cancellation policy or you’ll be staying 28 nights or more
For stays of less than 28 nights, the start date of your reservation is at least 9 days away (for flexible cancellation policies) or 13 days away (for moderate cancellation policies)
If you choose a different payment method during booking and switch your payment method to your bank account later, the savings won’t be applied to your original reservation.
Additionally, if you alter a short-term stay to a monthly stay, and the first payment was made with a non-pay-by-bank method, you won’t be eligible to pay with your bank account either.
The first time you select to pay with your bank account, you’ll be prompted to connect your bank account and a new window will pop up with a simple sign-in flow with our payment partnerStripe:
Click or tapAgreeto start the process
Select your bank and follow the secure sign in flow
Select which bank account you want to use and follow the prompts to connect your account
Once the connection is ready, click or tapDoneto continue checking out
After connecting your account, you’ll find your bank account in your payment wallet. Choose your bank account and click or tapConfirm and pay.For stays of 28 nights and more, your savings will be automatically applied.
For reservations of 28 nights or more, you’ll be charged for the first month upon confirmation. After that, you'll be charged the remainder in monthly installments. You can review the dates of your monthly payments on theConfirm and paypage.
Learn more aboutpaying for a monthly stay.

title: Paying and getting paid on behalf of someone else
content:
Paying and getting paid on behalf of someone else
Sometimes, you might need to pay for a reservation using someone else’s payment method or provide information to facilitate payouts for another person, such as a property owner. In these cases, we may need additional information to process the payment and comply with legal and regulatory requirements.
Note: If you provide someone else’s information, you must get their permission before sharing their details with us.
If you choose to pay using someone else’s payment method, such as their credit card or PayPal account, we’ll need certain details from you and the payment method owner. These details help us complete the transaction as outlined in ourPayments Terms of Service.
For example:
Credit card payments: We may request the name on the card, the card number, expiration date, security code, and billing address.
PayPal payments: We’ll ask for the PayPal account owner’s name and email address. You’ll then be redirected to PayPal, where the account owner can log in to confirm the payment.
If you’re arranging payouts for someone who is not a user (ex: a property owner or a business), you may need to provide their information to process the payment and fulfill legal obligations, such as tax reporting orKnow Your Customer (KYC)requirements.
Here’s the information we might request:
Personal details: Full name, date of birth, citizenship, and physical address.
Payment details: Bank account number and associated country.
Tax information (U.S. Only): Tax Identification Number (TIN).
Verification documents: In some cases, we may need a copy of the recipient’s government-issued ID. If required, we will contact the recipient directly using their contact information.
To understand how we use this information and your rights, please review ourPrivacy Policy.

title: Something went wrong with your payment
content:
Something went wrong with your payment
Is your transaction not going through? Find out why this can happen and what you can do to resolve a payment problem.
Your credit or debit card has expired
The billing address or phone number you entered doesn't match those associated with your credit or debit card
The payment method you tried isn't accepted in your area
You’ve exceeded the daily transaction amount or purchase limit set by your bank or financial institution
Your credit or debit card doesn’t have sufficient funds to make the payment
Fraud prevention has been triggered
If your credit or debit card has expired,update your payment method
Double-check the billing address, ZIP code, and phone number of your credit or debit card
Make sure thepayment method is accepted in your area
Check that yourpayment method has been confirmed
Contact your bank or financial institution to find outwhy your card might be getting declined

title: Why your payment is getting declined or isn’t working
content:
Why your payment is getting declined or isn’t working
Payments can be declined for a number of reasons. Here are some of the common errors you may be shown and what you can do to help resolve them.
If your bank requires authentication, they’ll send over an authentication request that Airbnb will display for you to complete. If you fail or exit out of the authentication request, you may be shown any of the following errors:
Looks like your bank declined the payment
If you fail the security check your transaction will be declined
Make sure your contact information with your credit card provider or bank is up-to-date, and contact them if you’re having trouble with the authentication request. Find out more abouthow to confirm your debit or credit card with 3DS authentication.
When the billing address, CVV, card number, or other info you entered doesn’t match what’s associated with your payment method, it could prevent your payment from going through. In those cases, you may receive one of the following errors:
There was an error adding your payment method
Postal code must match your bank's records
Server error prevented your request from being completed
Transaction not allowed
Specified target currency is not allowed for this country
It's important to double-check the info you’ve entered, making sure to add the details exactly as they appear on your card or bank statement. If you’re still having trouble, reach out to your bank or financial institution, or try using adifferent payment method.
If you receive any of the following errors, it’s possible that our systems detected unusual activity on your account:
You have reached your Airbnb access quota
Looks like this payment method can't be added
This credit card can’t be added right now
You’ll need to wait up to 24 hours to log in to your account and try making the payment again.
If you’re shown any of the following errors, you may need to use a different payment method:
Something went wrong
Unable to verify your card
Unable to perform action
Unable to authorize the payment to your credit card
Invalid amount
Payment Error: Amount is too large
Do not honor
First contact your bank or financial institution. If you’re still having trouble, try booking your reservation using adifferent payment method.
There might be restrictions on the currency at the time of payment due to factors like the country in which you reside or the payment method you're using. In these instances, you’ll receive the following error: Can’t complete this payment in your currency.
You can trypaying in the suggested currency, or use adifferent payment method.
It's possible that your payment method has a maximum transaction amount or daily limit set by your bank or financial institution. If the payment amount exceeds this limit, your payment won’t go through. In these cases, you’ll receive any of the following errors:
Let's try again. Sometimes changing your currency works.
account_volume_limit
It's best to contact your bank or financial institution to find out if they can provide a solution or increase your daily limit. You can also try using adifferent payment method.
When using Alipay as your payment method in the Airbnb app, you may receive the following error: Order does not exist - Alipay.
If this happens, try completing the booking on desktop or mobile browser.
It’s possible to receive an error that isn’t listed in this article. It’s best to first contact your bank or financial institution to find out why your payment isn’t working.
If you’re still having trouble, try using adifferent payment methodorcontact usfor help resolving the issue.

title: Why you’re being asked to confirm your payment method
content:
Why you’re being asked to confirm your payment method
Airbnb may ask you toconfirm your payment methodas a security measure to protect your account and ensure it's really you making the reservation.
When your payment method isn’t confirmed, you won’t be able to finish booking your reservation or make new reservations with that payment method.
Any upcoming reservations that are already confirmed won’t be affected.

title: Why you were charged twice for a reservation
content:
Why you were charged twice for a reservation
Finding an extra charge on your account might leave you puzzled. Here are some reasons you might have more than one charge for the same reservation and how to move forward in each case.
Before you submit a reservation request, you'll see whether yourchosen payment methodwill place a hold (called anauthorization) or charge on your account for the amount of the reservation.
Some payment methods or locations require that the full amount of the reservation is authorized at the time of your request. If your reservation is accepted, the authorization will turn into a real charge and the amount will be deducted from your payment account.
If the reservation request is declined or expires, you’ll either see the authorization disappear altogether or appear as a refund in your payment account. Depending on the processing time of your bank, the release of the authorization may take up to 7 business days.
Some payment methods or locations require that the full amount of the reservation ischarged at the time of your request. If your reservation request is accepted, the charge remains and your reservation is confirmed.
If the reservation request isdeclined or expires, you’ll be refunded immediately and automatically. Depending on the processing time of your bank, the refund may take up to 10 calendar days to process.
When you book a hotel through Airbnb, we collect the payment. But there may be additional charges, such as an incidental charge or hold, or a resort fee. Contact the hotel directly for more information.
If the situations above don’t apply in your case, you can usethe Resolution Center. You can also find info about what happensif you dispute a charge with your bank.

title: Verify your card using temporary authorizations
content:
Verify your card using temporary authorizations
To verify your card this way, we’ll send 2 temporary authorizations of $1.99 or less to your debit or credit card. Temporary authorizations are temporary holds that won’t actually be charged to your card.
Once you’ve completed the verification, the authorization hold will be released from your payment method. The release of the authorization may take up to 7 business days, depending on the processing time of your bank or financial institution.
To complete the verification process after you add a new card:
Log in to your card issuer’s website
Find the 2 small authorizations on your card
Enter those amounts when prompted on Airbnb

title: When there’s a payment hold or authorization on your payment method
content:
When there’s a payment hold or authorization on your payment method
A payment hold, also known as a payment authorization, is a pending charge on yourpayment method. Airbnb uses a payment hold for the full amount of the reservation at the time of booking to ensure that you have the funds or credit available to cover a payment.
When you book a reservation, we share the payment amount with your bank, credit card company, or other payment provider. We place a payment hold on your payment method to reserve this amount for Airbnb. If your reservation is accepted, the authorization will turn into a real charge up to 24 hours after your booking and the amount will be deducted from your payment account.
The payment hold is removed once a payment charge goes through. If youcancel a reservationafter booking and the actual payment has not occurred yet, Airbnb will remove the hold at the time of cancellation.
You might not notice when a payment hold is removed because it often happens behind the scenes. A payment hold may be replaced by the actual payment charge or it may be removed entirely when a reservation is canceled.
If your request is declined or expires, the authorization will disappear or appear as a refund in your payment account. Depending on the processing time of your payment provider, the release of the authorization may take up to 7 business days to reflect on their end.
Contact your payment provider if you have questions about how quickly a payment hold can be removed. An earlier release isn’t promised, but they may be able to help.

title: If a credit card or bank statement mentions an Airbnb plan fee
content:
If a credit card or bank statement mentions an Airbnb plan fee
If you notice a “plan fee" on your credit card or bank statement that seems like it’s connected to Airbnb, don't worry—it’s not an unexpected charge from Airbnb. Instead, it's a fee from your credit card company or bank when you choose to pay in installments.
Many credit cards and banks, such as Visa and Mastercard, offer installment plans to help spread out large purchases over time. When you opt into one of these plans, your statement may show the merchant’s name (ex: Airbnb) alongside the installment plan fee.
Examples of popular payment plans offered by major providers:
American Express Plan It
Citi Flex Pay
Chase Bank Pay Over Time
Apple Pay Later
Airbnb purchases will always start with “AIRBNB” followed by a confirmation code, and they may also be labeled as “Travel.”
Example:AIRBNB HMD2YW - Travel
If the description isn’t displayed like this, it’s not a direct charge from Airbnb.
Installment plan charges will appear differently, depending on the credit card or bank. It may be displayed as a charge in your account.
Example:PLAN FEE - AIRBNB HMD2YK - Fee and adjustments
Since this starts with “PLAN FEE,” it's a charge from your credit card or bank, not Airbnb. If you have any additional questions, contact your credit card provider or bank.

title: I disputed a charge from Airbnb with my bank, what happens next?
content:
I disputed a charge from Airbnb with my bank, what happens next?
When someone disputes a charge with their bank, a dispute process called a chargeback is initiated. Once a chargeback has been initiated, updates on the process will come from your bank, rather than Airbnb.
A chargeback is a formal payment dispute initiated by a consumer with their bank.
Chargebacks are usually filed if someone believes there’s an unauthorized, incorrect, or unexpected charge on their account. If you’ve filed a chargeback, you’ll need to contact your bank for more details on how their particular process works.
Once a chargeback has been filed, it can take up to 90 days for the bank to resolve it. You can check the status of this process, or cancel it, by working with your bank.
While the chargeback process is ongoing, your bank may temporarily deposit the disputed amount into your account—it’s important to note that this amount comes directly from your bank, not Airbnb. If it’s determined that the charge from Airbnb was valid, then these funds may be taken back by the bank.
When a chargeback is filed, Airbnb is no longer able to resolve the dispute with you directly, and any refund you receive will come from your bank, not from Airbnb.

title: Authenticate your credit or debit card when making a payment
content:
Authenticate your credit or debit card when making a payment
Sometimes your bank will need to verify it’s actually you making an online payment with your credit or debit card. This verification process uses the 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication framework. Like most online merchants, Airbnb has created a process in payment checkout to allow banks to perform 3DS authentication if they want to, and this appears as theConfirm your cardscreen after you select theConfirm and paybutton.
Be sure that you’ve updated your payment security settings with your credit card provider or bank, so that this authentication step happens the way that works best for you. Additionally, make sure your contact information with your credit card provider or bank is up-to-date.
Airbnb uses the 3DS framework to contact your card-issuing bank in order to check if they would like to authenticate the payment.
If the bank requires authentication, they will send over an authentication request that Airbnb will display for you to complete. This will pop up in theConfirm your cardprocess in the payment checkout screen.
Airbnb does not know or control what type of confirmation your bank will present, and it could vary based on the capabilities of the bank.
Once you complete the request as instructed by your bank in theConfirm your cardpop-up screen, Airbnb will attempt to charge your card.
If you fail or exit out of theConfirm your cardauthentication request, your bank will not allow your card to be charged and so the payment attempt will fail.
Banks and merchants all over the world may use 3DS for the security of their cardholders.
In Europe, a process calledStrong Customer Authentication, orSCA, allows banks to authenticate online payments made by their customers, and SCA uses the 3DS framework. This means that banks in many European countries will use 3DS for most online payments processed in Europe.
Strong Customer Authentication is designed to prevent unauthorized use of your card by confirming that you are the one making the payment.
You may see an alternate process asking you to use your device to confirm the payment, by taking the same steps you would typically use to unlock your device (ex: face recognition, fingerprint, or device passcode). This method is optional.
In this version of theConfirm your cardprocess, you may receive a message that “Google Chrome is trying to verify your identity on adyen.com”—this is expected.Using your device to confirm your card will not result in Airbnb or any partners collecting your biometric information, such as face or fingerprint images or your device passcode. In fact, that type of information will never leave your phone or device in this authentication process.
If you’re not getting One-Time Passcode texts from your bank when confirming your card, or are otherwise unable to complete the request from your bank:
Confirm your payment authentication settings and contact information with your credit card provider or bank.
If your bank security settings appear correct and further payment attempts still fail,  consider trying adifferent payment method.

title: Find your refund amount before or after canceling
content:
Find your refund amount before or after canceling
In most cases, your refund amount depends on your host’s cancellation policy and when you cancel. Find your refund amount before or aftercanceling your home reservationorcanceling your service or experience.
Find your refund amount before canceling on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to check
UnderReservation details, clickCancel reservation
Review for a detailed breakdown of your refund amount
Keep in mind:Your refund amount may change as your check-in date nears. If you don’t cancel right away, be sure to check the refund amount before canceling.
Find your refund amount after you've canceled on desktop
ClickMenu>Accountsettings>Payments
UnderYourpayments, clickManagepayments
Click your canceled trip (showingRefunded)
UnderRefundstatus, go toDetailsto review your refund amount
You can also review the cancellation confirmation email for your reservation to find any refund amount.
Eligible refunds are initiated by Airbnb as soon as you cancel the reservation, but how long it takes for you to receive the money depends on your bank or financial institution. Find the averagerefund timelines.
You may be entitled to a full refund or one larger than the standard refund of your host’s cancellation policy if:
You have to cancel because of amajor disruptive event
During your home stay, you’reexperiencing an issuecovered by theAirbnb Rebooking and Refund Policy

title: When you’ll get your refund
content:
When you’ll get your refund
If you’re eligible for a refund for ahome stayorservice or experience, we'll initiate your refund as soon as you cancel, but how long it takes for you to receive the money depends on your bank or financial institution. It’s based on how you paid, when you paid, and where you live.

You can track your refund a couple of ways. You can go toTrips, select the reservation and then selectTrack refund. Or you can:
Track your credit card refund on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Payments
ClickManage payments
Select the payment transaction to showRefund status
Contact your bank if you haven’t received your refund based on the refund timelines in this article. You’ll find your ARN (Acquirer Reference Number) in Refund status. Your ARN is a unique number that your bank can use to track your refund. Provide this number to your bank, and they can let you know when to expect your refund to deposit in your account.
If your original payment method’s account was closed, the refund sent by Airbnb won’t process. If this happens, you can contact your bank or financial institution to locate it. If you currently have an account with them, they may be able to transfer the refund to your new card or account. In some cases, they may send you a check for the refunded amount instead.
If you need the refund details to provide them, you can contact Airbnb—we can provide you with the details, including a reference number that your bank or financial institution can use.

title: How you'll get your refund
content:
How you'll get your refund
Find out how you’ll receive eligible refunds.
Refunds are issued to the original payment method that you used when you booked the reservation. In many cases, you’ll notice both a payment charge and a separate refund amount on your payment method.
If your original payment method’s account was closed, the refund sent by Airbnb won’t process. If this happens, you can contact your bank or financial institution to locate it. If you currently have an account with them, they may be able to transfer the refund to your new card or account. In some cases, they may send you a check for the refunded amount instead.
If you need the refund details to provide them, you can contact Airbnb—we can provide you with the details, including a reference number that your bank or financial institution can use.
If you cancel within the first 24 hours before the reservation is confirmed, we may not have charged your payment quite yet and only placed a hold on your payment method. In this case, we’ll simply remove the payment hold, which is often an invisible backend process. However, if your reservation has already been accepted, the refund will be subject to thehost's cancellation policy.
If you cancel a reservation in which you used a coupon to pay, the coupon can’t be used again.
If your host cancels a reservation in which you used a coupon to pay, your coupon code will remain active and can be used toward another reservation. Find out more aboutwhat happens to the coupon you used if your host cancels.
Refunds for booking credit are subject to your host’s cancellation policy and may be refunded as credit that can be used again. Read more abouthow booking credit works.
Eligible refunds will be initiated by Airbnb as soon as you cancel the reservation, but how long it takes for you to receive the money depends on your bank or financial institution. Find the averagerefund timelines.

title: Canceling during your stay
content:
Canceling during your stay
If anything unexpected comes up during your stay, try contacting your host to discuss a solution first. It’s likely that they’ll help you fix it right away. If you’d like to request a refund or cancel your reservation, we can help you start a request with your host.
You canmessage your host directlyto discuss a solution. If you come to an agreement with your host, there’s a higher chance your request will be approved.
You’ll need to start a request to cancel your reservation within 72 hours of finding the problem to be eligible for a refund.
You can:
Ask your host to fix the issue
Request a partial refund
Request to cancel for a full refund
If your host says yes, your refund will go back to your original payment method. If your host doesn’t reply or says no, you can ask Airbnb for help
Gather proof: Take photos or videos of the problem (for example, broken amenities).
Submit a request to your host: Explain the issue, add photos, and say how you want to resolve it.
Wait for a reply: If the host doesn’t help, ask Airbnb to step in. We’ll review ourRebooking and refund policy for homesto help resolve the issue.
In the rare circumstances that a large-scale event at your destination prevents completion of your reservation, you may be eligible for a refund under ourMajor Disruptive Events Policy.

title: Request a partial or full refund from your home host
content:
Request a partial or full refund from your home host
There may be a time when a guest would like to request a refund from their home host. That’s why we have theResolution Center—once a host and guest reach an agreement, they can request and send money between one another.
Just a note, the Resolution Center may not be available for use with some hotel stays.
You may encounter a minor issue during your home stay that might warrant a request for a refund. Maybe your host can’t fix it, but they’ve agreed to give you a small refund to make up for the inconvenience.
You can check if the problem or issue may be covered under ourrebooking and refund policy for homesor find outwhat to do if you’re experiencing an issue during your stay.
Alternatively, there may be a personal situation that arises prompting you to discuss with your home host the possibility of a partial or full refund.
Keep in mind: Issues that aren’t covered under ourMajor Disruptive Events Policyor our refund policies are still subject to the host’s cancellation policy. A refund outside of their cancellation policy is at the discretion of the host.
First, communicate any issues directly bymessagingyour host. This initial step often helps with a smoother resolution and mutual understanding between you and your host.
If you and your host aren’t able to come to an agreement, you'll have the option to ask Airbnb for help finding a solution when you submit a request in the Resolution Center.
Request a refund on desktop
ClickResolution Centerand then clickRequest money
Select your reservation and clickNext
UnderSelect a reason, choose the reason that best describes your refund situation
Upload optional attachments
Enter the refund amount and add a note
ClickConfirmand thenRequest
Note:You have up to 60 days after your reservation's checkout date to submit a Resolution Center request.
If you need to cancel a reservation, you may be eligible for a refund—it all depends on your host’s cancellation policy and when you cancel the reservation.
You can learn how tofind your refund amountbefore or after canceling your reservation. If you’re ready to cancel, here’s how to cancel yourhome reservation. You can also find outwhen you’ll get your refund.

title: Expedite an authorization void or refund
content:
Expedite an authorization void or refund
Unfortunately, this one’s out of our hands. It all depends onyour payment method’s processing time.

title: Canceling a service or experience for a refund
content:
Canceling a service or experience for a refund
Did you book a service or experience and now you can’t make it? If you cancel within the cancellation period, you’ll receive a full refund.
You can find info about your reservation in yourTrips(or reservation confirmation email). The cancellation policy is located underReservation details.
Missed the deadline for a free cancellation? Your host may still be willing to provide a refund. You can request an exception to your host’s cancellation policy by clicking or tappingCancel reservation, choose the reason and then clickRequest an exceptionto find out if they'd be willing to refund you. The host has 24 hours to respond to the request.
Learn more aboutservice or experience cancellation policies.
Coupons are non-refundable. If you cancel a reservation in which you used a coupon to pay, the coupon can’t be used again. Find out more abouthow coupons work.
Refunds for booking credit are subject to your host’s cancellation policy. Depending on when you cancel and the cancellation policy that applies to your reservation, you may get a refund of the booking credit that will be made available on your Airbnb account to use later.

title: Refunds in India
content:
Refunds in India
Are you expecting a refund due to a cancellation in India? Just be aware local credit card policies may affect when and how you get your refund.
Indian credit cards require two-factor authentication, so guests residing in India are charged for the full cost of the reservation when they submit a trip request. Once you’ve booked, we’re unable to change your payment method.
If the Host doesn’t accept your request or it expires, you’ll be refunded in full. We'll initiate the refund immediately, and you'll get it as soon as your bank or payment method provider has processed the refund.
Some banks and payment providers can take up to 15 business days to complete processing. If your refund hasn’t come through yet, it's probably on its way.
If you submitted multiple trip requests for the same dates and they were confirmed, the reservations aren’t automatically eligible for a refund. They’re still subject to the Host’s cancellation policy.
You can learnhow to change the dates for a reservationor you can find outhow to ask your Host if they’d be willing to give you a refund, outside of their cancellation policy.

title: What's refunded when you cancel a home reservation
content:
What's refunded when you cancel a home reservation
You were all set to travel and now something’s come up. Find out what can be refunded—it all depends on yourhost’s cancellation policyand when you cancel the reservation.
To quickly find out what your refund amount would be if you cancel your reservation now, follow the steps tofind out your refund amount before canceling.
Refunds are subject to your host’s cancellation policy. Depending on the length of your stay, how soon your reservation starts, the nights you’ve stayed, and the cancellation policy that applies to your reservation, you may get a full refund, a partial refund, or no refund if you cancel.
Note: If you’re canceling because youexperienced an issue during your stay, you may be eligible for a refund, regardless of your host’s cancellation policy.
Service fees are refunded if you cancel during the free cancellation period for your reservation. Find out more aboutservice fee refunds.
If you’ve only paid for part of your stay up front, your refund amount may be less than you expect, and will never exceed what you paid.
Learn more abouthow refunds work for reservations paid for with Klarna.
Coupons are non-refundable. If you cancel a reservation in which you used a coupon to pay, the coupon can’t be used again. Find out more abouthow coupons work.
Refunds for booking credit are subject to your host’s cancellation policy. Depending on when you cancel and the cancellation policy that applies to your reservation, you may get a refund of the booking credit that will be made available on your Airbnb account to use later.
If a hotel or other professional host collects property fees on-site, any refund of those fees is at the discretion of the host.
If you and your host come to an agreement that you’ll receive a refund of any amount, you can find outhow to request a refund in the Resolution Center.
You may be entitled to a full refund or partial refund outside of your host’s cancellation policy if:
You have to cancel because of amajor disruptive event
You have to cancel because of a reservation issue covered by therebooking and refund policy for homes

title: Changing a reservation paid for with Klarna
content:
Changing a reservation paid for with Klarna
With aKlarna payment plan, you can still make reservation changes, like changing the arrival and departure dates, or adding extra guests. However, you’ll need tosubmit a trip change requestto your host on Airbnb—Klarna can’t change reservations in any way.
Need to cancel? Find outhow canceling a reservation paid for using Klarna works.
To change a confirmed reservation, the first step is tosubmit a change requestto your host on Airbnb. If there's a difference in cost for the reservation, you can compare the original total and the new total before you submit your request.
If the host accepts the request, the change is processed.
Note: Klarna can’t change reservations in any way, and hosts don’t have any information on any Klarna payment plans that you have chosen to cover the cost of the trip.
Even with a Klarna payment plan, you can change your booking (for example, you can change your reservation dates or add extra guests). You can make these changes directly through Airbnb.
If the new reservation costs less after a change request, Airbnb will send the refund to Klarna for the difference. This refund may take up to 14 business days to reflect in your Klarna account.
If the new reservation change costs more than the original cost, you can use an existing payment method in your Airbnb account oradd a new payment methodto cover the difference. Additionally, you may be able to apply for a new payment plan with Klarna for an additional amount.
To check your refund status on desktop
ClickTrips
UnderCanceled trips, click the trip you want to check
ClickTrack refund
UnderRefund status, review your refund amount
Airbnb will send the refund to Klarna for the difference. This refund may take between 5-7 business days to reflect in your Klarna account.
However, once we send the refund to Klarna, we can’t provide additional information on the Klarna refund status. For any details of refunds issued by Klarna, contact theircustomer service.
If you’ve completed all scheduled installments with Klarna, then Klarna would issue the refund to your original method of payment used with Klarna. If your payment plan is still active, then Klarna would issue refunds as a balance to adjust to the remainder of their scheduled installments.
In the case of a significant delay in receiving the amount due from Klarna, you'll need to check the status with Klarna directly. If after 14 days you have not received a refund, contactKlarna's customer service.
The refund amount refers to the full or partial cost of your reservation and doesn't include any fees or interest included in a Klarna payment plan.
Paying in 3 or 4 payments with Klarna loans are interest-free, but fees may be applied by Klarna for late payments. If you pay monthly with Klarna, interest may be applied.
Once a booking is confirmed and you have set up a Klarna payment plan, if you are unable to make a payment to Klarna on time, Klarna may try again. If that payment attempt doesn’t work, the missed payment will be added to the amount of the next scheduled payment, and late fees may apply.
Learn more aboutwhat happens if you can’t pay on time using Klarna.
Note: If you are late with payment to Klarna, this information won’t be shared with Airbnb and has no effect on your Airbnb profile or personal information. All financial agreements with Klarna are strictly between Klarna and you.
