title: Refund Policy for Services and Experiences
content:
Refund Policy for Services and Experiences
Effective date: May 13, 2025
This policy describes when guests may be eligible for refunds for services and experiences. It also explains how guests can request refunds and how we will handle them.
If a guest’s service or experience is disrupted by a Covered Issue, then the guest is eligible for a full or partial refund. The term “Covered Issue” means any of the following:
A host cancels a service or experience, except when a host cancels because of a disruption caused by the guest (ex: the guest refuses to follow safety instructions)
A host is more than 15 minutes late for the service or experience, or does not arrive for the service or experience at all
The delivered service or experience differs substantially from what was advertised or agreed to with the guest (ex: the host provides an inaccurate starting location information or fails to deliver key aspects of what was promised)
A host is not prepared to host the service or experience (ex: inappropriate venue, missing essential equipment, or failing equipment)
A host damages a venue provided by the guest
If a host cancels a reservation, their guest automatically receives a refund, unless the cancellation is due to a disruption by the guest. In these cases, the guest will not be entitled to a refund.
For any Covered Issue other than a host cancellation, the guest should, whenever feasible, try to resolve the Covered Issue directly with their host. Guests may request refunds directly from hosts using theResolution Center.
For any issue that is not resolved directly with the host, the guest who made the reservation may submit a request bycontacting us. The request must be submitted no later than 72 hours after the Covered Issue occurs. And the request must be supported by relevant evidence such as photographs, communications between the host and guest, or other available documentation, which we will use to help determine whether a Covered Issue has occurred. If a guest demonstrates that reporting a Covered Issue within 72 hours was not feasible, we may allow for late reporting under this policy.
If a host cancels a reservation for any reason other than a disruption caused by the guest, the guest will automatically receive a full refund. If we determine that a Covered Issue other than a cancellation has disrupted a service or experience, we will provide a full or partial refund. How much we refund depends on the severity of the Covered Issue and the extent of the impact on the guest.
If a host has already provided a partial refund in response to a guest request, we may reduce the amount of any further refund under this policy to reflect what the host has already paid to the guest.
In most circumstances, we will attempt to confirm a guest’s reported concern by reaching out to their host. Hosts can also dispute a guest’s assertion of a Covered Issue bycontacting usor responding to our correspondence.
If a host cancels a reservation or is responsible for any other Covered Issue that disrupts a service or experience, the host may either receive no payout or may have their payout reduced by the amount of the refund to their guest.
This policy applies to all reservations made on or after the Effective Date and is applicable to the maximum extent permitted by law, which may imply guarantees that cannot be excluded. When this policy applies, it controls and takes precedence over the reservation’s cancellation policy. Covered Issues that are caused by the requesting guest are not covered by this policy. Submitting a fraudulent report violates ourTerms of Serviceand may result in account termination.
Our decisions under this policy are binding but do not affect other contractual or statutory rights that may be available. Any right that guests or hosts may have to initiate legal action remains unaffected. This policy is not insurance and no premium has been paid by any guest or host. All rights and obligations under this policy are personal to the booking guest and host of the reservation and may not be transferred or assigned. Any changes to this policy will be made in accordance with ourTerms of Service. This policy applies to services and experiences but does not apply tohomereservations.

title: Request or send money in the Resolution Center
content:
Request or send money in the Resolution Center
A host sending their guest a partial refund for a missing amenity, a guest sending their host a payment for breaking a coffee mug, and much more—the Resolution Center makes it easy to handle money-related requests for an Airbnb home, service, or experience.
Just keep in mind that:
You must use Airbnb’s Change reservation tool for reservation alterations. You can’t use the Resolution Center to collect payment for additional costs or provide refunds to nights, guest count, or pet count.
With few exceptions, hosts cannot charge guests for mandatory fees through the Resolution Center. Exceptions include:Select software-connected hoststhat may collect payment for mandatory fees and security deposits separately in the Resolution Center. These fees would be detailed in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout.In locations where Airbnb does not collect taxes or where hosts are legally required to collect them directly from guests, hosts must disclose taxes in the listing description and may collect them over the Resolution Center.
Select software-connected hoststhat may collect payment for mandatory fees and security deposits separately in the Resolution Center. These fees would be detailed in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout.
In locations where Airbnb does not collect taxes or where hosts are legally required to collect them directly from guests, hosts must disclose taxes in the listing description and may collect them over the Resolution Center.
Learn more about what theResolution Center can and cannot be used for.
Request money from your host or guest on desktop
ClickResolution Centerand then clickRequest money
Select the reservation and clickChoose
UnderWhat is this request for?, choose the reason that best describes why you’re requesting money
Enter the amount you’re requesting
Upload optional attachments and add a note
ClickNextand thenRequest
Send money on desktop
ClickResolution Centerand then clickSend money
Select the reservation and clickChoose
UnderWhat is this payment for?, choose the reason that best describes why you’re sending money
Enter the payment amount and add a note
ClickNext
Choose a payment method and then clickConfirm and pay
You can only submit a new Resolution Center request up to 60 days after a completed reservation (except for damage-related claims, which need to be filed 14 days within checkout).
You can respond to an existing Resolution Center request—like choosing to pay the full amount, paying a different amount, or declining the request—more than 60 days after checkout, as long as the request was submitted within the 60 days after completion.
Hosts can use the Resolution Center to request money from a guest before their confirmed reservation starts, but they can't send money to a guest until the reservation has started or has been canceled. Guests can send money to a host before their reservation starts, but they can’t request money from a host until their reservation has started.
If a guest cancels their reservation before it starts and their host has agreed to a full refund, either the guest or host can initiate it:
The guest can request a full refund from the host in the Resolution Center
The host can contact Airbnb to have our Community Support team refund the guest in full
Hosts and guests can both use the Resolution Center to send or request money from one another after the reservation starts.
If a guest cancels their reservation and it’s past the start date or time, their host can send a full refund to the guest in the Resolution Center.
The Resolution Center may not be available for use with some hotel stays. In those cases, guests of hotel stays and their hosts will need to resolve any money-related requests directly.

title: Airbnb service fees
content:
Airbnb service fees
To help Airbnb run smoothly and to cover the cost of services like 24/7 customer support, we charge a service fee when a booking is confirmed.
There are 2 different fee structures for stays: a split fee and single fee.
1. Split-fee
This fee structure is the most common and is split between the host and the guest.
Host service fee
Most hosts pay a 3% service fee, but some pay more, including some hosts with listings in Italy and Brazil. Hosts with listings in Brazil pay a 4% fee. The fee is automatically deducted from the total price (service fees are a percentage of the nightly price and anyfees charged by the host, but exclude the guest service fee and taxes) to calculate the host payout.
Find the host service fee on desktop
ClickToday>Menu>Earnings
Select the reservation code
UnderHost payout, find theHost service fee
Guest service fee
The guest service fee can be found in thePrice breakdownbeforebooking a reservation.
Guests pay a service fee ranging from 14.1% to 16.5% of the booking subtotal (the booking subtotal includes the nightly price and any additional fees charged by the host, but excludes the guest service fee and taxes). The guest service fee varies based on a variety of factors and may be higher or lower depending on the booking. The guest service fee is reduced for some stays of 28 nights or more.
For bookings where the guest pays using a different currency from the one set by the host for their listing, we adjust our fees to align with the value we provide our guests. For these cross-currency bookings, the guest service fee will include an additional amount, resulting in a guest service fee of up to 16.5% of the booking subtotal.
With this structure, the entire fee is deducted from the host payout. It’s typically 14–16%, though hosts with Super Strict cancellation policies may pay an additional 2%, and fees for stays of 28 nights or more may be less.
This fee structure is mandatory for traditional hospitality listings–such as hotels, serviced apartments, etc., as well ashosts who use property management software.
Depending on the laws of the jurisdiction involved,VATmay be applicable to the above fees. The service fee is VAT inclusive, where applicable.
We reserve the right to change our service fees at any time, and any changes will be made in accordance with our Terms of Service.
For service reservations, we typically charge a 15% fee, with a minimum fee of $6 USD (or the local currency equivalent), which may vary based on the daily exchange rate. For experience reservations, we typically charge a 20% service fee.

title: How pricing works for homes
content:
How pricing works for homes
The total price of your Airbnb reservation is based on the nightly price set by the host, plus fees or costs determined by either the host or Airbnb. In addition to this, listings in some geographical locations may include taxes as well.
Airbnb service fee:Guestservice feecharged by Airbnb—this provides 24/7 community support and helps everything run smoothly.
Cleaning fee:Charged by some hosts to cover the cost of cleaning their space after the stay.
Extra guest fee:Charged by some hosts for each additional guest beyond a set number.
Pet fee:Some hosts allow pets to stay in their listings for an additional charge which may be separate from cleaning fees.
Security deposit:Select software-connected hosts may set a security deposit using the appropriate fee field. Learn more aboutsecurity deposits.
Value Added Tax (VAT, JCT, and GST):Charged to guests who live in certain countries—find outmore about VAT.
Local taxes:Charged based on the location of the Host's place—find out more aboutlocal taxes.
You’ll be charged once a host accepts your reservation request, or immediately if you useInstant Book. You may be able tosplit the cost into multiple paymentsif your reservation meets specific criteria, or you can pay the full amount in one go.

title: Service fee refunds for home stays
content:
Service fee refunds for home stays
If you need to cancel your reservation for a home, you may be able to receive a refund on your entire reservation, including the service fee. So how does it work?
The service fee is refundable if you cancel before your reservation’s free cancellation period ends or if your host decides to refund you in full after you cancel.
Check your reservation’scancellation policyto find out if you’re within the free cancellation period and will be refunded the service fee if you cancel.
For reservations in Italy and for South Korean travelers in some cases, the service fee may be refundable if you cancel any time before check-in.
When it comes to things like nightly rates and the cleaning fee, you may be eligible for a refund. Find out more aboutwhat can be refunded.

title: Price changes based on dates
content:
Price changes based on dates
Just as planning a trip in July differs greatly from one in February, so can the price. Here’s howchoosing different datescan affect the nightly prices for a place.
To find the most accurate price for your trip, add dates whensearching for a place. Some Hosts have custom prices that override the default or minimum price for specific dates or time periods (including holidays, weekends, and weekly or monthly reservations).
If you submitted atrip change requestto your Host and they accepted, your reservation will be updated to reflect the changes at your Host’s current daily rate. As with any hotel or B&B, your Host may have raised (or even lowered, if you’re lucky) the prices since you originally booked your reservation.

title: Why some prices are crossed out
content:
Why some prices are crossed out
Good news! You’re being offered a great deal. We only cross out a price when the one being offered is at least 10% lower than usual for a particular night. Who doesn’t love knowing they’re on to a good thing?
Weekly or monthly discount: Hosts sometimesoffer discountson the nightly price for stays that are 7 nights or longer.
Early bird discount: Hosts can offer a discount on thenightly pricewhen guests book a certain number of days prior to their trip. For example, a Host might set a 15% discount for stays booked at least 2 months in advance.
Price drop: Hosts lower their price for specific dates that they choose.
Non-refundable discount: Places may offer a discount when you choose to book with thenon-refundable option.
We track all Host’s prices over time and then work out the median price over the previous 60 days. If a Host drops their price by 10% or more below the median for a particular date, we highlight the saving by crossing out the original price.
Let’s say you search for a night in June. We can see that over the last 60 days, the Host has changed the price for that night on 4 different occasions. We calculate the median price for this night by adding the middle 2 prices together, and dividing them by 2.
Price 1: $90Price 2: $100Price 3: $125Price 4: $140
Median price:($100 + $125) / 2 = $112.50
Next, we compare this median price to the current price of the night you searched for. If the current price is at least 10% less than the median ($112.50 in this example), we show you the median price crossed out, next to the discounted price.
Ex:$112.50$100

title: Collecting fees outside Airbnb
content:
Collecting fees outside Airbnb
Hosts are not permitted to collect anyfees related to Airbnb reservations outside of our platform, unless expressly authorized by us.
In limited cases, Airbnb might permitselect software-connected hoststo collect certain mandatory fees using a payment method outside of Airbnb⁠—as long as they’re included in the listing’s price breakdown at checkout. Examples of these fees include: resort fees (including the cost of amenities like pool, gym, or wifi), utility fees, and HOA fees.
Hotels may also collect payment off of the Airbnb platform for optional fees where it is in the course of normal business practices (ex: parking). Other hosts must collect payment for optional fees using theResolution Center.
In locations where Airbnb does not collect taxes or where hosts are legally required to collect them directly from guests, hosts may collect disclosed taxes off of Airbnb.
Most hosts are not allowed to charge security deposits. To address damage or accidents that happen during a stay, Airbnb offers top-to-bottom protection throughAirCover for Hosts.
For the small number of listings wheresecurity depositsare permitted to be collected outside of the Airbnb platform, hosts must disclose them in the appropriate fee field.
Hotels can also ask for a credit card or cash deposit at check-in to cover the cost of incidentals as part of their standard procedures, but this must be disclosed in the listing description.

title: Cleaning fees
content:
Cleaning fees
Who doesn’t like arriving into a clean and tidy space? A cleaning fee is a one-off charge that’s set by the host for cleaning a home. The cleaning fee is part of thetotal price.
If you cancel during the free cancellation period (if applicable) for your reservation, the full cleaning fee will be returned as part of your refund.  If you cancel outside of the free cancellation period for your reservation, the cleaning fee is considered part of the nightly price and any refund of the cleaning fee will be based on the host’s cancellation policy.
For any reservations booked prior to April 21, 2025, the cleaning fee will be returned in full if you cancel before check-in.

title: How to add and redeem coupons
content:
How to add and redeem coupons
Make sure you apply the coupon before you book, and keep in mind that if you cancel a reservation, the coupon can’t be used again.
Add a coupon on desktop
1. ClickMenu>Account settings>Payments2. UnderPayments, clickCoupons3. ClickAdd coupon4. Enter your coupon code and clickRedeem coupon
Redeem a coupon on desktop
If you have a coupon applied to your account, clickEntera couponon the checkout page
Select the coupon
ClickApply
You can’t apply a coupon after you book:Even if you contact Airbnb Support, we can’t apply a current or expired coupon after you’ve booked a reservation (future or past).
If you cancel, coupons are not refundable:Refunds are based on the host’s cancellation policy, and we can’t restore or reissue a coupon you redeemed in the past.
Expiration dates can’t be adjusted:Once a coupon has expired, it’s not possible to extend the expiration date or to get a replacement coupon. Expired coupons can't be used toward past reservations.
Limit one coupon per reservation:It’s not possible to apply multiple coupons to a reservation.

title: What should I do if my coupon code isn’t working?
content:
What should I do if my coupon code isn’t working?
Double-check to make sure you’re using the right code. If it’s still not working, keep these things in mind:
If a coupon has expired, there’s no way to reactivate it.
Only one coupon can be used per reservation.
A coupon can only be used once. If you cancel a reservation that was booked using a coupon, the coupon is no longer valid.
For reservations of 28 nights or more, of if you choose a payment plan when you book, a coupon will reduce the total price and the amount will be split across payments.
Only coupons issued by Airbnb are accepted.
If you use less than the full amount of a coupon, any unused discount amount will be forfeited.
Coupons can't be applied to a reservation you made in the past.
Only the person who received a coupon can use it on Airbnb. There’s no way to transfer a coupon to someone else.
Coupons can only be used for the type of booking they were issued for. For example, if a coupon can only be used toward a stay, then it can't be used toward an experience, or for anything else on Airbnb.
If a reservation booked with a coupon is altered later on, you'll need to pay the difference with your payment method, or, if the alteration results in a refund, we'll credit your payment method for the difference. However, you won't receive a refund for the coupon amount, and the coupon won't be able to be used again.
A coupon may have specific limitations; see the Terms & Conditions distributed with the coupon for details.

title: What happens to the coupon you used if your host cancels
content:
What happens to the coupon you used if your host cancels
If your host cancels your home, service, or experience reservation and you used a coupon, here’s what you need to know.
If your host cancels your reservation before check-in or start time, the coupon code you used will become active again
You can use the same coupon for another reservation, as long as it is still within the original expiration date
If the coupon has already expired, it cannot be used again
Sometimes, you may receive a new coupon due to the cancellation
You can only apply one coupon per reservation
If you want to use your original coupon instead of the new one, you may need to remove the new coupon on the checkout page before applying the original

title: Airbnb Coupon Terms
content:
Airbnb Coupon Terms
The following Terms and Conditions apply to coupons for use on the Airbnb platform (“Airbnb Coupons''). Other supplemental terms provided, along with a coupon, also apply to your use of Airbnb Coupons. Airbnb Coupons are one-time use and non-refundable. Coupons are not redeemable for cash, gift cards, or toward previous purchases, and cannot be transferred or combined with another offer, coupon, or discount. Airbnb reserves the right to any remedy, including voiding this coupon, in cases of suspected fraud, misuse, violation of ourTerms of Service,Payments Terms of Service, or technical errors. Please refer below for additional terms for specific Rebooking Coupons.
Rebooking Coupons
As part of AirCover, when a Host cancels your reservation unexpectedly oranother Travel Issuedisrupts a stay, Airbnb may issue you a Rebooking Coupon. The coupon is only valid for a stay with a check-in date within one year from the date the coupon was issued.
Rebooking Coupons expire 30 days from issuance. For more information, refer to your communication from Airbnb on the expiration date of your coupon. Rebooking Coupons may only be used toward a stay reservation with a reservation value (not including taxes or fees) that is equal to or greater than the value of the Rebooking Coupon.

title: Airbnb Guest Referral Program
content:
Airbnb Guest Referral Program
The Airbnb Guest Referral Program lets you refer your friends and family to explore and use Airbnb, and  you and your friends can earn rewards. Here's how it works:
Refer Friends:Share your unique referral link with your friends and family.
Register:Your friends sign up for Airbnb using your link.
Earn Rewards:Your friends get a coupon for their first stay, and you earn travel credits when they complete their first trip.
By participating, you agree to theAirbnb Guest Referral Program Terms and Conditions.
As a referrer, you must meet the following requirements:
Have completed at least one booking on Airbnb.
Have completed Airbnb’s identity verification process.
Be a resident of Brazil, mainland China, Germany, India, Italy, Japan, Mexico, or South Korea.
Have no records of fraud or other misconduct on Airbnb.
The friends you refer must meet the following requirements:
Not have an existing Airbnb account.
Register for a new Airbnb account using the invitation link.
Be a resident of Brazil, mainland China, Germany, India, Italy, Japan, Mexico, or South Korea.
Not be the same user as the Referrer and have no records of fraud or other misconduct on Airbnb.
You can refer as many friends as you like, but you can only earn rewards for up to 10 successful referrals per calendar year. Even after reaching this limit, your friends can still register and receive their coupon.
Your referral link is valid for 72 hours after it’s generated. If your friend doesn’t use it within that time, the link will expire, and you’ll need to send a new link.
Your friends will receive an Airbnb coupon for their first stay. They can view the coupon details, including amount and usage rules, in their account under “Profile > Payments & Payouts > Credits & Coupons.”
A successful referral happens when your friend:
Makes a stay booking that meets or exceeds the minimum amount specified for their country within 180 days of creating an account.
Completes their stay (the booking must not be canceled).
When your friend completes a successful referral, you’ll earn travel credits. These credits:
Are valid for 1 year from the date they’re issued.
Can be used for future homes, experiences, or services bookings on Airbnb.
Are automatically applied to bookings, but you can manage them on the payment page.
Can be stacked with other discounts, but not with coupons from the Guest Referral Program or gift cards.
Cannot exceed 15% of the total payment amount per booking.
There could be several reasons:
Eligible countries:The friends you referred may not be in one of the eligible countries, which are Brazil, mainland China, Germany, India, Italy, Japan, Mexico, and South Korea.
Non-qualifying first stay:Their first stays might not meet the Qualifying Booking criteria. A Qualifying Booking must:
Reach a specified minimum amount.
Be made within 180 days of creating an account.
Be accepted by a Host, paid for by your friends themselves, and completed without any cancellations.
Maximum Referrals Reached:You may have already reached the maximum limit of 10 Successful Referrals per calendar year.
When will I receive my Referral Rewards?
Referral Rewards are automatically sent to your account, typically within ten days after your friends’ qualifying trip is completed.
Where can I check  my Referral Rewards?
You can view your Referral Rewards balance and usage details under “Profile > Payments & Payouts > Credits & Coupons.”
What if I need to cancel or change a booking that used Referral Rewards?
If you cancel a booking, the system will first return the Referral Rewards to your account. Depending on the booking’s cancellation policy, the Rewards may not be fully refundable.
Referral links should be shared only with personal connections, like friends and family. Avoid posting your link on public forums or coupon sites.
If you believe there is an error in your Referral travel credit balance, please contact us. Airbnb may require you to submit relevant proof to confirm the balance.
Tax liabilities arising from receiving or using Referral travel credits are your sole responsibility. Similarly, tax liabilities from receiving or using an Airbnb coupon are the responsibility of the Referred User. Airbnb may collect taxes on services redeemed using Referral Rewards and/or coupons where applicable.
Airbnb may change or end the Guest Referral Program at any time without prior notice. However, any rewards or coupons already issued will still be valid according to the terms in place when they were issued

title: Host referrals overview
content:
Host referrals overview
Airbnb maintains referral, Superhost Ambassador, and similar programs that allow hosts to earn payments or other rewards when they, or the people they refer, take certain actions, such as completing their first stay on Airbnb. Some programs have specific requirements, which, if not set out in these Referral Terms, are included on their respective program pages.
Host referrals:By participating you agree to theHost Referral Offer and Program Terms, which you can read in the Help Center. The rules and limitations set out in this article also apply to the program.Co-host Network referrals:By participating you agree to theHost Referral Offer and Program Terms, which you can read in the Help Center. The rules and limitations set out in this article also apply to the program.
Ambassador referrals:By participating you agree to theHost Referral Offer and Program Terms, which you can read in the Help Center. The rules and limitations set out in this article also apply to the programAirbnb-provided referrals:By participating you agree to theSuperhost Ambassador Program Terms and Conditions, which you can read in the Help Center. The rules and limitations set out in this article also apply to the program.
You can refer individuals to list their home, experience, or service on Airbnb.
Home referrals
You can refer almost anyone who’s new to hosting on Airbnb and has never published a listing as the listing owner, even if they already have an Airbnb account. They’ll have to host an entire place in aneligible locationand complete aqualifying bookingfor you to receive a reward.
Experience referrals
Airbnb looks for hosts who can provide expertise, insider access, and connection.  And they can provide a wide range of experiences. Here are a few examples.
Food & drink:Food tours, cooking classes, tastings, dining experiences
History & culture:Landmark tours, museum tours, or cultural tours
Fitness & wellness:Workouts, wellness, and beauty experiences
Nature & Outdoors:Water sports, outdoor experiences, flying experiences, and animal experiences
Art & Design:Gallery tours, art workshops, architecture tours, shopping & fashion experiences
Learn more aboutAirbnb Experiences Standards & Requirements.
The experience will have to be approved and published in aneligible locationand they must complete a qualifying booking for you to receive a reward.
Service referrals
Airbnb is for more than homes. Now it’s for anyone that offers high-quality services that make a guest’s stay even better.  These include:
Private chefs
Catering
Prepared meals
Photography
Massage
Spa treatments
Personal training
Hair styling
Nails
Makeup
The service will have to be approved and published, and a qualifying booking must be completed in aneligible locationfor you to receive a reward.
Host referrals:You can refer almost anyone who’s new to hosting on Airbnb and has never published a listing, even if they already have an Airbnb account. They’ll have tohost an entire placeor host a service or experience in aneligible locationand complete a qualifying booking for you to earn a reward. Shared and private rooms aren’t eligible. Home listings created by referred hosts who useAPI-connected softwareare not eligible. Other exceptions include, but are not limited to, anyone who previously received a referral invitation, previously published a listing, or people that live in certain ineligible locations.Co-host Network referrals:You can refer almost anyone who’s new to hosting on Airbnb and has never published a listing, even if they already have an Airbnb account. They’ll have tohost an entire placein aneligible locationand complete a qualifying booking for you to earn a reward. Shared and private rooms aren’t eligible. Home listings created by referred hosts who useAPI-connected softwareare not eligible. Other exceptions include, but are not limited to, anyone who previously received a referral invitation, previously published a listing, or people that live in certain ineligible locations.
Ambassador referrals:You can refer almost anyone who’s new to hosting on Airbnb and has never published a listing, even if they already have an Airbnb account. They’ll have tohost an entire placein aneligible locationand complete a qualifying booking for you to earn a reward. Shared rooms aren’t eligible. Home listings created by referred hosts who useAPI-connected softwareare not eligible. Other exceptions include, but are not limited to, anyone who previously received a referral invitation, or people that live in certain ineligible locations.
Airbnb-provided referrals:Not applicable.
Rewards vary by listing location, type, and size, and are only available in eligible locations. Check thelist of eligible locations here. Referral reward amounts and eligible locations are subject to change at any time. Reward amounts apply to listings created via your referral link until the expiration date stated on the referral product page. The new host must complete a qualifying booking within 180 days.Terms apply. Amounts displayed on the referral creation page apply to listings in your country.
Find thesteps to submit your host referralin the Airbnb app.
Home host referrals:About 14 days after your referral completes their first qualifying booking, we’ll send your reward directly to your account. Depending on your payout method, it can take another 1–7 days for the funds to arrive in your account.
Experience host referrals:About 14 days after your referral completes their first qualifying booking, we’ll send your reward directly to your account. Depending on your payout method, it can take another 1–7 days for the funds to arrive in your account.Service host referrals:About 14 days after your referral completes their first qualifying booking, we’ll send your reward directly to your account. Depending on your payout method, it can take another 1–7 days for the funds to arrive in your account.
Co-host Network referrals:About 14 days after your referral completes their first qualifying booking, we’ll send your reward directly to your account. Depending on your payout method, it can take another 1–7 days for the funds to arrive in your account.
Ambassador referrals:About 14 days after your referral completes their first qualifying booking, we’ll send your reward directly to your account. Depending on your payout method, it can take another 1–7 days for the funds to arrive in your account.Airbnb-provided referrals:About 14 days after the person you are matched with completes their first qualifying booking, we’ll send your reward directly to your account. Depending on your payout method, it can take another 1–7 days for the funds to arrive in your account.
Home host referrals:There is no limit to how many referrals you can make to your network, but you can only earn a cash reward for a maximum of 25 referrals.Experience host referrals:There is no limit to how many referrals you can make to your network, but you can only earn a cash reward for a maximum of 25 referrals.
Service host referrals:There is no limit to how many referrals you can make to your network, but you can only earn a cash reward for a maximum of 25 referrals.
Co-host Network referrals:There is no limit to how many referrals you can make to your network, but you can only earn a cash reward for 2,000 referrals.
Ambassador referrals:There is no limit to how many referrals you can make to your network, but you can only earn a cash reward for 2,000 referrals.Airbnb-provided referrals:Not applicable.
Home host referrals:To qualify for your cash reward, the person you refer needs to complete their first reservation—in other words, their first guest needs to complete their stay and check out—within 180 days of acting on the referral by clicking “Create your listing” from your unique link.
Co-host Network referrals:To qualify for your cash reward, the person you refer needs to complete their first reservation—in other words, their first guest needs to complete their stay and check out—within 180 days of acting on the referral by clicking “Send message” from your unique Co-host Network referral link.
Experience host referrals:To qualify for your cash reward, the person you refer needs to complete their first reservation-- in other words, their first guest needs to complete their experience--- within 180 days of acting on the referral by clicking “Create your experience” from your unique link.Service host referrals:To qualify for your cash reward, the person you refer needs to complete their first reservation-- in other words, their first guest needs to complete their service--- within 180 days of acting on the referral by clicking “Create your service” from your unique link.
Ambassador referrals:To qualify for your cash reward, the person you refer needs to complete their first reservation—in other words, their first guest needs to complete their stay and check out—within 180 days of acting on the referral by clicking “Create your listing” from your unique link.Airbnb-provided referrals:To qualify for your cash reward, the person you refer needs to complete their first reservation—in other words, their first guest needs to complete their stay and check out—within 365 days of connecting with you on Airbnb as a Superhost Ambassador.
Home host referrals:A qualifying booking is a reservation that’s valued at $100 USD or the local equivalent. This amount includes pet fees and extra guest fees, and is calculated before taxes and other fees (includingAirbnb service fees). This amount is for the total value for all nights of the reservation, it does not have to be the nightly rate. But the value of the reservation (including all nights) must be at least $100 USD or more.
Co-host Network referrals:A qualifying booking is a reservation that’s valued at $100 USD or the local equivalent. This amount includes pet fees and extra guest fees, and is calculated before taxes and other fees (includingAirbnb service fees). This amount is for the total value for all nights of the reservation, it does not have to be the nightly rate. But the value of the reservation (including all nights) must be at least $100 USD or more.
Experience host referrals:A qualifying booking is a reservation that’s valued at $50 USD or the local equivalent (before taxes andAirbnb service fees). This amount is for the total value a guest pays for a reservation.
Service host referrals:A qualifying booking is a reservation that’s valued at $50 USD or the local equivalent (before taxes andAirbnb service fees). This amount is for the total value a guest pays for a reservation.Ambassador referrals:A qualifying booking is a reservation that’s valued at $100 USD or the local equivalent (before taxes andAirbnb service fees). This amount is for the total value for all nights of the reservation, it does not have to be the nightly rate. But the value of the reservation (including all nights) must be at least $100 USD or more.Airbnb-provided referrals:A qualifying booking is a reservation that’s valued at least $50 USD or the local equivalent (before taxes andAirbnb service fees). This amount is for the total value for all nights of the reservation, it does not have to be the nightly rate. But the value of the reservation (including all nights) must be at least $50 USD or more.
Host referrals:In order for us to keep track of your referrals, the person you refer will need to create their home, service, or experience listing by following the unique link you send them. That means going to the link, clicking Create your listing and then logging in or signing up to Airbnb.They can do all of this using the Airbnb app, too. Once someone starts creating their listing using your link, you can track their status on your referral dashboard.Co-host Network referrals:In order for us to keep track of your referrals, the person you refer will need to message you on the Co-Host Network. That means going to your link, clicking “message,” and then sending you a message. Once someone sends you a message via your referral link, you can track their status on your referral dashboard.
Ambassador referrals:Once someone starts creating their listing using your link, you can track their status on your referral dashboard.
Airbnb-provided referrals:Airbnb automatically tracks the referrals we assign, along with their expiration date.
If you create your listing via your referrer’s link, they can check whether you have started or published your listing, completed listing verification, and booked or hosted your first reservation. They can also check whether your first booking is eligible for a reward. If you don’t want to share this information with the person who referred you, you can create yourhome listing,service listing,  orexperience listingdirectly, instead.
The reward for home referrals will be based on the location, type, and size of the listing. The final reward amount will be determined once the qualifying booking is completed.

title: Booking a trip with travel credit from referrals
content:
Booking a trip with travel credit from referrals
OurAirbnb Guest Referral Programis currently only available to guests in these select countries: China, Germany, Italy, Japan, and Mexico.
If you signed up for Airbnb using a referral link, your referral coupon will automatically appear on the checkout page when you pay for your first qualifying reservation.
You must have a qualifying reservation to be eligible for referral travel credit. A qualifying reservation must meet a minimum value, which does not include any taxes or fees. Because the minimum amount can vary, you should refer to the invitation email you received or go to theinvitation pageto see which details about which reservations are eligible.
If your friend signed up for Airbnb using your referral link and completed a qualifying reservation, travel credit will be automatically added to your account. Your travel credit will appear on the checkout page when you pay for your next reservation.
Remember, you’ll get travel credit once your friend completes a qualifying trip. If your travel credit hasn’t shown up yet, it means your friend has not completed their trip yet or their reservation didn’t qualify.
Travel credit expires one year from the issue date.
If you apply travel credit to a reservation request that’s declined or expires, it can take up to 24 hours for it to appear back in your account.
Your coupon can only be used once. If you use referral credit on a reservation and then cancel that reservation, it can’t be used again.
The total amount of referral credits applied cannot exceed 15% of the booking’s total cost.
The referral travel credits cannot be used together with gift credits.

title: Find your Airbnb sign up referral coupon
content:
Find your Airbnb sign up referral coupon
If you signed up for Airbnb using a referral link, a referral coupon should automatically be added to your account after sign-up and appear on the checkout page when you pay for your first qualifying reservation.

title: Refer a friend to join Airbnb and get rewarded
content:
Refer a friend to join Airbnb and get rewarded
If you love to travel and share amazing experiences, why not get your friends and family in on the action? TheAirbnb Guest Referral Programlets you refer your friends and family to explore and use Airbnb, and you and your friends can earn rewards.
Refer Friends:Share your unique referral link with your friends and family.
Register:Your friends sign up for Airbnb using your link.
Earn Rewards:Your friends get a coupon for their first booking, and you earn travel credits when they complete their first trip.
Refer a friend on desktop
ClickProfile, and then clickRefer a friend
ClickShare your invite
Choose how you want to share your invite

title: How to earn V-points (Japan residents)
content:
How to earn V-points (Japan residents)
Calling all Japan residents who have a V-point account! You can earn V-points when you book accommodations on Airbnb.
You can use your points at any V-point alliance partners, online and across Japan.
Link your V-points account to your Airbnb account to earn 1V-pointfor every ¥200 (or foreign equivalent) paid when booking an accommodation on Airbnb, anywhere in the world. Airbnb Experiences are not included.
Once your accounts are properly linked, you’ll earn points for:
Nightly rate
Cleaning fee
Guest service fee
Coupons, local taxes, and deposits are not included.
If you cancel a booking and receive only a partial refund, you’ll receive points for any amount not refunded. Full-refunded canceled bookings don’t earn points.
Points are:
Based on the final value of your booking
Added to your account about one month after checkout
Forgot to link your V-point account to your Airbnb account before booking? You can still earn points if you connect your account before checkout.
VisitV-point | V-cardto:
Track your points
Manage your account
Disconnect your account
If you can’t log in to the V-point | V-card website, or for any V-point usage-related questions, refer to theV-point | V-card FAQ.

title: Host Referral Offer and Program Terms
content:
Host Referral Offer and Program Terms
Review our Host Referral Offer Terms and our Host Referral Program Terms below.
Last updated: May 13, 2025
For Referring Members
Referral Reward is available to Airbnb members that refer a new host with an experience, service, or home listing in aneligible location listed here.
To be eligible, new hosts must:
publish an experience, service, or ‘entire home’ listing as a ‘listing owner’ for the first time;
complete the new experience, service, or home listing’s first booking within 180 days from the time the new host clicks ‘create your experience’, ‘create your service’, or ‘create a listing’ using the referral link, or messages a co-host using a Co-Host Network referral link; and
ensure the first booking is worth at least $50 USD for experiences and services, and $100 USD for homes. For experiences and services, this value is before taxes and fees (including Airbnb fees). For homes, this value includes pet fees and extra guest fees, and is calculated before taxes or other fees (including Airbnb fees)
Listings that are ineligible include 1) private, shared, and hotel room listings and 2) homes,  experiences, and services that have been deactivated and republished. API-connected listings are ineligible unless otherwise specified. Reward amounts are in a range determined at the time the Referred Host creates their listing using the referral link and will remain valid for the duration of the period outlined on the referral pages. Amounts vary by listing location and the Referred Host’s listing type (including the number of bedrooms for home listings). Payout in USD or equivalent in local currency will be made within 30 days of completion of the Referred Host’s Qualifying Booking. Upon expiration of the period outlined on the referral pages, the offer shall immediately expire.
Check thereferral pagesfor offer expiration dates.
CheckHost Referral Program Termsbelow.
For Referred Members
By creating your listing via your referrer’s referral link or messaging a referring co-host on the Co-Host Network, you understand that information about your listing will be shared with your referrer, such as whether you have started or published your listing, completed listing verification, and booked or hosted your first reservation. They can also find whether your first booking is eligible for a reward.
If you don’t want to share this information with the person who referred you, you cancreate your home listing,experience listing, orservice listingdirectly, instead.
PLEASE NOTE THAT THEAIRBNB TERMS OF SERVICE,PAYMENTS TERMS OF SERVICE, ANDPRIVACY POLICYYOU AGREED TO APPLY TO YOUR PARTICIPATION IN THE HOST REFERRAL PROGRAM AND YOUR USE OF THE HOST REFERRAL DASHBOARD AND OTHER TOOLS AND WEBSITES PROVIDED BY AIRBNB. THESE PROGRAM TERMS AND CONDITIONS (“TERMS”) SUPPLEMENT THE AIRBNB TERMS OF SERVICE AND PRIVACY POLICY. PLEASE CONSULT THE AIRBNB TERMS OF SERVICE AND PRIVACY POLICY BEFORE AGREEING TO THESE ADDITIONAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS.
IF YOU RESIDE IN, OR THE ORGANIZATION YOU ARE ACTING FOR IS ESTABLISHED IN, THE UNITED STATES, PLEASE NOTE: THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE AND CLASS ACTION WAIVER CONTAINED IN THEAIRBNB TERMS OF SERVICEAPPLY TO ANY DISPUTE RELATING TO THESE ADDITIONAL TERMS.
IF YOUR COUNTRY OF RESIDENCE OR ESTABLISHMENT IS AUSTRALIA, SECTIONS 4, 6, 8, 12 and 14 OF THESE TERMS DO NOT APPLY TO YOU AND ARE REPLACED WITH SECTIONS 15, 16, 17, 18 and 19 OF THESE TERMS RESPECTIVELY.
1. Description of the Program
The Airbnb Host Referral Program (“Program”) allows members to earn money (check Payment section below) by referring new hosts who publish a listing for the first time on the Airbnb Platform (“Referred Hosts”) and successfully complete a Qualifying Booking.
Referring Members can only receive a Referral Reward payment for the first 25 Referred Home Hosts, the first 25 Referred Experience Hosts, and the first 25 Referred Service Hosts (i.e., the first 25 hosts they referred under this Referral Program) who complete their first Qualifying Booking in line with these Terms. Airbnb may offer other programs, products, or features that allow certain Referring Members to receive additional reward payments, as detailed in those other program pages, products, or features.
All Referred Hosts must be new, first time hosts. No payment will be made for listings created by current or former Airbnb hosts, or for bookings made by family or friends of the Referred Host, or for bookings where either the Referred Host or the Referred Host's listing has the same address as you.
Your participation in the Program is subject to these Terms. Any payments under this Program may not be combined with other Airbnb referral programs or incentives for the same Referred Host. By participating in the Program, you forfeit your ability to earn rewards or incentives via other Airbnb referral programs for Referred Hosts.
2. Use of the Dashboard
You may not use the Host Referral Dashboard (“Dashboard”) for any purpose other than tracking the progress of your Referred Hosts. You will respect the privacy of others and comply with applicable laws.
3. Referring Member Terms
You may only use this Program in good faith and in compliance with applicable laws. You may not represent yourself as an employee, intermediary, agent, partner, or representative, or as being authorized to bind Airbnb. You may not create materials (including business cards, websites or email addresses) bearing Airbnb branding or that imply any affiliation or agency relationship between you and Airbnb. However, you must clearly state that you are a Referring Member who is encouraging hosts to list on Airbnb. You will not receive payment for referring yourself.
Referrals should only be used for personal and non-commercial purposes, and only shared with personal connections that will appreciate receiving these invitations.
You may only use referral methods and techniques that people will appreciate and that are legal. You may only contact people who have given you their consent to be contacted, and you should not publish or distribute referral links to the general public on commercial websites or on social media platforms. Members are prohibited from “spamming” anyone with referral invitations. This includes mass emailing, texting or messaging people you do not know or using automated systems or bots through any channel to distribute your referral link. Members are prohibited from paying to advertise their referral links. You should not solicit potential hosts who live in buildings where short-term rentals are prohibited by law, such as government-sponsored housing. You must always be mindful to respect the privacy of others, be honest, open, and transparent about who you are and what you are offering (i.e. do not mislead). Make sure to tell your friends and others who you invite to join Airbnb to check the laws that may apply to them and that you educate them about what it takes to be a responsible host. More details are available in Airbnb’sresponsible hosting pages.
4. Payment
Subject to your compliance with these Terms, you will receive the amount specified in theDashboardafter the Referred Host completes their Qualifying Booking (the “Referral Reward”). Airbnb can change the Referral Reward amount at any time. The Referral Reward you receive will be in a range determined at the time the Referred Host creates their listing after following the referral link.
If you are based outside the US, you will receive the equivalent in your local currency (i.e., the currency of thePayout Methodthat you have selected for payouts).
Where applicable, the local currency equivalent will be calculated based on a currency conversion rate determined by us, which may not be identical to the real-time market rate.
Payouts will be made within 30 days of completion of each Qualifying Booking, to thePayout Methodyou designate on your Airbnb account. All payments hereunder shall be subject to thePayments Terms of Service(the “Payments Terms”). All provisions of the Payments Terms, including but not limited to Sections 3 and 5 of the Payments Terms, that apply to hosts, also apply to you. The Airbnb Terms of Service as they relate to the application of taxes apply to any Referral Reward payment.
Whether a payout is due shall be a decision made in the sole discretion of Airbnb, and solely based upon Airbnb’s records. Airbnb reserves the right to any remedy, including denial of payout or cancellation of your account, if fraud, tampering, wrongful conduct, or other violations of Airbnb's Terms of Service or these Terms are suspected.
5. Referred Member Terms
By creating your listing via your referrer’s referral link, you understand that information about your listing will be shared with your referrer, such as whether you have started or published your listing, completed listing verification, and booked or hosted your first reservation. They can also check whether your first booking is eligible for a reward.
If you don’t want to share this information with the person who referred you, you can create yourhome listing here, yourexperience listing here, and yourservice listing hereinstead.
6. Qualifying Bookings
A Qualifying Booking is a booking that: (i) is accepted by the Referred Host and paid for by the Guest, (ii) is not cancelled by either the Guest or the Referred Host at any time before or after the scheduled check-in for such booking, (iii) meets the minimum total value indicated in the Offer Terms, accompanying promotional materials, Airbnb website, or Airbnb mobile application for the type of booking referenced in the Offer Terms, and (iv) meets all other eligibility requirements listed in the Offer Terms.  For homes, the minimum total value includes pet fees and extra guest fees, and is calculated before taxes or other fees (including Airbnb fees). For experiences and services bookings, the minimum total value is the booking amount before taxes and fees (including Airbnb fees). At expiration, Airbnb reserves the right to adjust the minimum total value requirement for subsequent offers at its sole discretion, which may vary by jurisdiction and currency. Note that this total value may change based on currency fluctuations. The Qualifying Booking must be booked and paid for via the Airbnb platform, and the Referred Host must complete the stay, experience, or service before the Referring Member can receive a Referral Reward payment. If the Referring Member is the host or guest, or if the reservation is cancelled at any time, the reservation will not be considered a Qualifying Booking, and therefore no Referral Reward payment will be earned.
7. Multiple Referrals
A Referred Host may only use one referral link. If a Referred Host receives referral links from multiple Members, only the Member associated with the first referral link actually used by the Referred Host to create their listing will be eligible to receive a Referral Reward payment
8. Additional Incentives and Programs
From time to time, Airbnb may publicize additional incentives and programs applicable to particular locations and listing types. Such announcements will include specific information regarding the requirements for receiving such incentives in that location. Airbnb reserves the right to modify or withdraw these incentives at any time.
9. Taxes
Airbnb cannot and does not offer tax-related advice to you.
You are solely responsible for determining and fulfilling your obligations under applicable laws to report, collect, remit or include any applicable taxes, including, but not limited to, income, sales and use, VAT, and GST (“Taxes"), as well as your applicable tax reporting requirements on the payouts earned from the Program.
In certain jurisdictions, tax regulations may require that we collect and report tax information about you, or withhold taxes from payouts to you, or both. In the cases where Airbnb has a withholding obligation, Airbnb will withhold and remit the applicable taxes to the relevant tax authorities. If you fail to provide us with documentation that we determine to be sufficient to report tax information or to withhold taxes from payouts to you, we may hold payouts up to the amount as required by law, until sufficient documentation is provided.
Where applicable, or based upon request, you should issue a valid VAT invoice to Airbnb.
You agree that Airbnb may issue invoices or similar documentation for VAT, GST, consumption or other taxes for your payouts on your behalf to facilitate accurate tax reporting if available in your jurisdiction and offered by Airbnb.
10. Nature of Relationship
Your participation in the Program does not, under any circumstances, create an employment, agency, partnership, or other service relationship between you and Airbnb. You will not hold yourself out as, or give any person reason to believe, that you are an employee, intermediary, agent, partner, or representative of Airbnb. Airbnb will not carry any liability or other insurance on behalf of you, or in your favor, based on your participation in this Program. Referring hosts and referred hosts acknowledge and agree that Airbnb is not acting as a real estate broker for either party.
11. Warranties
You represent and warrant that: (a) you meet all eligibility criteria and possess all necessary consents and permits to participate in this Program; (b) you have no pre-existing obligations or commitments (and will not assume or otherwise undertake any obligations or commitments) that would be in conflict or inconsistent with or that would hinder your performance of your obligations under these Terms; and (c) you will fully comply at all times with all applicable laws and you will not infringe on any third-party rights (including Airbnb).
12. Termination
Airbnb may terminate your participation in the Program at any time, for any reason or no reason. The rights and obligations of the parties under these Terms will survive the expiration or termination of these Terms.
13. Severability
If any provision in these Terms are held to be invalid, void, or unenforceable, such provision (or the part of it that is making it invalid, void or unenforceable) will be struck and not affect the validity of and enforceability of the remaining provisions, to the extent permissible under applicable law.
14. Modification
Airbnb reserves the right, at its sole discretion, to modify the Program at any time by updating this page, the Airbnb website, Referrals Dashboard, or other product pages. Continuing to access the Dashboard or share your referral link with Referred Hosts will constitute acceptance of the revised Terms. If the modified Terms are not acceptable to you, your only recourse is to cease using the Dashboard and stop your Program participation.
15. Payment
Subject to your compliance with these Terms, the Referral Reward you receive will be in the range determined at the time the Referred Host creates their listing after following the referral link. The final reward amount will be visible to you in your referral dashboard after the Referred Host completes their Qualifying Booking and this is the amount that you will receive (the “Referral Reward”).  Airbnb may change Reward offers and the corresponding Referral Reward amounts, after offers expire.
If you are based outside the US, you will receive the equivalent Referral Reward in your local currency (i.e., the currency of thePayout Methodthat you have selected for payouts). In circumstances where the reward amounts were communicated to you in USD, the local currency equivalent will be calculated based on a currency conversion rate determined by us.
Payouts will be made within 30 days of completion of each Qualifying Booking, to thePayout Methodyou designate on your Airbnb account. All payments hereunder shall be subject to thePayments Terms of Service(the “Payments Terms”). All provisions of the Payments Terms, including but not limited to Sections 3 and 5 of the Payments Terms, that apply to hosts, also apply to you. The Airbnb Terms of Service as they relate to the application of taxes apply to any Referral Reward payment.
Whether the criteria for a payout has been met shall be a decision made in the sole discretion of Airbnb, acting reasonably in the circumstances. Airbnb reserves the right to any remedy, including denial of payout or cancellation of your account, if fraud, tampering, wrongful conduct, or other violations of Airbnb's Terms of Service or these Terms are suspected.
16. Qualifying Bookings
A Qualifying Booking is a booking that: (i) is accepted by the Referred Host and paid for by the Guest, (ii) is not cancelled by either the Guest or the Referred Host at any time before or after the scheduled check-in for such booking, (iii) meets the minimum total value indicated in the Offer Terms, accompanying promotional materials, Airbnb website, or Airbnb mobile application for the type of booking referenced in the Offer Terms, and (iv) meets all other eligibility requirements listed in the Offer Terms.  For homes, the minimum total value includes pet fees and extra guest fees, and is calculated before taxes or other fees (including Airbnb fees). For experiences and services bookings, the minimum total value is the booking amount before taxes and fees (including Airbnb fees). At expiration of an offer, Airbnb reserves the right to adjust the minimum total value requirement for any subsequent or updated offers, which may vary by jurisdiction and currency. Note that this total value may change based on currency fluctuations. The Qualifying Booking must be booked and paid for via the Airbnb platform, and the Referred Host must complete the stay,  experience, or service before the Referring Member can receive a Referral Reward payment. If the Referring Member is the host or guest, or if the reservation is cancelled at any time, the reservation will not be considered a Qualifying Booking, and therefore no Referral Reward payment will be earned.
17. Additional Incentives and Programs
From time to time, Airbnb may publicize additional incentives and programs applicable to particular locations and listing types. Such announcements will include specific information regarding the requirements for receiving such incentives in that location. Any additional incentive or program will be valid for the duration of the offer provided in the incentive or program and following expiry, Airbnb reserves the right to modify or withdraw these incentives.
18. Termination
You may terminate your participation in the Program at any time and for any reason by ceasing your participation in the Program. Airbnb may terminate your participation in the Program for any reason upon expiration of an offer, where no other offers are currently on foot. Airbnb may also terminate your participation in the Program immediately and without prior notice if (i) you materially breach these Terms, (ii) you violate applicable laws, or (iii) such action is necessary to protect the personal safety or property of Airbnb, its Members, or third parties (for example in the case of fraudulent behavior of a Member). The rights and obligations of the parties under these Terms will survive the expiration or termination of these Terms.
19. Modification
Airbnb may modify these Terms at any time. When we make material changes to these Terms, we will post the revised Terms on the Airbnb Platform and update the “Last Updated” date at the top of these Terms. If modifications occur during the period outlined in the offer referral link, we will also provide you with notice of any material changes by email, notifications through the Airbnb Platform, messaging service, or any other contact method made available by us and selected by you at least 30 days before the date they become effective. If you disagree with the revised Terms, you may terminate this agreement immediately as provided in these Terms. If you do not terminate your agreement before the date the revised Terms become effective, your continued participation in the Program will constitute acceptance of the revised Terms.

content:
Referring a host for a cash reward
Know someone you think would be a great host? You can send them a referral to Airbnb and earn a cash reward when they host a qualifying booking. Find more information in ourHost Referral Offer and Program Termsor check out thisoverview of the program.
Refer a host on desktop
The feature is only available in the Airbnb app, not on a browser.
You can also tapYour referralsto find your pending or accepted referrals. TapYour earningsto find the cash rewards that you have earned forqualifying bookings.
