title: Buying an Airbnb gift card
topic: Purchasing and using an Airbnb gift card
content:
Buying an Airbnb gift card
Airbnb gift cards can be used to give the gift of travel or a one-of-a-kind experience or service. They can be purchased online or in stores.
You can get Airbnb gift cards in stores and online, ordirectly from us! Depending where you buy your gift card, you can get a physical card or digital card sent to you or your recipient.
Purchase digital gift cards on Airbnb
Gift cards purchased onAirbnb.comare digital only. The following countries are available for purchase: Australia, Austria, Belgium, Canada, Croatia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Mexico, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Kingdom, and the United States.
Purchase physical gift cards from participating retailers
You can buy a physical gift card at participating store locations. The following countries are available to purchase a physical gift card: Austria, Belgium, Canada, Finland, France, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, New Zealand, Spain, Sweden, United Kingdom, and the United States.
If you’re in a country within the European Economic Area (EEA) region—Austria, Belgium, Croatia, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, and Sweden—you can purchase a gift card for someone else in one of those EEA countries as long as the recipient has a valid payment method within that country.
Gift cards never expire and once the gift card isadded to your account, it can be used for any stay or Experience around the world. You can alsocheck your balanceat any time.
Refundable credit: In the event that your booking is canceled, you can expect a refund to your gift card balance on your account if it was used during purchase.Cancellation policiesmay apply.
Use multiple gift cards:You can redeem multiple cards on a single Airbnb account.
Use gift cards multiple times: Available credit can be used multiple times up to the full value of your gift card.
Flexible use: Available credit can be used to book homes or experiences and services.  (Experiences and Services are not available for gift cards in the European Economic Area and Switzerland)
If you're having trouble with your digital gift card purchase on Airbnb, let us know:
To find out more about how gift cards work in the countries and regions where you can currently purchase and redeem them, visitAirbnb’s Gift Card Terms and Conditions.

title: Redeeming your Airbnb gift card
topic: Purchasing and using an Airbnb gift card
content:
Redeeming your Airbnb gift card
Airbnb gift cards can be purchased online or in stores, and can beredeemedat any time.
Here's how to add the funds from the card balance to your account.
Redeem a gift card on desktop
Clickgift card redemption
Follow the on-screen prompts to enter your redemption info
ClickRedeem gift card
To find out more about how gift cards work in the countries and regions where you can currently purchase and redeem them, visitAirbnb’s Gift Card Terms and Conditions.

title: How gift cards work
topic: Purchasing and using an Airbnb gift card
content:
How gift cards work
Airbnb gift cards are a versatile way to treat someone special (or even yourself) to a world of possibilities.
Airbnb offers two gift card formats: physical cards available from brick-and-mortar retailers, and digital cards available directly from Airbnb and other online merchants. Regardless of format, both types include a gift card number and PIN—these two numbers will makeredeeming your gift carda breeze.
Good news! Airbnb gift cards never expire. This is true whether the balance is still on the card, or has been loaded to your account. That said, we do recommendadding the gift cardto your account as soon as possible to avoid a lost/misplaced card situation.
Unfortunately, unless you know the missing gift card’s number and PIN, it’s very difficult, and often impossible for our team to recover them. This is one of the reasons we recommend adding your gift card to your account as soon as possible.
In many parts of the world, gift cards can be used for a variety of Airbnb stays and experiences, subject to regulatory restrictions. Please keep in mind that you’ll still need to provide an alternate payment method when booking, even if the balance covers the full amount of your reservation.
You can find more information on using gift cards forPay Part Now, Part Laterreservations here.
In many parts of the world, gift cards can be used for Airbnb experiences in addition to stays.  However, due to regulatory restrictions, if you’re based in theEuropean Economic Area, gift cards can only be used for stays.
If you’re encountering a problem using an Airbnb gift card, our team is happy to help. For the fastest resolution, we recommend double-checking the following before contacting an Airbnb agent, as many of the most commonly reported gift card issues can be fixed with one of the following steps:
Double check the card number and PIN. While it sounds simple, long alphanumeric codes are easy to misread!
If your card has already been redeemed, ensure your card has a balance.
Ensure your transaction can be paid for with a gift card. While most are eligible, gift cards can’t be used for payments related to theResolution Centeror collections.
If you’ve checked all of the following and are still experiencing an issue, our friendly Airbnb support agents will be happy to help.
To find out more about how gift cards work in the countries and regions where you can currently purchase and redeem them, visitAirbnb’s Gift Card Terms and Conditions.

title: Checking your gift card balance
topic: Purchasing and using an Airbnb gift card
content:
Checking your gift card balance
Airbnb gift cards can be purchased online or in stores, and can beredeemedat any time. Once you add the funds from the card to your account, you can check your Airbnb gift credits balance.
Track your gift card balance on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Payments
Go toAirbnb gift creditto review the current credit balance
ClickCurrent balancefor the list of gift cards applied to your account
When you book your stay or experience, the balance from any gift card funds on your account will be automatically applied. More good news: Once a gift card has been added to an Airbnb account, the gift card (claimed or unclaimed) won’t expire.
To find out more about how gift cards work in the countries and regions where you can currently purchase and redeem them, visitAirbnb’s Gift Card Terms and Conditions.

title: Using a Gift Card for a Pay Part Now, Part Later Reservation
topic: Purchasing and using an Airbnb gift card
content:
Using a Gift Card for a Pay Part Now, Part Later Reservation
The “Pay Part Now, Part Later” (PPNPL) option makes it easy to book a reservation and pay in two smaller installments. While Airbnb gift cards can be used with this option, here are some important things to keep in mind.
You can always choose to use a gift card for your first payment of a PPNPL reservation. Just make sure the balance has beenredeemedand that credits have been added to your account.
Since a reservation can be canceled if a second payment is not received, the PPNPL option uses a single, preferred payment method by default. Here’s how to change your payment method for the second part of a PPNPL reservation.
Add a gift card to an existing PPNPL reservation on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to apply gift card credits to
ClickGet receipts and manage payments
UnderUpcoming, click the payment you want to adjust
Go toPay with
Next toCredits applied, clickManage
Select the gift card credits you want to apply to your payment
ClickConfirm and pay

title: How to find your Airbnb gift card PIN
topic: Purchasing and using an Airbnb gift card
content:
How to find your Airbnb gift card PIN
Every Airbnb gift card has a Personal Identification Number (PIN) for security purposes. You’ll need this PIN toredeem your Airbnb gift card.
Both digital and physical Airbnb gift cards feature two key numbers you’ll need to unlock your balance: a card number (the longer one) and a PIN (the shorter one). For digital gift cards, you’ll receive your card number and PIN at the same time via email. Physical cards have both of these numbers on the back of the card. For security, each PIN is protected by UV ink, which we recommend scratching off gently with a coin.
We’ve all been there—in the excitement of redeeming a gift card, we scratch off a bit of the PIN. If your Airbnb card’s PIN is scratched off (or otherwise illegible),contact usfor help. Keep this information handy when requesting support:
The full card number
Readable parts of PIN
The receipt from the original purchase
In some cases, Airbnb can issue a replacement, even if you can’t read the PIN on your gift card.

title: U.S. Gift Card Terms and Conditions
content:
U.S. Gift Card Terms and Conditions
Last updated on July 29, 2025
These U.S. Gift Card Terms and Conditions ("Gift Card Terms") govern your use of Airbnb Gift Cards ("Gift Cards") on the Airbnb Platform. These Gift Card Terms constitute an agreement between you, the Cardholder, and Airbnb Gift Card Services, LLC.  By purchasing or using your Card, you agree to be bound by these Gift Card Terms.IMPORTANT:  These Gift Card Terms include resolution of disputes by arbitration on an individual basis instead of in court.
Your use of Gift Cards and the services available via the Airbnb Platform shall be subject to Airbnb’sTerms of Service(“Terms“) andPayments Terms of Service(“Payments Terms”). If you see an undefined term in these Gift Card Terms, it has the same definition as in the Terms and Payments Terms.
1. About Your Card.
Gift Cards sold in the U.S. are issued by Airbnb Gift Card Services, LLC (“Issuer”). Gift Cards can be purchased through www.airbnb.com/giftcards and from authorized third-party distributors. Gift Cards may be issued in either electronic or physical form, at the discretion of Issuer.
You must have an Airbnb account in order to use a Gift Card. If you do not have an Airbnb account, you’ll need to create one in order to use a Gift Card. You can add a Gift Card to your Airbnb account by visiting www.airbnb.com/gift. Once added, the entire Gift Card value will be transferred to and shown on your Airbnb account as your Gift Card balance. Gift Card balance(s) may only be redeemed toward the purchase of services on the Airbnb Platform provided via Issuer and its affiliates. Any purchase made with a Gift Card will be deducted from your Gift Card balance(s), and any unused Gift Card balance(s) will remain on your Airbnb account until used. If a purchase exceeds your existing Gift Card balance(s), the remaining amount due must be paid with another Payment Method. You can view your Gift Card balance(s) by signing in to your Airbnb account.
2. Expiration; Fees.
No fees of any kind apply to Gift Cards, and Gift Cards and Gift Card balance(s) do not expire.
3. Limitations.
Gift Cards purchased in the U.S. (not including U.S. territories or possessions) can only be used by individuals residing in the U.S., not including U.S. territories and possessions. You must also have a valid Payment Method issued in the U.S.
Gift Cards may only be applied towards charges for booking Accommodations, Experiences or Services; they may not be applied towards other charges in connection with services available on the Airbnb Platform, including but not limited to: Resolution Center payments, Damage Report amounts, or the purchase of other Gift Cards. Gift Cards cannot be reloaded, resold, transferred for value or redeemed for cash, except to the extent required by law. Gift Cards have no cash value. Once a Gift Card is added to an Airbnb account, its value cannot be transferred to another person or Airbnb account; a Gift Card balance on an Airbnb account may not be cashed out or transferred to another Airbnb account.
4. Lost, Stolen or Destroyed Gift Cards; Gift Cards Used Without Permission.
Please safeguard your Gift Card. Once a digital Gift Card is electronically transmitted to the purchaser or designated recipient, or a physical Gift Card is delivered to the purchaser or designated recipient, whichever is applicable, the risk of loss and title for the Gift Card pass to the purchaser or designated recipient. Issuer is not responsible if any Gift Card is lost, stolen, destroyed or used without your permission.
5. Fraud.
Issuer and its affiliates reserve the right to refuse to issue or honor a Gift Card or pursue any other legal remedy, in the event of suspected fraud. Issuer and its affiliates also reserve the right to refuse to honor a Gift Card that is obtained from unauthorized sellers and resellers. Issuer and its affiliates have the right to close an Airbnb account and charge alternative Payment Methods if a fraudulently obtained Gift Card is added and/or used to make or attempt purchases on the Airbnb Platform.
6. Limitation of Liability.
Issuer and its affiliates make no warranties, express or implied, with respect to Gift Cards, including without limitation, any express or implied warranty of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose. In the event a Gift Card is non-functional, your sole remedy, and our sole liability, will be the replacement of such Gift Card. Certain state laws do not allow limitations on implied warranties or the exclusion or limitation of certain damages. If these laws apply to you, some or all of the above disclaimers, exclusions, or limitations may not apply to you, and you may have additional rights.
7. Termination; Waiver
Your rights under a Gift Card will terminate if you materially fail to comply with these Gift Card Terms or if your Airbnb account is terminated or is suspended. Upon termination, you must cease all use of the Gift Card, and we may revoke your access to any Gift Card balance. Our failure to insist upon or enforce your strict compliance with these Gift Card Terms will not constitute a waiver of any of our rights.
8. Mandatory Binding Dispute Resolution and Individual Arbitration Agreement.
8.1 Application.This Arbitration Agreement applies to any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to these Gift Card Terms or the applicability, breach, termination, validity, enforcement or interpretation thereof, or any use of services provided by Issuer and its affiliates with respect to Gift Cards. If you attempt to bring any legal claim against Issuer in the U.S., this Arbitration Agreement will apply for determination of the threshold issue of whether this Section 8 applies to you, and all other threshold determinations, including residency, arbitrability, venue, and applicable law.
8.2 Overview of Dispute Resolution Process.Issuer is committed to participating in a consumer-friendly dispute resolution process. To that end, these Gift Card Terms provide for a two-part process for individuals to whom this Section 8 applies: (1) an informal negotiation directly with Airbnb’s customer service team (described in Section 8.3), and if necessary (2) a binding arbitration in accordance with the terms of this Arbitration Agreement. You and Issuer each retain the right to seek resolution of the dispute in small claims court as an alternative to arbitration.
8.3 Mandatory Pre-Arbitration Dispute Resolution and Notification.At least 30 days prior to initiating an arbitration, you and Issuer each agree to send the other party an individualized notice of the dispute in writing (“Pre-Dispute Notice”) and attempt in good faith to negotiate an informal resolution of the individual claim. You must send your Pre-Dispute Notice to Issuer by mailing it to Issuers’ agent for service:CSC Lawyers Incorporating Service, 2710 Gateway Oaks Drive, Suite 150N, Sacramento, California 95833. Issuer will send its notice of dispute to the email address associated with your Airbnb account. A Pre-Dispute Notice must include: the date, your name, mailing address, your Airbnb username, the email address you used to set up your Airbnb account, your signature, a brief description of the dispute, and the relief sought. If the parties are unable to resolve the dispute within the 30-day period, only then may either party commence arbitration by filing a written demand for arbitration with the arbitration provider designated pursuant to Section 8.6, below.  A claimant’s Pre-Dispute Notice requirement is a prerequisite to any arbitration, and a copy of the Pre-Dispute Notice and evidence that it was sent as required by this Section must be attached to any arbitration demand.
8.4 Agreement to Arbitrate; Delegation. You and Issuer mutually agree that any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to these Gift Card Terms or the applicability, breach, termination, validity, enforcement or interpretation thereof, or any use of Gift Cards or use of services provided by Issuer and its affiliates with respect to Gift Cards (collectively, “Disputes”) will be settled by binding arbitration on an individual basis (the “Arbitration Agreement”). If there is a dispute about whether this Arbitration Agreement can be enforced or applies to a Dispute, you and Issuer agree that an arbitrator will decide that issue. For the avoidance of doubt, you and Issuer agree that any question regarding arbitrability and the formation, enforceability, validity, scope, or interpretation of all or part of this Section 8, including any dispute over compliance with the Pre-Dispute Notice requirement and a party’s responsibility to pay arbitration fees, shall be resolved exclusively by an arbitrator.
8.5 Exceptions to Arbitration Agreement.You and Issuer each agree that the following causes of action and/or claims for relief are exceptions to the Arbitration Agreement and will be brought in a judicial proceeding in a court of competent jurisdiction (as defined by Section 8): (i) any claim or cause of action alleging actual or threatened infringement, misappropriation or violation of a party’s copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, patents, or other intellectual property rights; (ii) any claim or cause of action seeking emergency injunctive relief based on exigent circumstances (e.g., imminent danger or commission of a crime, hacking, cyber-attack); (iii) a request for the remedy of public injunctive relief; (iv) any claim or cause of action for vexatious litigation; or (v) any individual claim of sexual assault or sexual harassment arising from your use of the Airbnb Platform or Host Services. You and Issuer agree that the remedy of public injunctive relief will proceed after the arbitration of all arbitrable claims, remedies, or causes of action, and will be stayed pending the outcome of the arbitration pursuant to section 3 of the Federal Arbitration Act.
8.6 Arbitration Forum Rules and Governing Law.This Arbitration Agreement evidences a transaction in interstate commerce and the Federal Arbitration Act governs all substantive and procedural interpretation and enforcement of this Arbitration Agreement, and not state law. The arbitration will be administered by ADR Services, Inc. (“ADR”) (www.adrservices.com) in accordance with Rules 1, 6–7, 8–9, and 11–12, 45, 54, and 56 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure (“Selected Federal Rules") (https://www.uscourts.gov/rules-policies/current-rules-practice-procedure/federal-rules-civil-procedure) and ADR’s Arbitration Rules then in effect (the “ADR Rules”), except as the Selected Federal Rules or ADR Rules are modified by or conflict with this Arbitration Agreement.The ADR Rules are available atwww.adrservices.com. If an arbitration demand is submitted to ADR Services in accordance with this agreement and the ADR Rules, and ADR Services cannot or will not administer the arbitration, the arbitration will be administered by the American Arbitration Association (“AAA”) in accordance with the Selected Federal Rules and the AAA’s Consumer Arbitration Rules (the “AAA Rules”) then in effect, except as modified here. The AAA Rules are available atwww.adr.org.. If the AAA cannot and will not administer the arbitration, you and Airbnb shall confer and select an alternative arbitral forum, and if we are unable to agree, either you or Airbnb may ask a court to appoint an arbitrator pursuant to 9 U.S.C. § 5. In that event, the arbitration will be conducted in accordance with the rules of the appointed arbitral forum, unless those rules are inconsistent with the provisions of this Arbitration Agreement.
8.7 Modification to ArbitrationA Rules - Arbitration Hearing/Location.In order to make the arbitration most cost-effective, efficient, and convenient, any required arbitration hearing in an arbitration wherein the amount in controversy does not exceed $1,000,000 shall be conducted remotely via video conference except as otherwise agreed by the parties or instructed by the arbitrator. Any required arbitration hearing in an arbitration wherein the amount in controversy exceeds $1,000,000 shall be conducted in San Francisco County except as otherwise agreed by the parties or instructed by the arbitrator. If the amount in controversy is $10,000 or less, the parties agree to proceed solely on the submission of documents to the arbitrator, provided that the arbitrator has discretion to decide to hold a hearing in response to the reasonable and proportionate request from a party.
8.8 Modification of Arbitration Rules - Arbitration Fees and Costs.Your arbitration fees and your share of arbitrator compensation shall be governed by the ADR Rules and the ADR Services fee schedule (available atwww.adrservices.com). If you have a gross monthly income of less than 300% of the federal poverty guidelines, you are entitled to a waiver of arbitration fees and costs, exclusive of arbitrator fees. You may request a fee waiver by providing the arbitration provider with a declaration under oath stating your monthly income and the number of persons in your household. If a fee waiver is granted by the arbitration provider and you provide Airbnb with documents necessary to prove that your gross monthly income is less than 300% of the federal poverty guidelines, Airbnb will pay your share of any arbitrator fees.
8.9 Modification of Arbitration Rules - Claims Brought for an Improper Purpose of In Violation of This Arbitration Agreement.Either party may make a request that the arbitrator impose sanctions upon proving that the other party or its attorney(s) has asserted a claim or defense that is groundless in fact or law, brought in bad faith or for the purpose of harassment, or is otherwise frivolous. As allowed by applicable law, the arbitrator shall impose sanctions equal to the requesting party’s reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs upon finding that a claim or defense is groundless in fact or law, brought in bad faith or for the purpose of harassment, asserted in violation of Fed. R. Civ. P. 11(b) (treating the arbitrator as “the court”), or is otherwise frivolous. Either party may seek dismissal of any arbitration filed in violation of any provision of this Arbitration Agreement. Either party may assert in arbitration a counterclaim for the other party’s initiation of proceedings concerning an arbitrable Dispute without complying with or otherwise in violation of the requirements of this Arbitration Agreement. Upon finding that a party has initiated proceedings concerning an arbitrable Dispute without complying with or otherwise in violation of the requirements of this Arbitration Agreement, the arbitrator shall award the other party its actual damages, including but not limited to reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs.
8.10 Arbitrator’s Decision.The arbitrator will issue a written decision which shall include the essential findings and conclusions upon which the arbitrator based the award. Judgment on the arbitration award may be entered in any court with proper jurisdiction. The arbitrator may award any relief allowed by law or the ADR Rules, but declaratory or injunctive relief may be awarded only on an individual basis and only to the extent necessary to provide relief warranted by the claimant’s individual claim.
8.11 Jury Trial Waiver.You and Issuer acknowledge and agree that both parties are each waiving the right to a trial by jury as to all arbitrable Disputes.
8.12 No Class Actions or Representative Proceedings.You and Issuer acknowledge and agree that, to the fullest extent permitted by law, we are each waiving the right to participate as a plaintiff or class member in any purported class action lawsuit, class-wide arbitration, private attorney general action, or any other representative or consolidated proceeding. Unless we agree in writing or as provided in this agreement, the arbitrator may not consolidate more than one party’s claims and may not otherwise preside over any form of any class or representative proceeding. If there is a final judicial determination that applicable law precludes enforcement of the waiver contained in this paragraph as to any claim, cause of action or requested remedy, then that claim, cause of action or requested remedy, and only that claim, cause of action or requested remedy, will be severed from this agreement to arbitrate and will be brought in a court of competent jurisdiction. In the event that a claim, cause of action or requested remedy is severed pursuant to this paragraph, then you and we agree that the claims, causes of action or requested remedies that are not subject to arbitration will be stayed until all arbitrable claims, causes of action and requested remedies are resolved by the arbitrator.
8.13 Modifications of Arbitration Rules - Offers of Judgment.At least 10 days before the date set for the arbitration hearing, you or Issuer may serve a written offer of judgment on the other party to allow judgment on specified terms. If the offer is accepted, the offer with proof of acceptance shall be submitted to the arbitration provider, who shall issue an award accordingly. If the offer is not accepted prior to the arbitration hearing or within 30 days after it is made, whichever occurs first, it shall be deemed withdrawn and cannot be given as evidence in the arbitration, other than with respect to costs (including all fees paid to the arbitration provider). If an offer made by one party is not accepted by the other party, and the other party fails to obtain a more favorable award, the other party shall not recover their post-offer costs and shall pay the offering party’s costs (including all fees paid to the arbitration provider) from the time of the offer.
8.14 Severability.Except as provided in Section 8.11, in the event that any portion of this Arbitration Agreement is deemed illegal or unenforceable, such provision shall be severed and the remainder of the Arbitration Agreement will be given full force and effect.
8.15 Amendment to Agreement to Arbitrate.If Issuer amends this Section 8 after the date you last accepted these Gift Card Terms (or accepted any subsequent changes to these Gift Card Terms), you may reject that change by sending us written notice within thirty (30) days of the date the change is effective. Your notice must include your name, mailing address, the date of the notice, your Airbnb username, the email address you used to set up your Airbnb account, your signature, and an unequivocal statement that you want to opt out of the amended Section 22. You must either mail your notice to this address: 888 Brannan St, San Francisco, CA 94103, Attn: Arbitration Opt-Out, or email the opt-out notice to arbitration.opt.out@airbnb.com. Rejecting a new change, however, does not revoke or alter your prior consent to any earlier agreements to arbitrate any Dispute between you and Issuer (or your prior consent to any subsequent changes thereto), which will remain in effect and enforceable as to any Dispute between you and Issuer.
8.16 Survival.Except as provided in Section 8.12, this Section 8 will survive any termination of these Gift Card Terms and will continue to apply even if you terminate your Airbnb account.
9.Miscellaneous.
Gift Cards sold in the U.S. (not including U.S. territories or possessions) are issued by Issuer. Issuer is responsible for the operation and maintenance of the Gift Card program. Issuer is the sole legal obligor to you, the Cardholder; provided, however, that Issuer may assign its obligations with respect to the Gift Card at any time, in which case such assignee shall become the Issuer and sole legal obligor to the Cardholder. Issuer’s affiliates and related entities bear no responsibility or liability for any Gift Cards, and you hereby knowingly release Issuer’s affiliates and related entities from any and all liability or claims of any nature whatsoever arising in connection with Gift Cards. When you purchase, receive, or use a Gift Card, you agree that the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, without regard to principles of conflict of laws, will govern these Gift Card Terms.
10. Severability.
Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, if any part of these Gift Card Terms is deemed invalid or inapplicable, such provision shall be modified or restricted to the extent and in the manner necessary to render it valid, legal, and enforceable. If such provision cannot be so modified or restricted, it shall be excised from these Gift Card Terms without affecting the validity, legality, or enforceability of the remainder of these Gift Card Terms, which shall be fully enforced.
11. Changes to Gift Card Terms.
Issuer reserves the right to modify these Gift Card Terms at any time. When we make material changes to these Gift Card Terms, we will post the revised Gift Card Terms on the Airbnb Platform and update the “Last Updated” date at the top of these Gift Card Terms. Your continued access to or use of the Airbnb Platform and/or failure to close your Airbnb account will constitute acceptance of the revised Gift Card Terms.

If a purchase exceeds your existing Gift Card balance(s), the remaining amount due must be paid with another Payment Method. You can view your Gift Card balance(s) by signing in to your Airbnb account.
No fees of any kind apply to Gift Cards, and Gift Cards and Gift Card balance(s) do not expire.

Gift Cards may only be applied towards charges for booking Accommodations, Experiences or Services; they may not be applied towards other charges in connection with services available on the Airbnb Platform, including but not limited to: changes to existing reservations, Resolution Center Payments, Damage Report amounts, or the  purchase of other Gift Cards. Once a Gift Card is added to an Airbnb account, its value cannot be transferred to another person or Airbnb account; a Gift Card balance on an Airbnb account may not be cashed out or transferred to another Airbnb account.
Please safeguard your Gift Card. Issuer and its affiliates reserve the right to any remedy, including refusal to issue or honour a Gift Card in the event of suspected fraud. To the maximum extent permitted by law, we make no warranties, express or implied, with respect to Gift Cards, including without limitation, any express or implied warranty of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose. To the maximum extent permitted by law, in the event a Gift Card is non-functional, your sole remedy, and our sole liability, will be the replacement of such Gift Card. Certain laws do not allow limitations on implied warranties or the exclusion or limitation of certain damages. If these laws apply to you, some or all of the above disclaimers, exclusions or limitations may not apply to you, and you may have additional rights.
Your rights under a Gift Card will terminate if you materially fail to comply with these Gift Card Terms or if your Airbnb account is terminated or is suspended. If your suspended account is reactivated, your Gift Card rights can be used again. Issuer’s failure to insist upon or enforce your strict compliance with these Gift Card Terms will not constitute a waiver of any of Issuer’s rights.
Any dispute or claim relating in any way to Gift Cards will be resolved in accordance with the Governing Law and Dispute Resolution section of the Terms.

Airbnb Ireland UC8 Hanover QuayDublin 2, D02 DP23Ireland

Issuer reserves the right to modify these Gift Card Terms at any time. We will also provide you with notice of any material changes by email at least 60 days before the date they become effective, and will inform you about your right to terminate your Terms of Service agreement with Airbnb. title: Locating your VAT invoice
content:
Locating your VAT invoice
AValue Added Tax (VAT)invoice is provided whenever VAT is assessed on Airbnb service fees. Your invoice is finalized and issued when a reservation is accepted and includes your information (name, address, etc.) as you entered it in your Airbnb account.
You can access individual VAT invoices for each reservation or download VAT invoices in bulk in the regions where Airbnb generates VAT invoices. VAT invoices are only available for the past 6 months.
Access a VAT invoice for a reservation on desktop
ClickToday>Reservations
ClickUpcomingorCompletedto find the reservation you want
Click the three dots next to the reservation
SelectVAT Invoice
Download VAT invoices on desktop
ClickToday>Reservations
ClickExport
Select your file type (CSV or PDF format) and choose the month you wish to download.
ClickDownload
If youadd a verified VAT ID numberto your account, it will be included on VAT invoices created thereafter. For reservation details, check your host confirmation.
A link to the VAT invoice is available in your email receipt, under the Airbnb service fee.
For reservation details, reference your billing receipt. If you need additional documentation for travel, you cancontact your hostbefore booking.
Find the VAT invoice for your reservation on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to check
UnderPayment info, clickGet receipt
If you have more than one payment, go toPayments, select the payment you want to open, and the receipt will include a link to the VAT invoice.

title: Finding receipts from past trips
content:
Finding receipts from past trips
Need a receipt from a past trip? No problem. You can download, print, or email your receipt directly from yourProfilefrom your desktop,  browser, or the Airbnb app.
Find your receipt from a completed reservation on desktop
ClickProfileand selectPast trips
Click the reservation you'd like a receipt for
Click the reservation's listing
UnderReservation details, clickGet receipt

title: Where guests can find their payments
content:
Where guests can find their payments
Find and review completed and upcoming payments for your home, experience, and service reservations as a guest.
To find payments for reservations on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Payments
UnderYour payments, clickManage payments
UnderCompleted, click to open your receipt
This shows payments you’ve made for reservations since 2018. If you need to find payments made before 2018, you cancontact Airbnb Community Supportfor assistance.
To check upcoming payments on desktop
ClickTripsand select the reservation you want to check
UnderReservation details, clickPayment info
ClickGet receipts and manage payments
UnderUpcoming, you'll find your scheduled payment date and amount
Payments for things related to your home stay, service, or experience that you’ve made to hosts through the Airbnb Resolution Center are shown separately from your other payments. Learnhow to find your Resolution Center payments.

title: Print your reservation details
content:
Print your reservation details
Prefer paper? No problem! You can print thereservation detailsfor an upcoming or completed booking from your browser.
Print your reservation info on desktop
1. ClickTripsand select the reservation you want
2. UnderReservation details, clickPrint details
Trying to print a receipt for a reservation? Find out how tofind your receipt as a guestor how toprint receipts and payout infoas a Host.

title: Managing your reservation receipt
content:
Managing your reservation receipt
When you make payments on Airbnb, the billing receipt generated at the time of payment is final and can’t be changed.
You’ll find details here about the most frequently asked questions regarding receipt changes and when a new receipt is issued.
Any receipt that has already been generated won’t update based on info you add or change after the payment has been made.
This includes changes to your reservation like:
Updating guest names
Adding business travel information
Other modifications that don’t change the cost of your trip
The currency shown on your receipt is the currency you used to make the payment, and a new receipt in a different currency can’t be generated.
Receipts are generated in the language associated with your account settings. Receipts cannot be revised to any other languages other than what was set in your account settings at the time the receipt was created.
Receipts are issued whenever payments are made, like when you make a payment for a reservation or if you’ve been issued a refund. For example, if you make a modification to your reservation that changes the cost—like adding to the number of guests or changing the dates—a new receipt will be issued if you make a payment for the additional cost or if you’ve been issued a refund for a decrease in the cost.
If you make a modification to your reservation that results in no price change, a new receipt won’t be issued.
Sending your host a payment using the Resolution Center won’t change any receipts that have already been issued, but you will receive a new receipt for the Resolution Center payment in your email.
If you’ve paid for a reservation usingscheduled paymentsand you have an upcoming payment scheduled, any changes made before the upcoming payment is processed will be shown on the new receipt.
For example, if you adjust the number of guests or change the trip dates before the scheduled payment, your new receipt will show these updated details.

title: How do I expense my business trip using Concur TripLink?
content:
How do I expense my business trip using Concur TripLink?
If your company usesConcur’s TripLink service, you’ll need to link your Airbnb and Concur accounts to have your Airbnb reservation receipts automatically uploaded to your Concur account.
To link your Airbnb and Concur TripLink accounts:
Go to Airbnb in theConcur App Center, and clickConnect
Log in to your existing Airbnb account or, if you don’t already have an account, create a new one
Once your accounts are linked, you can have the receipt for any Airbnb reservation appear in your Concur account by marking the box next toSend this booking to Concuron the reservation request checkout page.
You'll need to unlink your accounts by logging in to your account using a browser (not using the Airbnb app).
To unlink your Airbnb and Concur TripLink accounts:
Go toAccount
SelectSettings
UnderUnlink From Concur, clickUnlink

title: What is my Taiwan eGUI?
content:
What is my Taiwan eGUI?
A Taiwan Electronic Government Uniform Invoice (eGUI) is provided whenever Taiwan VAT is assessed on Airbnb service fees or on the accommodation price plus any fees for other items, such as cleaning and extra guests.
Airbnb collects VAT on the guest service fee from guests who reside in Taiwan. Airbnb also collects VAT on the full accommodation price plus any fees for other items, like cleaning fees or fees for extra guests, if applicable, from all guests staying in Taiwanese listings.
An eGUI is finalized and issued when a reservation is accepted, and includes your information (name and email address) as you entered it for your Airbnb account. Airbnb is not able to modify an eGUI after it's been issued.
An eGUI is not issued to business trip reservations.
To access the eGUI for your reservation, a notification will be sent to the email address associated with your Airbnb account indicating the eGUI is ready for download at the Taiwan Ministry of Financee-Invoice Platform. You’ll also receive an email notification when a booking has been accepted, altered, and/or cancelled and Taiwan VAT applies. Accessing the eGUI on the e-Invoice Platform for your reservation isoptional.
For reservation details, please check your billing receipt. If you believe you need additional documentation for travel, we encourage you tocommunicate with your hostbefore booking to see if they're able to assist you.

title: Brazil receipts and invoices
content:
Brazil receipts and invoices
When closing an operation through Airbnb, the system will automatically generate a receipt, which is accessible in your profile on the Airbnb platform. This receipt has no effect for tax purposes, but you can use it to prove your reservation and payment.
Airbnb will only issue an invoice for the Airbnb service fees (NFS-e) in accordance with the legislation in force and under the terms of the Special Regime Authorization - SEI no 6017.2021/0025032-5 published by the municipality of São Paulo.
Attention: Airbnb will not issue any invoice regarding the total value of the accommodation.
In your Airbnb user profile, you can view all receipts for all operations already carried out via the Airbnb platform. To access them, simply follow the steps below:
For Hosts
You can access your individual receipts for each booking.
To access a booking receipt:
Go to theTodaytab and clickShow all to access your Reservation(you will need to access your account on a computer or browser)
ClickDetailson the reservation you want
ClickPrintto view and print the details of a reservation
To access your bulk booking receipts:
Go toMenuand selectTransaction History(you will need to access your account on a computer or browser)
ClickExport to CSVto download the file
For guests
To access your booking receipt:
Go toTripsand choose your trip (you will need to access your account on a computer or browser).
ClickYour Accommodation Booking. If you are already on your trip, you may need to click on the check-in date.
ClickGet Receipt.
You can also receive your receipt via your booking confirmation email by selectingPayment Informationand clicking or tappingGet Receipt.
A link to the receipt is also available in the email receipt, under the Airbnb service fee.
To learn more about the reservation, review the billing receipt. If you believe you need additional documentation to travel, we recommend that you communicate with your Host before confirming your reservation to see if they can help.

title: Value Added Tax (VAT) and how it applies to you
content:
Value Added Tax (VAT) and how it applies to you
Value Added Tax (VAT) is a tax assessed on the supply of goods and services. In certain countries, such tax may be referred to as Goods and Services Tax (GST), service tax, or consumption tax (collectively referred to as “VAT” in this article).
Depending on your country of residence or location of listing, Airbnb is required to apply VAT toAirbnb service feeson your reservation in countries that tax electronically supplied services.
In certain countries, you may not be charged VAT on Airbnb service fees if you are registered for VAT or your stay is for business. However, you may still need to self-assess the VAT and report and declare the transaction in your VAT return depending on your local VAT laws.
To let us know you are registered for VAT, add VAT or Tax ID to your account inTaxes. Find out more aboutdetailed VAT ID format information.
For hosts
Add your VAT or Tax ID numberhere
Canada GST Registration
Add your GST/HST numberhere
India GST Registration
Add your GST/HST numberhere
For guests booking business travel
Add your business’ VAT or Tax ID numberhere
Click or tap theIs this a work trip?selection when you make your booking request
Learnhow to find your VAT invoice.
VAT is charged on the guest service fee for a reservation. If you change your reservation, VAT adjusts to reflect any change in the service fee.
VAT is charged on the Host service fee for a reservation. If a reservation is changed, VAT adjusts to reflect any change in the service fee.
Depending on your country of residence or location of your listing, you may need to assess VAT (or other taxes such asoccupancy taxes) on the accommodation and/or experience services you provide to guests. Learn more about howtaxes work for Hosts.
We encourage you to consult your tax advisor if you need assistance assessing VAT on the services you provide.
Disclaimer
The information here is a general guide only, which does not take into account your personal circumstances, is not intended to be tax advice and should not be relied upon as such. If you have any questions about taxes, we suggest that you consult your tax advisor or local tax authorities.
Please note that we do not update this information in real time, so you should check and confirm if the laws, tax rates, or procedures have changed recently.
If you are a customer in Albania,Airbnb service feesare subject to 20% VAT.
To find out more, check the tax authority’s officialwebsite.
If you are a customer in Armenia, Airbnb service fees are subject to 20% VAT.
To find out more, check the taxauthority’s official website.
If you are a customer in Australia,Airbnb service feesare subject to 10% GST.
If you are registered for GST, you may not be charged GST on Airbnb service fees. However, you may still be required to declare GST on your GST filing.To declare that you are registered for GST,add your Australia Business Number (ABN)here.
If, since providing your ABN, you have ceased being registered for GST, pleaseupdate your information.
To find out more, check the tax authority’s officialwebsite.
If you are a customer in Austria,Airbnb service feesare subject to 20% VAT.
If you are registered for VAT or your stay is for business, you may not be charged VAT on Airbnb service fees but you may be required to declare VAT on your VAT filing. To let us know you are registered for VAT, add your VAT ID numberhere.
To find out more, check the tax authority’s officialwebsite.
If you are a customer in the Bahamas,Airbnb service feesare subject to 12% VAT.
If you are registered for VAT in the Bahamas, you may not be charged VAT on Airbnb service fees but you may be required to declare VAT on your VAT filing. To let us know you are registered for VAT, add your Bahamian Tax Registration Numberhere.
To find out more, check the tax authority’s officialwebsite.
If you are a customer in Belgium,Airbnb service feesare subject to 21% VAT.
If you are registered for VAT or your stay is for business, you may not be charged VAT on Airbnb service fees but you may be required to declare VAT on your VAT filing. To let us know you are registered for VAT, add your VAT ID numberhere.
To find out more, check the tax authority’s officialwebsite.
If you are a customer in Brazil,Airbnb service feesare subject to ISS and PIS/COFINS regardless of your tax status.  Please note that between September 1st 2022 and March 31st 2025 our fees were temporarily subject to a special treatment for federal taxes under the Special Regime created under Law 14.148/2021, therefore the Airbnb fees were only subject to ISS for reservations booked during that period.
To find out more, check the tax authority’s officialwebsite.
If you are a customer in Bulgaria,Airbnb service feesare subject to 20% VAT.
If you are registered for VAT or your stay is for business, you may not be charged VAT on Airbnb service fees but you may be required to declare VAT on your VAT filing. To let us know you are registered for VAT, add your VAT ID numberhere.

title: Value Added Tax (VAT) on experiences and services and how it applies to you
content:
Value Added Tax (VAT) on experiences and services and how it applies to you
Value Added Tax (VAT) is a tax assessed on the supply of goods and services. In certain countries, such tax may be referred to as Goods and Services Tax (GST), service tax, or consumption tax (collectively referred to as “VAT” in this article).
Depending on your country of residence or location of the experience or service, Airbnb is required to apply VAT to theAirbnb service feeon your reservation in countries that tax electronically supplied services.
Airbnb manually issues VAT invoices monthly for experiences and services booked in the countries where VAT is applicable. Hosts will receive instructions via email, on how to access the invoices.
In certain countries, you may not be charged VAT on the Airbnb Experience or Service service fee if you are registered for VAT. However, you may still need to self-assess/declare VAT with your VAT return depending on your local VAT laws.
To let us know you are registered for VAT, add your VAT or Tax ID to your account in theTaxessection of your account. Find out more aboutdetailed VAT ID format information.
For hosts
Go toAccount > Taxes
SelectAdd VAT ID number
Complete the VAT ID form
VAT may be charged on the host service fee for an experience or service reservation. If a reservation is changed, VAT adjusts to reflect any change in the service fee.
Depending on your country of residence or location of your experience or service, you may need to assess VAT on the experience or service you provide to guests. We encourage you to consult your tax advisor if you need assistance assessing VAT on the services you provide.
Disclaimer
The information here is a general guide only, which does not take into account your personal circumstances, is not intended to be tax advice and should not be relied upon as such. If you have any questions about taxes, we suggest that you consult your tax advisor or local tax authorities.
Please note that we do not update this information in real time, so you should check and confirm if the laws, tax rates, or procedures have changed recently.

If you are a customer in Chile, theAirbnb service fees for experiences and servicesare subject to 19% VAT.
If you are registered for VAT in Chile, you may not be charged VAT on the experience or service service fee but you may be required to declare VAT on your VAT filing. To let us know you are registered for VAT, add your Chilean Tax Registration Numberhere.
Please note that if you provide your Chilean Tax Registration Number to us, we may need to provide such data to the Chilean Tax Authorities. Learn more about theChilean VAT regime.
To find out more, check the tax authority’s officialwebsite.

Please note that we may need to provide the information that you are a customer at Airbnb to the Colombian Tax Authorities.
Learn more about Colombian taxeshereor check the tax authority’s official website.

title: Taxes for guests
content:
Taxes for guests
Sometimes, local tax regulations require hosts to charge taxes to guests. We recommend that hosts include any taxes in the price of the reservation, but some may require them to be paid through theResolution Centerupon check-in instead.
We ask that hosts add any necessary taxes to their listing description and disclose them to guests prior to booking. If you’re a guest and want to be on top of it, ask your host about taxes ahead of time.
In some locations, Airbnb may collect and remit certain local taxes on behalf of hosts. The taxes vary and may include calculations based on a flat rate or percentage rate, number of guests, number of nights, or property type booked, depending on local law. When you book a listing in one of these locations, the local taxes collected will be displayed automatically when you pay, and they will appear on your receipt once your reservation is confirmed. Find out abouttax exemption refundsfor certain locations.
Additionally, Airbnb is required to collectVATon its service fees in countries that tax Electronically Supplied Services. Find the full list of countries requiring this tax forstays,experiences, and services.

title: Add your VAT ID number
content:
Add your VAT ID number
If you have a Value Added Tax (VAT) ID number registered with the European Commission, or with an authority outside the European Union (EU), you may want to add it to your Airbnb account.
Learn moreabout VAT and how it applies to you. As a host, you might want to considerupdating your pricingwhen your VAT ID is available for use.
Depending on your country of residence or location of listing, Airbnb is required to apply VAT toAirbnb service feeson your reservation in countries that tax electronically supplied services. Once your verified VAT registration number is added, it will take up to 24 hours to go into effect.
You can add your VAT ID number whether you’re a host operating as a business or a guest paying with an employer’s payment method.
When your VAT ID number is successfully verified by the European Commission through the VAT Information Exchange System (VIES), you won’t be charged VAT on our service fees. It will be your responsibility to self-assess whether you need to pay VAT for the use of our services.
The VAT ID number may be known under a different name based on your place of registration.
As within the EU, it will be your responsibility to self-assess whether you need to pay VAT for the use of our services, and you want to consider updating your pricing.
In Switzerland, Japan, and Indonesia, the presence of a VAT ID will not exempt you from VAT charges applied to service fees based on local law.
Add your VAT ID number on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Taxes
ClickAdd VAT ID Number
Enter your VAT ID number verification information
ClickAdd
If your VAT ID number isn’t successfully verified by the European Commission, you may want to try re-entering it. Airbnb isn’t able to associate a VAT ID number with your account without this verification, and we can’t troubleshoot problems. For a list of correct formats for VAT ID numbers by member state and additional information, please seethe European Commission's VIES FAQ.
If your VAT ID number can’t be verified and you determine you need to self-assess VAT on our service fees, we encourage you to consult a tax advisor for assistance.

title: Tourism tax exemption refunds
content:
Tourism tax exemption refunds
Some municipalities exempt travelers from the tourism tax or allow for a reduction in the amount based on their circumstances. Examples include children, residents of the municipality, or travelers visiting a relative in the hospital. Here’s more detail on a few popular destinations:
France
Germany
Italy
Lithuania
Portugal
Switzerland
To find out if you qualify for a refund or a reduction, visit the municipality’s tourist tax webpage or contact its tax or treasury office.

title: Taxes for photographers and other service providers
content:
Taxes for photographers and other service providers
If you’re a photographer, translator, or other service provider paid through your Airbnb account, be sure tocomplete your taxpayer informationso we can process your payouts.
US persons may fill out a Form W-9. In January, if you’ve submitted a Form W-9 and earned $600 or more during the previous year, we’ll provide you with a Form 1099-NEC showing your earnings under “Box 1 – Nonemployee compensation.” Corporate service providers will not receive a tax form.
Non-US persons may submit a Form W-8 to certify that their earnings are not subject to US tax.
If you’re unsure which form to submit, consult a tax professional for help.Find more information about US federal tax.

title: Create an account
content:
Create an account
Creating an Airbnb account is free and easy. To sign up, visit Airbnb.com, or download the Airbnb app from the App Store or Google Play.
You can sign up for a new account on desktop or mobile browser, or in the Airbnb app by providing any of the following:
An email address and a password
Your phone number
Google or Apple account
Naver account (for South Korea)
Every Airbnb community member is required to go through our identity verification process. For hosts, we'll collect this when you publish your listing. We’ll collect this for guests when you book. You can begin the verification processhere.
If more information is needed, we’ll let you know so you can complete the creation of your account.
You’ll need toverify your identitybefore you can get started hosting. Every community member is required to go through this process.
If you have more than one Airbnb account, you unfortunately can’t merge accounts. However, you can delete or deactivate the account you use the least. Learn more aboutwhat to do if you have multiple accounts.

title: Log in to your Airbnb account
content:
Log in to your Airbnb account
Whether you’re just getting started or simply want a refresher on how to get into your account, we’re here to help you get access.
We’re glad you’re here. You can start bycreating an account. You can also find everything you need to know aboutgetting started.
When you created your account, you chose the way that you sign in. It could be via email, phone number, Apple ID, Facebook or Google account, or Naver (South Korea).
Log in on desktop
ClickAccount
Select your login method from the options listed
Enter your login details and follow the instructions
If you’re are experiencing difficulty logging in, try the following options:
Make sure you’re connected to a stable internet connection.
You can use the phone number or email associated with your Airbnb account to log in.
You can alwaysreset your passwordif you forgot it.
If you’ve logged in to Airbnb with your Facebook credentials in the past, you’ll need to useone of these methodsto log in.
You’re automatically taken to your guest account when you log in. If you’re also a host, you canswitch between traveling and hostingon airbnb.com and the Airbnb app.

title: Switching between hosting and traveling
content:
Switching between hosting and traveling
Many hosts also book homes, services, and experiences through Airbnb as a guest. How do you switch your account between hosting and traveling and back again?
Your account must be set tohostingto manage your reservations and communicate with your guests.
Using the Airbnb app:HoldProfileand then tapHosting.
Using a browser:Next to your profile photo, click or tapSwitch to hosting.
To manage your own travel, services, and experiences, your account must be set totraveling.
Using the Airbnb app:HoldMenuand then tapTraveling.
Using a browser:Next to your profile photo, click or tapSwitch to traveling.

title: Which internet browsers work best on Airbnb?
content:
Which internet browsers work best on Airbnb?
For the best experience using the Airbnb site, we suggest using the most up-to-date version ofGoogle Chrome,Mozilla Firefox,Microsoft Edge, orOpera.
Browser developers frequently make improvements to provide you with a faster, more secure online experience. Newer browsers also support a wider range of designs and features than older browsers.
Many features of the Airbnb website don't work with an outdated or unsupported browser—and we often can't provide technical support in this case.

title: How do I use Sign in with Apple on Airbnb?
content:
How do I use Sign in with Apple on Airbnb?
Sign in with Appleallows you to use Face ID, Touch ID, or your device’s passcode instead of a password on Airbnb.
Sign in with Apple is currently available only when creating a new Airbnb account. If you already have an Airbnb account, log into your account as you normally would.
You can also use Sign in with Apple with a web browser or Android device when creating a new account. Check outApple’s support articlefor how to do this on other platforms.
If you used an Apple forwarding email address to sign up, reviewApple’s support articleto find out more about obtaining login details. You can also use thisApple support articleif you don’t know what your Apple forwarding email address is.
If you created an Airbnb account with your Apple ID, you weren’t required to create a password. Even though you don’t have a password, you can create one by following the instructions forresetting your password.
Creating a password will not change your ability to log in with your Apple ID from an iOS device in the future.
If you’d like to stop signing in with Apple, check outApple’s support article. Keep in mind that this does notdelete your Airbnb accountThis process simply disconnects your Apple ID from your Airbnb account and deactivates the Sign in with Apple option.

title: As a mainland China resident, what do I need to know to sign up for an Airbnb account?
content:
As a mainland China resident, what do I need to know to sign up for an Airbnb account?
If your place of residence or establishment is China (which for these purposes, excludes Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan):
Under our Terms of Service, you'll contract with Airbnb Singapore Private Limited (“Airbnb Singapore”) for all activities, except for limited exceptions called out under our Terms of Service.
Under our Payments Terms of Service, you’ll contract with Airbnb Payments UK Ltd.
Under our Privacy Policy and Privacy Supplement for China Users, your information is collected, stored and processed by Airbnb Singapore, and a copy of your account data will be transferred to, stored and processed by Airbnb Internet (Beijing) Co., Ltd. (“Airbnb China”). Similar to other hospitality or short-term rental companies that do business in China, Airbnb Singapore and Airbnb China may disclose your information to Chinese government agencies without further notice to you. Your  information may be further shared with service providers, including in China, to help run our business. Our service providers may also disclose your information where required by law.
If you don’t reside in China, please update theWhere You Livesection of your profile of your account settings.

title: Disconnect your social accounts from Airbnb
content:
Disconnect your social accounts from Airbnb
You can disconnect your social accounts from your Airbnb account at any time. Keep in mind that after you disconnect your social accounts, you’ll no longer be able to log in to Airbnb using that account.
Disconnect your social accounts on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Login & security
UnderSocial accounts, clickDisconnectnext to each account and follow the instructions
Learn more about what you can do if you’rehaving trouble logging into your Airbnb account after disconnecting your social account.

title: Login to Airbnb accounts through Facebook is no longer supported
content:
Login to Airbnb accounts through Facebook is no longer supported
Airbnb no longer supports Facebook login credentials as a way to create an Airbnb account, log in to an existing account, or connect an Airbnb account to a Facebook account.
If you used your Facebook credentials to log in to your Airbnb account in the past, you can use these instead:
The phone number associated with your Airbnb account can always be used to log in.
The email associated with your Airbnb account can always be used to log in. If you're not sure which email is associated with your Airbnb account, you can try looking for emails from Airbnb in your inbox.
Contact us
If you no longer have access to the email or phone number associated with your Airbnb account, please contactCustomer Support.
Keep in mind, you canreset your passwordif you forgot it.

title: Add or change your profile photo
content:
Add or change your profile photo
Whether you're a guest who reserved the perfect stay, experience or service, or a host welcoming guests, a clear and recognizable profile photo can help establish credibility and authenticity.
Edit your photo on desktop
ClickProfile>Edit profile
Under your profile photo, clickEdit
Choose a photo and clickSubmit
When selecting a profile photo, our guided photo capture experience will locate your face and give you AI-powered tips to help ensure it’s clear and visible.
Tips may include:
Centering your head in the frame with auto-zoom
Zooming in on yourself if your photo has multiple people in it
Suggesting that you provide a photo where your face is facing the camera
Letting you know if your photo is too blurry to use
Recommending against adding a photo without you in it—like photos of landscapes or animals
These tips are optional—you can bypass the recommendations and add the photo of your choice, just make sure it meets the standards of ourcontent policy.
Note:Additional photo requirementsapply for co-hosts to be displayed on theCo-Host Network.
While host photos are always visible on Airbnb, guest profile photos aren’t shown until after the hosthas accepted the reservation. Additionally, while a profile photo is recommended for all Airbnb users, it’s not mandatory for guests.

title: Reset or change your password
content:
Reset or change your password
Having trouble logging in? Forgot your password? We’ve all been there. Here's how to reset or change your password.
Reset your password on desktop
ClickForgot password
Enter the email associated with your account
ClickSend resetlink
You'll be emailed a link to reset your password.
If you created an Airbnb account by connecting to Facebook, Google, Apple, or using your phone number, you weren’t required to create a password.
If you’d like to create a password to log in directly, simply log out of your account and follow the steps for resetting your password. Even though you don’t yet have a password, going through the reset process will create a temporary password that allows you to create a permanent one.
If you’ve requested the reset link more than once, make sure you’re using the right email address. If you’re not sure which email address you used, try searching your email inbox for previous Airbnb messages. If you don’t find any, you may need to try another email address.
You may also need to disable your email service’s link preview (ex: Outlook) or mail-scanner feature, which could cause the link to expire before you open it.
Change your password on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Login & security
Next toPassword, clickUpdate
Enter your current password
Enter and confirm your new password
ClickUpdate password

title: Quick access to all your essential account settings
content:
Quick access to all your essential account settings
Once you’re logged in to Airbnb, yourAccount settingsis a convenient place to access any personal settings and preferences, from editing your email address to choosing your default language.
Personal information: View or edit your personal details, including your legalname, preferred name,phone number,email address, physical address,emergency contact, and identity verification status
Login and security: Update your password and secure your account with two-factor authentication, view device history, and approve shared access to your account
Privacy and sharing: Turn read receipts on or off, allow search engines to include your listing in results, update review settings, request your personal data, opt-into anti-discrimination research, choose what information is shared with third parties, and delete your account
Notifications: Choose notification preferences for your account, including offers and updates, and how you want to be contacted
Payments and payouts: Add payment methods, review your payments, view credits and coupons, add or change a payout method, and view your earnings dashboard
Translation: Select whether you want reviews, descriptions, and messages automatically translated into the language you’ve set to view Airbnb in
Taxes: Manage taxpayer information and your tax documents
Professional hosting tools: Manage several properties in one place
Accessibility: Choose whether you prefer infinite scroll, add zoom and pan controls to maps

title: Merging multiple accounts
content:
Merging multiple accounts
For one reason or another, you might have more than one Airbnb account. Unfortunately, there’s no way to merge or move information or bookings between them.
Our advice? Choose the one you’d like to use going forward anddeactivate or deletethe other one. Any pending reservations on that account will be canceled, so be sure to wait until they’re complete before deleting it.

title: Change your name in your profile
content:
Change your name in your profile
While we’ll need your legal name for some stuff, we’re happy to make you feel welcomed by using your preferred first name on your public Airbnb profile.
You can add a preferred first name when initially signing up for an Airbnb account, in addition to your legal name. Your preferred name will be shown to other hosts and guests across our Airbnb app and website–on your profile, in reviews, and in messages.
Don’t want to add a preferred first name? No problem. We’ll use your legal first name.
Add a preferred first name on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Personal information
Go toPreferred nameand clickAdd
Add the name you want to display on your profileand clickSave
We do need your legal name to help verify your identity, and your legal name may show in some scenarios, such as on tax or insurance documents.
Your preferred first name can be a nickname, or a variation of your legal name, or just whatever you want hosts and guests to call you.
There are few things you can’t use:
A fake name or a name that impersonates another person, such as a public figure
Multiple names (ex: John and Jack Smith)
Don’t include a title (ex: Dr.)
No offensive or suggestive words of any kind
Names and other content on Airbnb should also always follow ourContent Policy.
If you’re hosting as a business on Airbnb, you can use the preferred name field to reflect the name of your business.
Note: Co-hosts on theCo-Host Network may have different requirementsabout using their business name.

title: Change your email address
content:
Change your email address
New email? No problem. Just remember, your email address can only be linked to one Airbnb account at a time.
Edit email address on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Personal information
Go toEmail addressand clickEdit
Make your changes and then clickSave
If you’re getting a notification that your email address is already in use but you can’t remember your password, tryresetting it.

title: Edit phone numbers on your account
content:
Edit phone numbers on your account
Need to edit your phone number? No problem. Keeping your phone number current—and removing any old numbers—is a good way to keep your account safe.
Edit your phone number on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Personal Information
Go toPhone numberand clickEdit
Make your changes, then clickSave
After a booking is accepted, all phone numbers on an account will be displayed to the other party, whether you're a guest or a host.
Once you have a phone number associated with an account, you’ll have the option to log in to Airbnb using that phone number.
If you have more than one phone number listed, or your phone number is shared across multiple accounts, you’ll need tolog in with an email address.

title: Disconnect your social accounts from Airbnb
content:
Disconnect your social accounts from Airbnb
You can disconnect your social accounts from your Airbnb account at any time. Keep in mind that after you disconnect your social accounts, you’ll no longer be able to log in to Airbnb using that account.
Disconnect your social accounts on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Login & security
UnderSocial accounts, clickDisconnectnext to each account and follow the instructions
Learn more about what you can do if you’rehaving trouble logging into your Airbnb account after disconnecting your social account.

title: Login to Airbnb accounts through Facebook is no longer supported
content:
Login to Airbnb accounts through Facebook is no longer supported
Airbnb no longer supports Facebook login credentials as a way to create an Airbnb account, log in to an existing account, or connect an Airbnb account to a Facebook account.
If you used your Facebook credentials to log in to your Airbnb account in the past, you can use these instead:
The phone number associated with your Airbnb account can always be used to log in.
The email associated with your Airbnb account can always be used to log in. If you're not sure which email is associated with your Airbnb account, you can try looking for emails from Airbnb in your inbox.
Contact us
If you no longer have access to the email or phone number associated with your Airbnb account, please contactCustomer Support.
Keep in mind, you canreset your passwordif you forgot it.

title: Add an emergency contact
content:
Add an emergency contact
Say you find yourself in an emergency—it’s good for us to have a contact on file to help you out. You can add up to 4 contacts to your account.
Add an emergency contact on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Personal information
Go toEmergency contactand clickAdd
Enter your emergency contact's name, relationship to you, preferred language, email, country code, and phone number
ClickSave
You can add additional contacts, or remove an existing contact. Just click or tapAdd a new emergency contactorRemove.

title: Changing the language on Airbnb
content:
Changing the language on Airbnb
Mother tongue or another one? You can change your preferences any time in order to browse Airbnb and receive communications like emails and messages in the language of your choice.
Update your preferred language on desktop
ClickMenu>Account settings>Global preferences
Next toPreferred language, clickEdit
Select your preferred language and clickSave
If you’d like to temporarily browse Airbnb in a different language, you can click or tap the language iconandSelect a language. We’ll revert back to your preferred language when you visit next time.

title: Change your public profile location
content:
Change your public profile location
Guests and hosts on Airbnb have the option to add a city toWhere I livein their public profile. This information will appear on the profile and may be shown outside of your profile, like in listings or reviews.
We show the location you add to your profile, but never your mailing or full address for your listing or your account.
Edit your location on desktop
ClickProfile>Edit profile
ClickWhere I liveto update or remove your location
ClickSaveand then clickDone
You can update yourProfilewhenever you choose. Learn more aboutmanaging your profile.

title: Updating your host profile: What I do for work
content:
Updating your host profile: What I do for work
You're already known as a host, so why not add to your profile to be better known as a person? You can share interesting information about yourself, such as your profession, or if you don't have a traditional job, your life's calling.
The details you choose to share about yourself are featured across Airbnb—like in search and your profile—and are visible to guests.
Updating your profile will override any previous work highlights, both in search and in your profile.
Edit your profile on desktop
ClickProfile>Edit profile
ClickMy work
Add what you want displayed on your profile
ClickDone
For some privateroomhosts who provided descriptions of their work that were longer than 20 characters, a short highlight of their information may be displayed to guests in search. In this case, the information included on your profile will remain the same, but in search, it may appear differently.
Need to make changes to other account info? Learn how tomanage your profile.

title: Add your business details
content:
Add your business details
Getting a prompt to add or change your business details? No problem. You can edit your business details from your host dashboard when requested.
If requested to update business details that need to be verified to comply with legal requirements (such as your legal business name or business registration details), you'll be asked to acknowledge that we’ll need to verify your new info before you can continue.
Add business details on desktop
ClickMenu>Accountsettings>Business details
ClickAddorEditon the field you want to update
Add your new information and clickSave
If updating your phone number or email address, clickNextto confirm the new info using a one-time code
Edit legal business details on desktop
ClickMenu>Accountsettings>Business details
ClickMake updates here
Read the verification notice and clickGot it
Select what info you want to update, clickEdit infoand clickNext
If prompted to add information about any beneficial owners or account managers, clickNextto add or to skip, then clickSave and exit
Review and clickConfirm

title: Overview of your profile
content:
Overview of your profile
Airbnb is a community made up of people – people you travel with, people who host your stays and experiences, and other travelers you might meet along the way.
Your Airbnb profileis how you introduce yourself on Airbnb, and it represents you as an authentic, trustworthy individual to others in our community. It includes your verified identity, profile photo, hometown, languages spoken, personal interests, fun facts like your favorite song, and even travel stamps representing each trip you’ve taken on Airbnb.
You can update your profile anytime, and it’s visible to hosts and other guests whenever you book, join a trip, or leave a review. It helps build trust with hosts you might want to stay with, guests who are interested in staying at your place, and other guests you meet on experiences.
Your profile is public and profile details may be shown across Airbnb— in reviews, in group message threads, and on group reservations.  If you are a host, your photo may be highlighted in search, on your listings, and in emails to guests. In addition, your profile is visible to everyone in yourConnections.
If you’re a guest, your profile is shared with your host, and if you choose toshare your profile on an experience, your profile photo and the state or country associated with your hometown will be shown on the public guest list for the experience.Update your profileto make sure that you show up how you want to for your connections, hosts and other guests on Airbnb.
Learnwhere else your profile information may appear.
The profile is made up of details about your identity, reputation, history on Airbnb, and details you provide such as fun facts and languages spoken.  The info shared varies whether you’re a host, guest, or both.
The info common to everyone is your first name, profile photo, the number of years you’ve been on Airbnb, if you’ve completed identity verification, what month and year you were first identity verified, and any reviews you’ve given or received.
Host and guest profiles feature details you provide about yourself, all of which are optional. These include basic information like languages spoken, and the decade you were born, and fun facts like where you’d most like to travel, where you went to school or work, and favorite song.
Additionally, both hosts and guests can choose to include interests, travel stamps, which include locations,and dates of trips on their profile. Sharing interests and past trips help showcase things you may have in common with other people.
Find out more aboutwhat info is shown on your profile.
While Airbnb automatically adds some basic information like your first name and number of trips to your profile, any additional informationyou choose to add is optional. This means that how much info is included and shared—like what languages you speak or your interests—is up to you.
While we encourage all users to have profile photos, we do require them for hosts.
